R+ Classification View — Multi-Sector (FY3/2026)

2026-06-05 · 5 TOPIX-33 sectors · per-sector filter + transparency block
TOPIX-33 sector: 情報・通信業 (Information & Communication)
Total listed in sector 600
Qualifying as R+ 82
R- (currently listed) 0
Delisted / TOB (historical) 0
For IT sector, revenue growth = business growth. No alternative filter needed.

Why these filter options?

Why use Revenue ↑ (default)?
In plain terms: For IT and SaaS companies, revenue is the cleanest signal of business growth. When an IT company's revenue grows, it almost always means more customers, larger contracts, or expanded usage of their software — all things that reflect genuine business strength. There are no big commodity prices or external cycles distorting the top line. So for the IT sector, 'revenue up (売上○)' captures what investors actually care about: is the business getting bigger? That's why this is the only filter offered here.
▸ Durably overlooked — multiple business-up years, stock not catching up 3 names
In plain terms: these companies have grown their business steadily for 3 or more years in a row, but the stock price still has not reflected it. Rule used: 2 or more prior years tagged "Q2" (business UP, stock DOWN) — which means at least 3 consecutive years of the same mismatch. Why this matters: if the market overlooks growth once it could be noise — but overlooking it 3 years running suggests something structural. Often these are small or mid caps without good analyst coverage, or names sitting in a sector that institutional investors have rotated away from. If the structural reason resolves (broker coverage starts, sector re-rates, governance improves) the stock can re-rate sharply. This is the strongest "asymmetric upside" signal in the four R+×S- groups.
3697 SHIFT Inc. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: SHIFT is an independent Japanese IT company specializing in software quality assurance (QA) — its core business is "third-party verification", testing other companies' software before launch to find bugs. It has expanded into software-development support, an AI-powered automated test service "DQS" (DX Quality Service), and consulting. Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY8/2026 H1 (Sep 2025 - Feb 2026): revenue ¥72.035 billion (+16.8%), ordinary profit ¥6.609 billion (-16.0%); operating margin fell from 14.4% YoY to 11.0% in the latest 3-month quarter. Full-year guidance: revenue ¥150.0 billion (+15.5%), adjusted OP ¥20.0 billion (+13.4%), adjusted ordinary profit ¥20.0 billion (+16.3%), net income ¥13.5 billion (+24.3%). FY8/2025 was a record year: revenue +17% to ¥129.8 billion, OP +48%, gross margin a record 34.7%. Dividend remains zero. Why did business move this way? First, heavy upfront investment in AI-QA service "DQS" — mass hiring of engineers and aggressive sales-and-marketing push spending out the door. Hiring restrained in the prior year normalized this year, so personnel costs jumped. Second, short-term borrowing for working capital rose, lifting interest expense and pressuring ordinary profit. Third, AI-related revenue is expected to contribute only in future periods, putting SHIFT in a "spend first, earn later" valley. Why did the stock move this way? First, on June 2, 2026 Daiwa Securities downgraded SHIFT two notches from top rating "1" to "3" AND slashed the target price from ¥1,700 to ¥780 — a signal that trust in the AI-investment payback timing collapsed. Second, on June 3 the stock crashed -12.21% to ¥694.7, after already hitting a year-low of ¥590 on February 24. Third, at PER ~41x SHIFT is a high-valuation name where slowing profit growth is punished hard; with no dividend and lingering goodwill amortization from past M&A, long-term holders have no income reason to stay. UNVERIFIED: FY8/2027 guidance, specific DQS revenue scale, and broader analyst-action details beyond the confirmed Daiwa downgrade could not be verified.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue compounded each year for the past 3 years on durable business execution, BUT the stock has failed to re-rate (2025 stock flat/— YoY) — the gap is that the market's residual derating stance from prior years hasn't broken despite multi-year consistent growth. Durability of the mismatch is what makes it worth attention.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (strategic-investment margin trough):* the inverse split-signal year — revenue still growing but profit DOWN, because SHIFT front-loaded heavy AI investment (building the DQS modernization service), spiking SG&A. Q1 FY8/2026 operating profit fell 19.9% to ¥2.8bn, missing expectations. The stock cratered from the ~¥1,200 area (Oct 2025) toward ~¥666 (Apr 2026), roughly -49% over six months and ~-64% off the trailing-year high of ¥1,827.5. Revenue ◎ / profit ✕ (deliberate investment, not demand loss), stock ✕✕.
4071 Plus Alpha Consulting Co.,LTD. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Plus Alpha Consulting (TSE Prime) describes itself as a "visualization-platform company" that builds SaaS for big-data-driven business-efficiency and decision-support. The flagship is HR Solutions' talent-management SaaS "Talent Palette". Marketing Solutions is the second pillar. How did business move: FY9/2025 full-year: revenue +14.2% to ¥9.341 billion, OP +32.2% to ¥3.692 billion — sharp growth, with an 81.1% equity ratio. FY9/2026 guidance: revenue ¥19.5 billion (+14.1%), OP ¥7.5 billion (+17.6%), ordinary ¥7.5 billion (+18.7%), net profit ¥5.2 billion (+59.6%). Dividend ¥29 → ¥38 (+¥9 hike). FY9/2026 Q1: revenue +14.0%, OP +49.5%. Why did business move this way: First, Talent Palette continues high growth driven by enterprise-customer wins — large new logos like Nojima boosted both enterprise share and ARPU. Second, marketing-spend rationalization sharply lifted OP margin. Third, on May 12, 2026 Talent Palette added AI-agent capabilities — evolving to an "AI-era strategic HR platform". Why did the stock move this way: Despite strong results the stock was at ¥2,335 on May 11 — barely moving. That is the central puzzle. First, as a 2021-IPO growth name it has been normalizing down from the listing-era premium for years. Second, mid-cap value sits below many big-institution minimum sizes. Third, the silver lining is the current PER of 19.6x — the bottom of its historical 20-100x range.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue compounded each year for the past 4 years on durable business execution, BUT the stock has failed to re-rate (2025 stock flat/— YoY) — the gap is that the market's residual derating stance from prior years hasn't broken despite multi-year consistent growth. Durability of the mismatch is what makes it worth attention.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (record results — derating finally arrests):* stock ¥1,163 (Apr 7) – ¥2,612 (Aug 14); Apr 2025 ¥1,163 was the absolute multi-year low (-74% from peak), then recovered to ¥2,612 by August (+125% off the spring low). **FY9/2025 actual (announced Nov 14, 2025): revenue ¥17,084M (+22.8% YoY organic / +83% reported including segment expansion), operating profit ¥6,378M (+40.8% YoY), operating margin 37.3%** (high for SaaS); enterprise customer ratio rising, ARPU rising, HR Solutions segment leading. Plan achievement: revenue 100.5%, OP 104.6% per IR. Sign summary: revenue rose +22.8% YoY organically; operating profit rose +40.8%; operating margin 37.3% record; shares rallied off ¥1,163 spring low to ¥2,612 high (Aug 14) — +125% intra-year. 業績 ◎◎ (record results + margin expansion) / 株価 ◎ (recovery from multi-year low).

Cautious side: stock still well below the Nov 2021 peak of ¥4,480 (Aug 2025 high ¥2,612 = -42% off ATH); the 2021 peak was hype-driven post-IPO mania at a level unjustified by fundamentals, so it may not be a meaningful target; revenue mix shift from SMB toward enterprise raises ARPU but lengthens sales cycle; competitive HR-SaaS pressure (Money Forward, freee, Visional all expanding).

Sources:
- https://equity.jiji.com/storage/tdnet/140120241107515258.pdf (FY9/2024 通期決算短信, Nov 8, 2024)
- https://finance-frontend-pc-dist.west.edge.storage-yahoo.jp/disclosure/20251114/20251113501423.pdf (FY9/2025 通期決算短信, Nov 14, 2025)
- https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/378612 (FY23 +41.2% / +39.3% strong-growth context + mid-term plan revision)

4165 PLAID,Inc. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: PLAID (4165) provides 'KARTE,' a CX SaaS platform. It collects and analyzes behavioral data of every visitor to a company's website or app in real time, then dynamically delivers personalized messages and screens. Sold primarily to large enterprises. Listed on TSE Growth. How did business move? FY12/2026 Q1 revenue was 3.696 billion yen (+15.9%) and operating profit 299 million yen (-30.1%). H1 cumulative: revenue 7.766 billion yen (+18.6%). Full-year guidance: revenue 20.577 billion yen (+53.6%), OP 2.183 billion yen (+52.5%). Dividend: 0 yen. Why did business move this way? First, sales and marketing spend has been front-loaded to accelerate enterprise penetration of KARTE — a classic SaaS 'invest first, harvest later' pattern; this is the main reason Q1 OP fell -30.1%. Second, R&D investment in product expansion compressed margins. Third, ARPU expansion at existing customers and new large-deal additions are driving the 18%+ revenue growth. Why did the stock move this way? First, rising interest rates have broadly derated growth SaaS stocks — high-PSR names like PLAID get hit hardest. Second, the Q1 operating-profit decline of -30.1% spooked the market. Third, analyst coverage is thin and dividend is zero.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue compounded each year for the past 4 years on durable business execution, BUT the stock has failed to re-rate (2025 stock flat/— YoY) — the gap is that the market's residual derating stance from prior years hasn't broken despite multi-year consistent growth. Durability of the mismatch is what makes it worth attention.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (FY9/2025 — growth holds, profit thin):* for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, revenue was ¥13.40bn, up 21.87% — strong top line. But profitability is thin and wobbling: net margin only ~7%, EBITDA margin ~3%, the most recent quarter's revenue (¥3.29bn) slightly missed the ¥3.35bn estimate, and quarterly net income fell ~32% sequentially. 業績 ◎ on revenue, but with a profit-quality caveat; 株価 ✕ (still weak) → Q2, medium confidence — watch the profit line, because the ◎ is being carried by revenue while earnings wobble.
▸ All financials up, stock still fell — clean mismatch or profit-taking sell-off 2 names
In plain terms: revenue grew, operating profit grew, AND net profit grew — but the stock STILL fell 30% or more in the year. Rule used: rev_dir=up, op_dir=up, net_dir=up, AND stock YoY < -30%. Why this matters: there is no operational excuse — the gap between fundamentals and price is unusually clean. The most common explanations are (a) profit-taking after a prior rally that took the stock too far ahead of earnings, (b) a one-off event scare (regulation rumor, key-person resignation, accounting noise) that did NOT actually affect the business, or (c) sector rotation pulling capital out indiscriminately. These can be the cleanest mean-reversion opportunities — IF you can confirm the scare or rotation is the real cause and the underlying business is still on track.
5244 jig.jp Co., Ltd. SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -34.0%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: jig.jp's main business is the live-streaming app "FUWATCH" — a C2C live-streaming platform where streamers broadcast to viewers, and viewers buy items (gifts). A TSE Growth small-cap. Also operates IoT and hometown-tax-payment-related new businesses. How did business move? FY3/2026 revenue ¥14.631 billion (+6.1%, slight up), OP ¥1.976 billion (-1.8%, slight down). FUWATCH performed well, but personnel and ad costs pressured profit. Q3: revenue ¥11.201 billion (+9.3%), OP ¥1.604 billion (+4.3%). FY3/2027 guidance: revenue ¥16.5 billion, OP ¥1.98 billion (+0.2%, essentially flat). Equity ratio 66.94%. Why did business move this way? First, the core "FUWATCH" held up steadily. Second, three cost pressures simultaneously squeezed profit. Third, the same investment phase continues next fiscal year. Why did the stock move this way? Down -34% YoY for full-year 2025. First, live-streaming market competition intensifying — well-capitalized new entrants drive up CAC. Second, FY27 OP guidance of just +0.2% read as "end of the growth narrative". Third, TSE Growth small-cap structural weakness. Stock stalled around ¥278.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue, operating profit, and net profit ALL grew sharply in 2025 (clean three-line acceleration), BUT the stock fell -34.0% YoY — the gap is a clean directional disconnect at material size. Either the market is pricing in a non-financial concern (governance, accounting, sector risk) the headline numbers don't show, or the market simply hasn't caught up yet.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥201 (4-7) – ¥339 (1-15); close ¥216 (-34.0% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥13800M (+12.7%), OP ¥1500M (+36.4%), NP ¥1050M (+23.5%). 業績 ◎ / 株価 ✕.

3778 SAKURA internet Inc. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -37.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: SAKURA internet is a Japanese cloud and data center operator centered on its Ishikari Data Center. The company offers corporate cloud services, GPU infrastructure for generative AI, and a domestic cloud officially adopted for the Japanese Government Cloud program. How did business move? FY3/2026 revenue reached a record 35.30 billion yen (+12.4%), but operating profit swung from +4.14 billion yen the prior year to a 0.40 billion yen loss. Net profit also fell sharply. January-March operating profit was just 0.71 billion yen (-54.2%). FY3/2027 guidance projects revenue of 45.0 billion yen (+27.5%) and operating profit of 1.5 billion yen, returning to the black. ~1,100 NVIDIA B200 GPUs installed (largest in Japan). Why did business move this way? First, generative-AI GPU infrastructure (+20.3%) and cloud services (+9.4%) drove record revenue, but heavy upfront investment in equipment and hiring tipped operating profit into the red. Second, surging depreciation and lease costs from data center expansion compressed margins. Third, official adoption as a Government Cloud provider in March 2026 opened a new sales channel. Why did the stock move this way? First, the stock crashed 9.60% to 3,295 yen on April 27 with volume up 415% after earnings; the market punished cloud-business uncertainty and the investment-driven operating loss. Second, the premium narrative as a top Japanese AI-cloud play (2023-2024) is fading. Third, structural scale gaps versus hyperscalers and concentration of AI-investment money into chipmakers leave only limited buying for mid-cap names.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: FY3/2025 fundamentals grew sharply (rev +44%, OP +369%, NP +351%) on Sakura Cloud / generative-AI demand — BUT the stock fell -37.5% YoY because the generative-AI-cloud narrative cooled off the 2023-2024 mania peak. The gap is sector-narrative cooling, not a fresh business mismatch the analyst hasn't seen.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥2677 (7-30) – ¥5020 (2-4); close ¥2780 (-37.5% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥31412M (+43.9%), OP ¥4145M (+368.7%), NP ¥2937M (+350.7%). 業績 ◎ / 株価 ✕.

<<CANONICAL FY2025>>

▸ Revenue up, profit compressed — derated on margins, potentially recoverable 17 names
In plain terms: revenue keeps growing, but operating margins have been squeezed. Rule used: 業績 sign = "mixed" (= revenue up, but operating profit OR net profit down). Why this matters: the squeeze is usually one of four things — (a) up-front investment (R&D, marketing, hiring) that depresses current profit but builds future revenue, (b) input-cost inflation (wages, materials, energy), (c) pricing pressure from competition, or (d) FX / one-off impairments. The market has marked the multiple DOWN because earnings look weaker, but the business still has top-line momentum. What to watch: the margin trend in the next 2-3 quarters. If management has a credible cost or pricing plan, margin recovery often triggers a sharp re-rating because both EPS and the multiple rise together. This is the "patient money" cohort — recovery typically takes 1-2 years.
3653 Morpho Inc. other small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -41.6%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Morpho is an independent developer and licensor of image-processing AI software — its technology is embedded in smartphone camera correction algorithms, automotive AD/ADAS (advanced driver assistance), DMS (driver monitoring), telematics, and industrial machine-vision products, often invisibly inside customer devices. Listed on the TSE Growth market. How did business move? FY10/2026 Q1 was rough: revenue fell -32.2% YoY to ¥474 million, with an operating loss of ¥391 million (widening loss). Operating margin deteriorated sharply from -19.7% to -82.5%. Despite this, full-year guidance was left unchanged: revenue ¥3.5 billion (+4.1%), operating profit ¥100 million (+118.1%), net income ¥70 million — an aggressive bet on H2 recovery. Dividend remains at zero. Why did business move this way? First, weakness in the core Smart Device segment is the direct cause — Morpho continues to develop new Chinese smartphone-maker customers but the wins are not yet showing in Q1 numbers. Second, smartphone-market maturation (flat global shipments) is structurally shrinking the image-processing-engine revenue base. Third, upfront investment in new areas like the automotive "Morpho Automotive Suite" (launched 2025) continues, but revenue contribution lies in the future while costs hit today. Why did the stock move this way? First, the stock has been weak — it hit a 10-year low of ¥609 on March 30, 2026 as the market refused to believe full-year guidance, judging that recovering a -32% Q1 decline in H2 is unrealistic. Second, while it rebounded to a ¥681 close on May 28, it remains stuck in a ¥600-700 range on thin volume — a wait-and-see mode. Third, the combination of no dividend, ongoing losses, and being a small-cap Growth-market name pushes Morpho off institutional screens, so buyers are simply absent. UNVERIFIED: FY10/2027 guidance, specific mid-term plan targets, and concrete large-order amounts for automotive deals could not be verified from public sources.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -41.6% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥775 (Dec 23) – ¥1,536 (Feb 14); close ¥790 (-41.6% YoY). FY10/2025 rev ¥3.36B (+2% flat-up), OP ¥45M (-82%), NP -¥77M (back to loss). 業績 ✕ (OP collapsed -82%, NP back to loss; turn proved fragile) / 株価 ✕.

3668 COLOPL,Inc. content_games small ▸
Revenue: mixed Stock 2025 YoY: -11.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: COLOPL is a Japanese smartphone game developer and operator — best known for hits like "Shironeko Project", "Quiz RPG: Wizard and Black Cat Wiz", and "Dragon Quest Walk". It also operates location-based services and an "investment-incubation business" investing in entrepreneurs and XR (VR/AR) startups. Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY9/2026 H1 (interim) was rough — revenue ¥10.088 billion (-28.2%), OP ¥533 million (-62.3%), ordinary profit ¥1.435 billion (-29.2%), net income ¥825 million (+364% on special factors). The Entertainment segment offset declining legacy-title revenue with cost cuts; the Investment-Incubation segment swung to an operating loss. Interim dividend ¥0; year-end dividend forecast undecided. FY9/2024 had been the first full-year loss since listing. Why did business move this way? First, gradual revenue decline of flagship long-running titles like Shironeko Project — past their 10-year mark, even collaboration events lifting rankings cannot offset structural attrition. Second, App Store / Google Play 30% platform fees and user-acquisition ad costs are flat regardless of company size, structurally disadvantaging mid-sized publishers. Third, the Investment-Incubation segment's prior-year special gains did not repeat, swinging to a loss and adding volatility. Why did the stock move this way? First, the day after the May 7, 2026 interim release (May 8), the stock fell -7.64% (-¥32) and was at ¥376 by May 11. Second, sector-wide derating of mobile-game stocks — Japan's smartphone-game market is flat, and Chinese titles are eroding Japanese publishers' share. Third, with PBR near 1x, no dividend, and limited analyst coverage as a small Prime-market game stock, there are essentially no institutional buyers. UNVERIFIED: FY9/2026 full-year guidance, year-end dividend forecast, and specific new-title release plans could not be verified.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -11.1% YoY because operating margins compressed (NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥407 (4-7) – ¥545 (11-12); close ¥429 (-11.1% YoY). FY9/2025 rev ¥25933M (-0.2%), OP ¥1002M, NP ¥-306M (loss). 業績 mixed / 株価 ✕.

3760 CAVE Interactive CO.,LTD. content_games small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -29.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: CAVE Interactive develops games, specializing in shoot-em-ups, and is primarily known for arcade-era classic IPs such as "Dodonpachi", "Mushihimesama", "Deathsmiles", and "Touhou Gensou Eclipse", mostly on mobile. Through subsidiary "DeLaGame" it also develops and operates other smartphone games. In parallel it runs a video-distribution-related business. Listed on TSE Standard. How did business move? FY5/2026 Q1: revenue ¥2.627 billion (-11.6%), operating loss ¥565 million. Game segment -10.6%. 9-month cumulative ordinary loss ¥852 million. Full-year guide: ordinary loss ¥640 million, revenue -14.8%. The prior year FY5/2024 had been a hit-year — "Touhou Gensou Eclipse" (released Nov 2023, 200,000+ pre-registrations) drove revenue ¥12.27 billion (+76.3%) and OP ¥1.87 billion (+667.4%); this year is the payback. ROE -62.3% reflects meaningful equity erosion. Why did business move this way? First, the "Touhou Gensou Eclipse" hit-driven boom is not repeating — heavy dependence on a single hit means the year after sees a revenue valley with no follow-up release. Second, video-distribution-related business posted a segment loss — fragmentation of live-streaming platforms and a surge of creators intensified competition (-32% revenue, loss). Third, the shoot-em-up niche makes "hit-or-miss" new-title risk especially acute. Why did the stock move this way? First, the full-year loss-reversion guide and -14.8% revenue forecast kept the stock in a downtrend — 1-year total return below -29%. Second, as a TSE Standard small-cap with limited analyst coverage, there are essentially no institutional buyers to absorb sell pressure. Third, PBR has come down to the low single digits, already pricing in the deterioration — expectations are bombed-out, but until a clear re-rating trigger appears, low-volume wait-and-see drift continues. UNVERIFIED: FY5/2026 final results, FY5/2027 guidance, and concrete new-title pipeline details could not be verified.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -29.4% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥681 (4-7) – ¥1100 (11-12); close ¥734 (-29.4% YoY). FY5/2025 rev ¥13969M (+13.8%), OP ¥113M (-39.5%), NP ¥24M (-83.7%). 業績 mixed / 株価 ✕.

3776 BroadBand Tower, Inc. SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -0.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: BroadBand Tower (BBT) operates urban-type data centers based in Otemachi, Tokyo. The business has two main pillars: a Computer Platform segment and a Media Solutions segment (cable TV platform and information services for local governments). Recently the company added 5G-ready facilities and is pushing into AI / IoT areas. How did business move? FY12/2026 Q1: revenue 3.19 billion yen (+2.9%) and operating profit 224 million yen (+41.0%). However, full-year FY12/2026 guidance is for sharp decline: revenue 13.4 billion yen (-12.4%), operating profit 0.5 billion yen (-38.4%), ordinary profit 0.49 billion yen (-46.1%), and net profit 0.1 billion yen (-66.7%). Annual dividend is set at 3 yen (interim 1 yen, year-end 2 yen), up 1 yen YoY. Why did business move this way? First, Q1 Media Solutions did well by capturing local-government demand. Second, full-year guidance assumes a decline in the Computer Platform segment. Third, corporate IT continues to migrate to hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), creating a structural headwind to in-house data center utilization. Why did the stock move this way? First, even though Q1 showed solid growth, the harsh full-year ordinary-profit guidance of -46.1% left the stock directionless. Second, although BBT should be a data-center play that benefits from the AI boom, the company has not announced clear pivots toward GPU clusters or AI workloads. Third, the small market cap and extreme historical PER range leave institutional coverage thin.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -0.5% YoY because operating margins compressed (NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥116 (6-19) – ¥206 (1-10); close ¥183 (-0.5% YoY). FY12/2025 rev ¥15289M (+13.9%), OP ¥811M (+21.4%), NP ¥300M (-25.8%). 業績 unclassified / 株価 unclassified.

3844 COMTURE CORPORATION SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -24.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: COMTURE is an independent Japanese SIer, providing cloud-platform construction, data-utilization, and generative-AI solution development plus operational maintenance for large and mid-sized clients. It has partnerships with SAP, Microsoft, AWS, Google Cloud, and Salesforce. How did business move? FY3/2026 consolidated ordinary profit is on track for about 5.0 billion yen (+7.3%), the 14th consecutive year of record profit. Q3 YTD revenue was 28.04 billion yen (+4.4%) with operating profit nearly flat at +0.7%. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue 42.0 billion yen (+10.2%), operating profit 4.7 billion yen (+0.8%), net profit 3.23 billion yen (-1.7%). Annual dividend rises to 50 yen. Why did business move this way? First, cloud-platform construction, data-utilization, and generative-AI engagements remained strong. Second, in a tightening talent market, wage hikes, headcount growth, and new-graduate training costs compressed operating-profit growth. Third, reinvestment costs ran ahead of revenue — office consolidation, engagement events, and new core-system rollout. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite the remarkable 14-year record-profit streak, the stock stayed range-bound (1,268 yen low on May 18 to 1,749 yen high on Jan 23) with no breakout. Investor commentary often cites lack of in-house products. Second, COMTURE sits outside flashy themes like AI, data centers, quantum computing. Third, while the 48.42% payout ratio creates income appeal, structural wage-inflation margin pressure prevents both growth and income investors from becoming decisive buyers.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -24.4% YoY because operating margins compressed (profit lines under pressure). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥1373 (4-7) – ¥2269 (7-23); close ¥1710 (-24.4% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥36341M (+6.3%), OP ¥4630M (+0.7%), NP ¥3160M (+0.8%). 業績 unclassified / 株価 ✕.

3858 Ubiquitous AI Corporation SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -14.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Ubiquitous AI (formerly Ubiquitous) develops embedded-software middleware: connectivity software (TCP/IP, Bluetooth, Wi-Fi) and security products. The company renamed itself Ubiquitous AI to signal a strategic pivot to Edge AI. How did business move? FY3/2026: revenue 3.924 billion yen (-5.2%), operating loss 0.201 billion yen, net loss 0.518 billion yen due to goodwill impairment from past M&A and restructuring costs. Dividend: 0 yen. Stock: 355 yen (-2.74%), YTD high 615 yen, YTD low 277 yen. Why did business move this way? First, goodwill from past acquisitions was deemed unrecoverable and a lump-sum impairment was booked. Second, the company is restructuring — cleaning up unprofitable businesses and adjusting headcount. Third, the embedded-middleware core itself shrank 5.2%, and the Edge AI business has not yet reached monetization. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite the losses, the stock is oddly stable — the thin float means sellers are also scarce. Second, as of December 2024, 70.5% of all listed Japanese companies (2,870 names) had no analyst price targets, and small-caps like Ubiquitous AI are easily dropped. Third, the name change and AI-strategy signaling drew brief thematic attention (the 615 yen January high), but with no concrete Edge-AI contract wins, the stock settled back into a thin-trading range.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -14.2% YoY because operating margins compressed (profit lines under pressure). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥277 (4-7) – ¥615 (11-12); close ¥363 (-14.2% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥4138M (+19.0%), OP ¥105M (+47.9%), NP ¥97M. 業績 mixed / 株価 ✕.

3914 JIG-SAW株式会社 other small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -20.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: JIG-SAW (TSE Growth) is an IT services company that provides 24/7 monitoring and operations for cloud infrastructure. When companies use cloud services like AWS or Azure, JIG-SAW continuously monitors uptime and handles troubleshooting and recovery — that is the flagship System Management business. They also offer IoT services. How did business move: FY12/2025 full-year: revenue +4.39% to ¥3.625 billion, net profit -12.64% to ¥418 million — a profit decline driven by upfront investment. FY12/2026 Q1 rebounded sharply: revenue +17.4% to ¥1.039 billion (first quarter ever above ¥1 billion), operating profit +64.2%, ordinary profit +60.8%. Full-year guidance is not disclosed. Dividend remains zero. Why did business move this way: First, the flagship System Management business is solid — stock-type recurring revenue is compounding. Second, IoT segment new-service rollouts added incremental revenue. Third, the upfront investment from FY12/2025 entered a monetization phase from Q1, so margins jumped. Why did the stock move this way: Despite the strong Q1, the stock continues its multi-year downtrend. First, full-year guidance is not disclosed — investors find the "invisible future" a structural worry. Second, JIG-SAW is a small-cap on TSE Growth with limited major-broker analyst coverage. Third, the zero-dividend policy excludes income-oriented long-term investors. Q1 prompted a short-term bounce but a multi-quarter beat streak is needed to reverse the long downtrend.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -20.4% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥2,208 (Oct 17) – ¥4,290 (Oct 31); close ¥2,690 (-20.4% YoY). FY12/2025 rev ¥3.63B (+4%), OP ¥549M (-1%), NP ¥418M (-13%). 業績 ✕ (revenue growth decelerating + NP -13% material; margin compression continuing from 2024) / 株価 ✕.

Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: oRo (TSE Prime) runs two businesses. Cloud Solutions provides the ZAC series for project-based businesses (IT, consultancies, ad agencies). Marketing Solutions provides digital-marketing support services. How did business move: FY12/2026 Q1: revenue +18.7% to ¥2.35 billion, OP +22.3% to ¥812 million. Full-year guidance: revenue ¥9.572 billion (+15.2%), OP ¥2.93 billion (+10.6%), net profit ¥2.147 billion (+13.2%). ZAC assumes 104 new contracts (+22 YoY) and ARPA ¥599,900 (+¥46,000). Dividend ¥50/year (¥25 interim + ¥25 year-end). Why did business move this way: First, ZAC won large new contracts and became the main revenue driver. Second, Marketing Solutions profit grew sharply. Third, ZAC ARPA expansion improved revenue quality. Caveat: net margin compressed YoY due to fixed-cost loads. Why did the stock move this way: Despite solid results the stock is -29% over 12 months, closing around ¥1,962 on May 15. First, minkabu flags "Analyst coverage: none" — zero institutional coverage. Second, forward PER of ~15x denies oRo a SaaS-growth premium. Third, the dual portfolio dilutes valuation.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -18.1% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.

4053 Sun* Inc. SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -35.7%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Sun Asterisk (TSE Prime, often Sun*) is an IT company that does "digital product co-creation". The flagship Creative & Engineering business uses substantial offshore development (large engineering bases in Vietnam). Also runs an education business. How did business move: FY12/2025: revenue +9.3% to ¥14.835 billion, OP -27.1% to ¥1.052 billion, ordinary -31.4% to ¥990 million. Q4 alone was extreme: ordinary profit -99.1% to ¥2 million. FY12/2026 guidance: revenue ¥18.201 billion, OP ¥1.714 billion, ordinary ¥1.9 billion (+90.4%) — sharp rebound expected, marking 7 consecutive years of revenue growth. Dividend remains zero since 2020. Why did business move this way: First, the original investment thesis broke — customer growth slowed below plan, and increased use of external partners dragged margins. Second, the FY12/2026 rebound is heavily a bounce off the depressed prior-year base. Third, the IFRS transition and education-business restructuring add accounting-level discontinuity. Why did the stock move this way: Despite the recovery profile the stock remains depressed, trading near ¥396. First, minkabu flags "Analyst coverage: none" — zero institutional research. Second, the shock of the August-2025 downward revision broke investor trust. Third, mid-cap value falls short of many large-institution minimum-size thresholds. Until margins re-expand, upside stays heavy.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -35.7% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

4344 Sourcenext Corporation SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -12.6%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Sourcenext (4344) was originally known for the PC security software 'Virus Security ZERO.' In recent years, AI translation device 'Pocketalk' (operated by subsidiary Pocketalk Inc.) has grown into the main business, with expanding US sales to education and medical institutions. How did business move? FY3/2025: revenue 11.455 billion yen (+1.1%), gross profit 5.336 billion yen (-12.6%), operating loss 3.48 billion yen, net loss 3.8 billion yen. FY12/2025: revenue 9.274 billion yen, operating loss 1.308 billion yen. FY12/2026 Q1 ordinary loss 323 million yen. Pocketalk overseas sales +20.7%. The company withholds FY2025-2026 guidance. Why did business move this way? First, payroll and outsourcing costs for global expansion of Pocketalk (especially US) front-loaded beyond expectations. Second, the new 'Pocketalk S2' launch triggered returns of older units, forcing inventory write-downs. Third, the direct-sales-site system migration added one-off costs. Pocketalk Inc. is preparing for an IPO with Mizuho Securities as lead. Why did the stock move this way? First, with guidance withheld, institutions can't build models — buyers are structurally absent. Second, the 3-year average PER is uncomputable due to continued losses; only PBR 3.06x is meaningful. Third, expected Pocketalk IPO is the only bright spot, but timing is undetermined.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -12.6% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

4443 Sansan,Inc. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -24.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Sansan (4443) is a Japanese business-cloud company with two flagship products. "Sansan" digitizes paper business cards and shares contact information across the company. "Bill One" is a cloud invoice-receipt and accounts-payable automation service. Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY5/2026 Q3 YTD revenue 39.27 billion yen (+26.1%), adjusted OP 6.09 billion yen (+131.1%) — sharp growth. Bill One sustains +40.7%. Full-year guidance was raised: revenue 53.57-54.00 billion yen (+24-25%), adjusted OP 8.04-8.64 billion yen (+126-143%). Mid-term FY5/2027 adjusted-OP-margin target was lifted to 20-23%. No dividend. Why did business move this way? First, the flagship Sansan grows steadily by lifting existing-customer pricing. Second, Bill One is #1 in cloud invoice-receipt and keeps +40.7% growth. Third, SG&A ratio improvement plus revenue scaling drove strong operating leverage — adjusted OP margin moved from ~15.4% to ~21.9%. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite strong results, rising Japan long-term rates derated high-PER growth names like Sansan — stock fell over 20% from a YTD high of 2,614 yen in roughly two months. Second, concern that Bill One's rapid scale-up is inflating personnel costs faster than profit. Third, US AI-firm technology announcements drove a broader death of SaaS selloff. SBI Securities' 2,900 yen target sits well above the 1,600-1,700 yen stock.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -24.4% YoY because operating margins compressed (NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥1,590 (Dec 11) – ¥2,614 (Feb 17); close ¥1,734 (−24.4% YoY). FY5/2025 rev ¥43.2B (+27.4%), OP ¥2.8B (+109.9%), NP ¥424M (−55.5%). 業績 mixed (rev/OP up, NP down) / 株価 ✕.

4448 kubell Co., Ltd. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -40.3%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: kubell (4448) was renamed in 2024 (formerly Chatwork). It runs Chatwork, a business chat for SMBs with ~7.92 million registered IDs. Recently it has expanded into BPaaS (Business Process as a Service), bundling Chatwork with back-office outsourcing. Listed on TSE Growth. How did business move? FY12/2025 Q3 YTD: revenue 6.948 billion yen (+12.4%), OP 274 million yen (+48.6%), ordinary profit 251 million yen (+48.4%). Recent quarter: revenue +12.5%, OP +400.8% — dramatic profit recovery. BPaaS segment +60.7%. Mid-term plan: FY12/2026 revenue 15 billion yen, CAGR 30%+, long-run EBITDA margin 40% target. Why did business move this way? First, BPaaS rode SMB labor shortages, winning new orders at +60.7%. Second, leveraging Chatwork's 7.92 million-ID base for BPaaS cross-sell is working. Third, cost cuts and headcount optimization drove OP recovery faster than revenue. EPS flipped from negative to positive. Why did the stock move this way? First, the 2020-22 COVID collaboration-tool premium collapsed — stock fell ~-90% from a listing high of 2,624 yen to a listing low of 261 yen in March 2026, a structural derating. Second, persistent distrust of long-term competitive position vs Slack, Microsoft Teams, and LINE WORKS. Third, +400% OP growth comes off a near-zero base — absolute yen is still small.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -40.3% YoY because operating margins compressed (profit lines under pressure). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

4480 MEDLEY,INC. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -38.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: MEDLEY (4480) runs digital platforms for the medical/care industry across two main businesses. The HR Platform operates Job Medley and Job Medley Academy training. The Medical Platform provides DX solutions to healthcare institutions. Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY12/2026 Q1: revenue 10.189 billion yen (+25.2%), EBITDA 631 million yen (+48.1%) — strong growth, but operating loss of 67 million yen. Full-year guidance: revenue 46.4 billion yen (+26.1%), EBITDA 5.8 billion yen (+20.3%), OP 2.95 billion yen (+37.2%), ordinary 3.25 billion yen (+47.6%), net profit 1.8 billion yen (+84.5%). Medium-term target: revenue 100 billion yen and EBITDA 20 billion yen by FY12/2029. Dividend likely none but UNVERIFIED. Why did business move this way? First, both customer bases expanded — HR-platform establishments to 457,000 (+2.0%), medical-platform institutions to 23,000 (+1.2%) — supporting +25.2% revenue. Second, EBITDA +48.1% shows strong pre-D&A cash-generation improvement. Third, front-loaded growth investments drove Q1 operating loss; full-year still guides to strong profit growth. Why did the stock move this way? First, on Feb 13, 2026 full-year earnings, stock fell -11.76% (-253 yen) — investors distrust the revenue grows but profit doesn't follow pattern. Second, the Q1 print also saw -6.19% — cool reception again. Third, valuation already elevated (forward PER 32.6x, PBR 3.97x), and the margin gap vs industry leader M3 (OP margin 26.9% vs MEDLEY's 12.9%) is a sticking point.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -38.5% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

4488 AI inside Inc. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -20.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: AI inside provides DX Suite, a cloud-based AI-OCR service, listed on the TSE Growth market. Users scan paper documents and the AI automatically reads and converts text/numbers into digital data. The service targets Japan's heavy reliance on paper-based workflows. How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: revenue 4.747 billion yen (+7.9%), net profit 351 million yen — a turn-to-profit (prior year was a loss). DX Suite contracts grew to ~3,105 and users to ~66,812, with churn at an extremely low 0.59%. Equity ratio rose to 72.6%. FY3/2027 guidance projects further growth, specific figures UNVERIFIED. No dividend. Why did business move this way? First, DX Suite license count and user count keep rising as Japanese corporate paperless / DX demand expands structurally. Second, churn at 0.59% is exceptionally low — textbook SaaS retention. Third, cost structure improved and the in-house AI processing engine lifted gross margin. Why did the stock move this way? First, re-rating hopes from the turn-to-profit. But FY26 is the 'first year' of profitability. Second, as a TSE Growth small-cap, analyst coverage is thin. Third, in the AI-OCR market, global giants Google and Microsoft are embedding similar OCR features, and the competitive threat caps the upside.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -20.1% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥2350 (4-7) – ¥4995 (1-15); close ¥2600 (-20.1% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥4400M (+5.0%), OP ¥200M (-20.0%), NP ¥-497M (loss). 業績 mixed / 株価 ✕.

9432 NTT,Inc. telecom large ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -0.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview NTT (Nippon Telegraph and Telephone) is Japan's largest telecom group — comprising NTT Docomo (mobile), NTT East/West (fixed-line and fiber), NTT Data (corporate IT). Government holds roughly 32.25% of issued shares. Earnings movement FY3/2026: revenue ¥14.41T (+5.1%, record-level), net ¥1.037T (+3.7%). Dividend hiked for the 16th consecutive year to ¥5.4. However, FY3/2027 net profit guided -5.5% to ¥980B. Mid-term plan revised to '2030 powered by AIOWN', pushing the EBITDA ¥4T target back 3 years to FY3/2031. Why earnings moved this way 1) Global Solutions drove consolidated growth. 2) Domestic ICT declined on competition, price-cut pressure, and 5G capex. 3) FY27 decline factors: high US/EU rates pushing interest expense, M&A debt, AI/data-center depreciation, NTT Data integration costs. Why the stock moved this way 1) FY27 profit decline + 3-year deferral of mid-term targets — stock held in ¥149.8-¥152.6 range, PER 12.4x, yield 3.60%. 2) Government-share-sale overhang (~32.25%) acts as a ceiling. 3) July 2023 1-for-25 stock split widened retail base; now positioned as 'stable income' rather than 'growth.'
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -0.2% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (flat — growth normalizing, stock range-bound):* stock ¥135 (Apr 7) – ¥167 (Aug 20); close ¥158 (−0.2% YoY, essentially flat). **FY3/2025 actual: revenue ¥13,702B (+2.5%), operating profit ¥1,647B (−14.3%), net profit ¥1,002B (−21.6%)** per Tempest — OP and NP DECLINED. Sign summary: revenue rose modestly; OP fell −14.3%; NP fell −21.6%; close flat. 業績 mixed (rev up, OP/NP down) / 株価 ✕ (close flat-to-down).

9444 Toshin Holdings Co.,Ltd other small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -33.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company Overview Toshin Holdings (TSE Standard) is a 1988-founded holding company. Its core business was operating au and SoftBank mobile retail shops centered on the Chubu region. The company is currently in corporate-reorganization proceedings — a special-situation name. Earnings Movement FY3/2026 Q3 cumulative: revenue 13.04 billion yen (+2.2% YoY), ordinary 75 million yen (-43.6%), Q3 net 426 million yen (-35.6%). FY3/2027 guidance UNVERIFIED. Dividend effectively suspended. Total liabilities approximately 162 billion yen. Why the earnings moved this way 1) Improper-accounting issue uncovered at a subsidiary directly hit profits. 2) Mobile-retail industry itself in structural decline. 3) Accounting scandal broke covenants with lending banks. Nov 22, 2025 TSE designated 'Special Attention Issue'; May 8, 2026 filed for corporate reorganization at Tokyo District Court. Why the stock moved this way 1) May 29, 2026 stock surged +50 yen (+27.93%) to 229 yen — speculative limit-up after the reorganization filing, NOT fundamentals. 2) Institutional investors fully withdrew. 3) Price formation now depends on legal outcomes — reorganization plan approval, 100% capital reduction, dilution risk. Not recommended for general investors.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
Gap framing: Revenue kept growing in 2025 on continued demand for the core business, BUT the stock derated -33.8% YoY because operating margins compressed (OP down and NP down). The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.

9468 KADOKAWA CORPORATION content_games mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +1.0%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company Overview KADOKAWA (TSE Prime, 9468) is a major Japanese entertainment conglomerate operating across publishing, anime production, games (FromSoftware), education/EdTech (N High School / S High School), and live events — all built around IP. Overseas IP expansion and global monetization is the mid-term theme. Earnings Movement FY3/2026 actuals: revenue 282.91 billion yen (+1.8% YoY), OP 8.10 billion yen (-51.3%), ordinary 11.70 billion yen (-34.1%), net 1.28 billion yen — sharp profit decline. Guidance cut from 16.7B to 10.3B in November, final 8.10B. A 2.7B yen extraordinary loss for goodwill amortization at Doga Kobo. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue 300.3B (+6.1%), OP 10.1B (+24.7%), NP 5.8B (+353.7%). Why the earnings moved this way 1) Publishing & IP-Creation OP fell -51.6%. Raising the number of new-author titles fragmented marketing resources. 2) Anime & Live-Action segment swung to operating loss. 3) Games declined on absence of prior-year mega-titles. Only Education / EdTech grew (revenue +13.5%, OP +19.4%). Why the stock moved this way 1) Repeated within-year downward revisions damaged 'guidance credibility.' 2) Streaming platforms (Netflix etc.) have become pickier — mid-tier anime can no longer count on overseas licensing. 3) FY27 OP +24.7% sketches a recovery, but until a new flagship hit or concrete overseas IP success appears, re-rating remains capped.
Pattern (bucket-templated):

The gap is the market pricing in the margin pressure ahead of any margin recovery — recoverable if margins normalize, harder to break if the compression is structural.

▸ Loss-making SaaS de-rated — market stopped paying for "growth-at-any-cost" 4 names
In plain terms: high-PER SaaS companies that grew revenue strongly but kept posting losses or thin margins. The market stopped tolerating "growth at any cost" and rotated capital out of the cohort. Examples in this bucket: Money Forward, freee, JMDC, Appier Group. Rule used: ticker-specific override for documented loss-making / thin-margin SaaS names. Why this matters: this is a cohort-wide derating, not company-specific failure. Industry commentary even labels it "SaaSの死" (the death of SaaS) — investors shifted from "growth-only" to "Rule of 40" (revenue growth + operating margin ≥ 40%) as the new bar. If these companies pivot to profitability or hit the Rule of 40 milestone, the cohort can re-rate together. What to watch: management language shifting from growth-only to profitability, the first quarter of sustained GAAP profit, and broker upgrades that follow a clean margin print.
3994 Money Forward,Inc. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -3.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Money Forward (TSE Prime) is a major fintech that develops and operates cloud accounting, HR/labor, and expense-management SaaS for SMEs and sole proprietors. The flagship "Money Forward Cloud" puts back-office work in the cloud. Also runs "Money Forward ME" personal-finance app and Fintech services. How did business move: FY11/2026 Q1: revenue +25.3% to ¥14.67 billion, SaaS ARR +34.2% to ¥44.303 billion. Operating profit flipped POSITIVE at ¥168 million (vs prior -¥580 million), net profit ¥1.828 billion (vs prior -¥1.12 billion), adjusted EBITDA ¥2.81 billion (2.4x YoY) — a record-high quarterly OP. Business-segment ARR +36.8%; Fintech ARR +90%. Full-year guidance: revenue ¥53.4-57.55 billion, adjusted EBITDA ¥8-10 billion. New shareholder-incentive program launches. Why did business move this way: First, the operating-profit flip against analyst consensus expecting an OP loss — years of upfront investment finally clicked into operating leverage. Second, Business-segment SaaS ARR accelerated +36.8% despite a larger base, while Fintech grew +90%. Third, the planned July-2026 launch of "AI Cowork" supports price hikes and ARPU expansion. Why did the stock move this way: First, the day after results (April 15, 2026) the stock surged +¥700 (+18.05%) to ¥4,577 — a massive beat triggered re-rating. Second, on May 1, 2026 a GitHub unauthorized-access incident emerged, temporarily suspending bank-account integration; PTS shares briefly fell ~2%. Third, the new shareholder-incentive program raised long-hold incentive. With PER around 137x, continued quarterly profitability is needed for the next leg.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock -3.4%; bucket=saas_derated.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (first-ever NP positive — operational inflection delivered, stock ambivalent):* stock ¥3,330 (Apr 9) – ¥6,794 (Aug 19); year-end close ¥4,668 (−3.4% YoY). **FY11/2025 actual (announced January 2026 — POST-calendar-window): revenue ¥50,350M (+24.7% YoY), operating loss ¥(2,653)M (loss narrowed from ¥(4,735)M), net profit ¥1,587M per senior-verified primary source (FIRST-EVER FULL-YEAR NET PROFIT IN COMPANY HISTORY)**; irbank.net reports NP ¥2,463M (consolidated vs parent-attributable line-item difference); Tempest reports profit ¥1,587M matching senior. Operating loss continued to narrow; net profit turn-to-black achieved on a combination of operating-loss narrowing + one-off positive non-operating items per management commentary. Stock action during calendar 2025: massive intra-year recovery (+104% from Apr 9 low ¥3,330 to Aug 19 high ¥6,794) on quarterly trajectory data as Q1/Q2/Q3 results showed continued ARR growth + operating-loss narrowing — but stock gave back gains by year-end (close ¥4,668, −3.4% YoY) suggesting market skepticism about durability of the inflection. Sign summary: revenue rose +24.7% YoY; operating loss narrowed +¥2.1B; **net profit first-ever positive ¥1,587M from prior net loss ¥6,331M**; shares had +104% mid-year rally (Apr → Aug) on quarterly inflection signal then gave back gains (close −3.4% YoY). 業績 ◎ (NP first-ever positive + revenue acceleration + OP loss narrowing) / 株価 mixed (close down −3.4% YoY but mid-year +104% rally; year-end ambivalent on inflection durability).

The senior-verified ¥1,587M FY11/2025 NP from the primary 短信 is the load-bearing figure for the first-ever-positive claim. Operating-loss line is identical across sources; revenue is identical. The 株価 mixed call for 2025 is honest writer judgment — close down but mid-year rally was substantial, and the 株価 unclassified outcome is the expected discipline outcome (canonical block surfaces as unclassified + split_ambiguous, the system flags for human review).

4180 Appier Group, Inc. ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Appier Group (4180) is a Taiwan-headquartered AI marketing-platform company. Provides AI-native SaaS that optimizes digital ad delivery. Global footprint across Taiwan, Singapore, Japan, US. Listed on TSE Mothers (now Growth) in 2021. How did business move? FY12/2025 revenue 43.737 billion yen (+28.4%), ARR 48.26 billion yen (+33.1%), OP 2.976 billion yen (+50.2%), EBITDA 6.85 billion yen (+39.4%). Net profit fell to 2.56 billion yen (-12.6%) on M&A costs. FY12/2026 Q1: revenue 12.102 billion yen (+29.4%), OP 185 million yen (+153.4%). FY12/2026 guidance: revenue 54.013 billion yen (+23.5%), OP 4.313 billion yen (+44.9%). Why did business move this way? First, AI marketing-platform demand keeps growing double-digits globally. The strategic move to integrate generative AI / LLMs across the entire product suite is simultaneously lifting ARPU and bringing in new customers. Second, the M&A-led growth strategy adds capabilities but acquisition costs temporarily compress net profit. Third, Q1 OP +153% came from gross-margin improvement and disciplined cost management. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite strong fundamentals the stock is sharply down from IPO. Second, the market constantly weighs competition with global giants like Salesforce and Adobe in Martech. Third, relative valuation (PSR/PER) is still elevated — small misses trigger selling.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=saas_derated.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (record-year #5 — operating margin expanding):* stock ¥991 (Nov 19) – ¥1,875 (Feb 14); Nov 2025 ¥991 was a new multi-year low (only marginally above 2024 Aug ¥928). **FY12/2025 actual (announced ~Feb 2026): revenue ¥43,737M (+28.4% YoY), operating profit ¥2,976M (+50.2%), ARR ¥48,259M (+33.1%), EBITDA ¥6,854M (+39.4%), net profit ¥2,558M (-12.6% on tax/other), operating margin expanding** per IR + ARR data; 5 consecutive years of record-high operating profit. Sign summary: revenue rose +28.4% YoY; operating profit rose +50.2%; ARR rose +33.1%; EBITDA rose +39.4%; operating margin expanding; shares fell to ¥991 multi-year low (Nov 19) despite the +50% OP growth — stock didn't recognize the continued acceleration. 業績 ◎◎ (record OP + margin expansion + ARR acceleration) / 株価 ✕ (still under-priced de-rated grower).

Cautious side: stock still well below the 2021 IPO peak ¥2,559 (Nov 2025 low ¥991 = -61% off ATH); the 2021 peak was post-IPO mania at a level unjustified by the loss-making business at the time, so it may not be a meaningful target; net profit declined -12.6% in FY25 despite strong OP — needs human verification on whether tax effect or operational issue; competitive AI-SaaS pressure from larger global players (Salesforce, Adobe AI tools).

4478 freee K.K. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -1.0%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: freee (4478) provides cloud accounting, HR/labor, and sales-management SaaS for small and mid-sized businesses, competing directly with Money Forward (3994). The vision is an end-to-end cloud platform spanning tax filing, accounting, payroll, and sales. Listed on TSE Growth. How did business move? FY6/2026 Q3 YTD revenue 30.843 billion yen (+29.3%), platform ARR 42.481 billion yen (+23.2%) — sustained high growth. Ordinary profit fell -65.5% to 357 million yen due to front-loaded investment. Full-year guidance was raised: revenue 41.93 billion yen (+26.0%), adjusted OP 2.52 billion yen (+33.7%). The prior period (FY6/2025) marked freee's first-ever net profit of 1.37 billion yen. Market cap ~125.9 billion yen, forward PER 91.33x, PBR 6.18x. Why did business move this way? First, continued front-loaded investment in engineer hiring, feature expansion, and enterprise sales — ARR and customer base keep growing. Second, the software capitalization accounting treatment helped produce the prior-year P&L profit, but core cash-generation remains limited. Third, +29% revenue against declining ordinary profit means a step-up in margin profile is not yet visible. Why did the stock move this way? First, even after FY6/2025's first-ever profitability, the FY6/2026 outlook implied FLAT margins — stock fell over -14% in two days post-earnings. Second, the broader death of SaaS theme derates business-software names. Third, top rival Money Forward swung to operating profit in Q1 April 2026 and surged sharply — the contrast made the read on freee even harsher.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock -1.0%; bucket=saas_derated.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (first-ever profit year):* stock ¥2,800 (Jan 15) – ¥4,330 (Aug 5); close ¥3,005 (−1.0% YoY). FY6/2025 rev ¥33.27B (+30.8%), OP ¥610M (FIRST-EVER OP turn-to-black), NP ¥1,370M (FIRST-EVER NP turn-to-black). 業績 ◎ (revenue acceleration + first-ever OP/NP profit) / 株価 ✕ (close −1% YoY — close flat despite operational inflection).

4483 JMDC Inc. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +1.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: JMDC is one of Japan's largest medical big-data companies. It collects anonymized medical claims and health-checkup data, then sells it to pharma marketing teams, insurance underwriters, and medical research institutions. It also runs a telemedicine platform. Parent company is Omron (over ~50% stake after the 2023 TOB). How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 cumulative: revenue 36.488 billion yen (+23.2%), operating profit 7.767 billion yen (+37.1%). Full-year company guidance is OP 11.5 billion yen (+31.9%). Q3 cumulative ordinary profit is on pace for a record high. Specific FY3/2027 guidance is UNVERIFIED. Dividend yield is only ~0.6%. Why did business move this way? First, the flagship Health-Big-Data segment grew +26.4%, driven by structural pharma and insurance demand for data. Second, data-sales has a very high marginal margin: once data is collected, selling it to one more customer costs almost nothing. Third, monetization of the telemedicine business is progressing. Why did the stock move this way? First, the expectation gap. Q3 progress ratio of 65.0% is below the 5-year average of 67.1%, and full-year guidance slightly trailed analyst consensus. Second, the parent-subsidiary listing structure with Omron owning the majority limits free float. Third, medical-data is a specialized niche sector with thin analyst coverage.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock +1.5%; bucket=saas_derated.

▸ Orphan — strong fundamentals but no analyst coverage 4 names
In plain terms: companies whose business is performing well — but almost no broker covers them and institutional ownership is near zero. The stock drifts because there is simply nobody telling the story to capital allocators. Examples in this bucket: HENNGE, Cybozu, TechMatrix, JAPAN MATERIAL. Rule used: ticker-specific override for names with documented coverage gaps and small institutional ownership. Why this matters: about 70% of TSE-listed companies have ZERO analyst coverage (industry stat). When growth is durable but nobody writes about it, the stock can stay cheap for years. But the trigger is mechanical — when ONE broker initiates coverage with a Buy, the pool of potential buyers expands overnight and the stock can re-rate sharply. What to watch: new broker initiations, IR-activity ramp-up (English presentations, overseas NDRs), TSE PBR-improvement disclosure progress, and any change in cross-shareholding that could free up float.
3762 TechMatrix Corporation SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: TechMatrix is an independent Japanese IT company providing corporate IT infrastructure across two main pillars: (1) "Information Infrastructure" — reselling and maintaining cybersecurity products (Cortex XSIAM etc., SOC services) and networking gear; (2) "Application Services (CRM etc.)" — customer-service solutions like "FastHelp" for contact centers. It also operates a medical-image cloud service "NOBORI". Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year company guidance: revenue ¥73.0 billion (+12.5%), OP ¥7.6 billion (+14.0%), net income attributable to parent owners ¥4.88 billion (+20.2%) — projected 11th consecutive year of record-high profit. FY3/2025 actuals were revenue ¥64.882 billion (+21.7%), OP ¥6.668 billion (+14.0%), net income ¥4.06 billion (+14.7%). Dividend rising from FY3/2025 annual ¥34 to FY3/2026 ¥36. Why did business move this way? First, Information Infrastructure cloud-security products are strong — XDR/SOC offerings like Cortex XSIAM are winning new customers and renewing existing contracts. Second, CRM offerings like "FastHelp" are capturing contact-center DX demand for stable growth. Third, tightening information-security regulations are structurally raising corporate security spend across Japan. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite 11 consecutive years of record profits, in IT services "compounders without a dramatic narrative" lack the eye-catching themes that attract growth investors. Second, the relatively elevated PBR reflects "the market already pricing in high expectations", so even minor misses trigger corrections. Third, very limited overseas business means no global-rerating catalyst. UNVERIFIED: FY3/2026 full-year actuals, FY3/2027 guidance, and the NOBORI medical-image-cloud revenue scale could not be verified.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=orphan_no_coverage.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (record-year confirmation — clean ◎):* FY3/2025 actual (announced May 9, 2025): revenue ¥64,882M (+21.7% YoY, +¥11,578M increase, **record high**), operating profit ¥6,668M (+14.0%, +¥818M), net profit ¥4,060M (+14.7%). **11 consecutive years of record profit** per management context. FY3/2026 guidance: revenue ¥73,000M (+12.5%), OP ¥7,600M (+13.9%), net ¥4,880M (+20.2%). Stock ¥1,720 (Apr 7) – ¥2,584 (Jan 31). Jan 2025 was actually the prior-year-end momentum continuation; through 2025 the stock declined from Jan high to Apr low, then recovered (per the Nikkei range data — high was Jan, low was Apr). Stock-move catalyst: FY3/2025 record results + 11-year streak + raised FY3/2026 guidance + Firmus acquisition contribution. 業績 ◎ on record results + acceleration / 株価 mixed (Jan peak followed by spring decline — profit-taking after the strong 2024 rally is plausible but no source names it).

Cautious side: import-reseller business model dependence on North American technology vendors (FX-sensitive, supplier-relationship risk); Firmus integration risk; concentration in network security as a single growth driver.

Sign summary: revenue rose +21.7% YoY; operating profit rose +14.0%; net profit rose +14.7%; record revenue, record operating profit, 11 consecutive years of record profit; FY3/2026 guidance raised; shares rallied to ¥2,584 high (Jan 31); business strong, stock strong.

4475 HENNGE K.K. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +1.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: HENNGE (4475) is a Japanese corporate cloud-security company. Its flagship HENNGE One bundles single sign-on, email encryption, and identity/access management — a leading IDaaS vendor in Japan. Listed on TSE Growth. How did business move? FY9/2025 full-year: revenue 10.924 billion yen (+30.59%), net profit 1.358 billion yen (+64.18%), ordinary profit 1.85 billion yen (+85.4%) — 4 consecutive years of record profit. HENNGE One hit its 10 billion yen ARR target. FY9/2026 guidance: revenue 12.834 billion yen (+17.5%), OP 2.057 billion yen (+14.7%), ordinary 2.073 billion yen (+11.9%), net profit 1.595 billion yen (+17.4%) — 5th consecutive year of growth expected. Dividend lifted from 5 to 6 yen. Long-term ARR 20 billion yen target by FY9/2029. Why did business move this way? First, HENNGE One has a long-standing leading position in domestic IDaaS with high retention. Second, enterprise cloud adoption and rising cyber threats keep demand for unified security + usability solutions. Third, upselling features and license-price increases also lifted revenue quality. Why did the stock move this way? First, fundamentals back the stock — H1 FY9/2026 ARR rose +14.7% to 11.904 billion yen. Second, valuation is already high (PER 40x-plus) limiting upside. Third, thin major-broker coverage means structurally limited institutional buying. Capital is concentrating in AI/data-center themes, derating infrastructure-security SaaS relatively.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock +1.8%; bucket=orphan_no_coverage.

4776 Cybozu,Inc. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -2.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Cybozu makes business collaboration tools and cloud services. The flagship is "kintone", a no-code cloud platform for building business apps. Other products include "Cybozu Office" and "Garoon" groupware. Cloud contracts total about 70,000 companies with 3.6 million user licenses. How did business move? FY12/2025: revenue ¥37.43 billion (+26.1%), OP ¥10.11 billion (+106.4%, more than doubled), ordinary ¥10.33 billion (+93.5%), net ¥7.08 billion (+99.2%). kintone alone: ¥21.69 billion (+33.9%). Cloud-related revenue ¥34.485 billion (+28.7%). FY12/2026 guide: revenue ¥42.17 billion (+12.7%), OP ¥10.51 billion (+4.1%). Q1: revenue ¥10.25 billion (+17.0%), OP ¥3.01 billion (+15.3%). Dividend hike + share buyback also decided. Why did business move this way? First, kintone customer-count growth continues (39,000 domestic companies). Second, pricing + upsell — kintone ARPA rose +27% YoY in Q2. Third, deeper penetration in large-enterprise accounts. Why did the stock move this way? Market cap ¥115.9 billion, forecast PER 16.2x, PBR 6.25x. First, the IR pivot since Feb 2024 continues to improve institutional analyzability. Second, the share-buyback announcement drove a sharp rebound. Third, FY26 OP growth of just +4.1% — held down by upfront costs for the June 2026 kintone AI launch — disappointed some investors.

6055 JAPAN MATERIAL Co., Ltd. SI_IT_services mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -8.9%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Japan Material is an infrastructure-services company for semiconductor factories. It supplies high-purity gas, chemical management, and equipment maintenance at semi fabs. A classic "picks-and-shovels" model. Also handles 3D imaging tools and solar power. How did business move? FY3/2026 revenue ¥57.976 billion (+10.1%), OP ¥14.64 billion (+30.9%). Q3 YTD revenue ¥41.732 billion (+18.0%), OP ¥10.003 billion (+46.1%). FY3/2027 guidance: revenue ¥61 billion (+5.2%), OP ¥15.5 billion (+5.9%). Dividend: ¥24 → ¥27 (+¥3), forecast payout ratio ~30.8%. Why did business move this way? First, the core Electronics segment benefits from expanding semi capex. Second, generative-AI proliferation and data-center demand drive leading-edge semi capex — TSMC Kumamoto, Rapidus Hokkaido are tailwinds. Third, the two-pillar structure captures profit in both customer capex and operating phases. Why did the stock move this way? Past 12 months up +38.94%. First, on May 29, 2026 a mid-tier domestic broker reiterated "Bullish" and raised the target to ¥2,400. Second, within the semi theme, an understated but solid winner reputation is settling in. Structural constraints: small-cap, limited institutional coverage, heavy concentration on a few large customers.

▸ AI disrupted — business model directly threatened by generative AI 3 names
In plain terms: companies whose core business is being eaten by generative AI. Not "AI will help them" — "AI will replace what they do." Examples in this bucket: VisasQ (expert-consulting platform competing with AI search), HEROZ (legacy AI software leapfrogged by LLMs), NSD (person-month SI where AI compresses the engineer-hour cost they bill). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names where Round 2 verification documented AI as a structural threat to the business model. Why this matters: the derating is not a temporary glitch — it is the market re-pricing the long-term TAM (total addressable market) of these businesses. These stocks can stay cheap for years unless the company successfully pivots to "AI-augmented" services or finds a defensible niche (proprietary data moats, regulated industry barriers, etc.). What to watch: explicit AI-strategy pivots from management, partnerships with AI infrastructure players (OpenAI, Anthropic, hyperscalers), and the competitive response (price cuts, service redesign, new product launches).
4382 HEROZ, Inc. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -50.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: HEROZ (4382) is an AI company listed on TSE Standard, famous for the shogi AI 'Ponanza' that beat professional players. Current core business is B2B AI services: 'AIX' (enterprise AI agents) and 'AI Security' (AI-powered cyber defense). Recurring-revenue share exceeds 60%. How did business move? FY4/2024: revenue +62.4% to 4.841 billion yen, OP +75% to 451 million yen. FY4/2026 H1: revenue 3.098 billion yen (+9.6%), OP 258 million yen (+191.2%). Q3 cumulative: revenue 4.687 billion yen (+7.8%), OP 358 million yen (+126.4%). Full-year guidance projects revenue 6.4 billion yen (+7.9%) and OP 500 million yen (+63.2%). FY4/2026 results to be announced June 12, 2026. Forecast dividend 0%, ROE 1.1%, ROA 0.62%. Why did business move this way? First, riding the AI wave, the AIX segment accelerated by implementing generative AI features. Second, AI Security also kept growing double digits. Third, continued front-loaded investment keeps net profit thin. Subsidiary underperformance prompted the downward revision. Why did the stock move this way? First, PBR is 0.68x — below 1x. The market is signaling 'the future value of the business is below current net assets,' an unusually low valuation for an AI-related company. Second, weak profitability — forecast ROE 1.1% / ROA 0.62% are extremely low. Third, with the June 12 full-year results pending, the market is in wait-and-see mode; the stock was 701 yen flat at low levels.
Pattern (bucket-templated):

4490 VisasQ Inc. internet_platform small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: -55.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Note: the input file labels 4490 as 'Visional,' but the actual issuer of ticker 4490 is VisasQ — Visional's correct ticker is 4194. VisasQ runs Japan's leading spot-consulting marketplace, matching companies with subject-matter experts for hourly advisory calls. In 2021 it acquired US-based Coleman Research Group as the foundation for overseas expansion. How did business move? FY2/2026 Q3 cumulative: operating profit +12.1% YoY, with progress vs. full-year plan at 87.1% — on track. Ordinary profit was 901 million yen. But Q2 standalone was weak: revenue 4.774 billion yen (-3.3%), OP 499 million yen (-14.2%). Full-year company guidance projects OP of 1.03 billion yen (-16.1%) — 'revenue up, profit down.' In April 2026, an additional impairment loss on Coleman-related software-development costs was booked as a special loss. No dividend. Why did business move this way? First, domestic spot-consulting is steady, but US Coleman continues to drag. Second, the Q3 profit improvement came mainly from short-term cost control. Third, continued growth investment makes profits easy to compress. Why did the stock move this way? First, the legacy of the ~10 billion yen Coleman acquisition and the subsequent cumulative ~14.4 billion yen impairment loss / special loss. Second, the April 2026 additional impairment confirmed that 'goodwill risk isn't over.' Third, the spot-consulting business model is inherently labor-intensive, so it can't reach pure-SaaS-like margins.

9759 NSD CO.,LTD. SI_IT_services mid ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +2.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: NSD (9759) is a Japanese independent IT company providing system development, maintenance, and operations services. Priority area is DAS (Digital Architecture Solution) — cloud-based DX system development. Also covers financial IT, industrial IT, and security products. Labor-intensive model. How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: revenue 117.81B yen (+9.3% YoY), OP 19.07B (+13.2%), ordinary 19.33B (+13.4%). DAS segment grew +15.8% to 57.58B. FY3/2027 ordinary profit guidance 19.7B — eighth consecutive record. Dividend: 96 yen for FY26, 97 yen planned for FY27. Total return ratio 70%+ and payout ratio 50%+, combined with buybacks. Why did business move this way? First, large-enterprise DX demand pushed DAS up +15.8%, well above the overall +9.3% — mix shift. Second, financial IT and industrial IT both grew simultaneously. Third, security products also contributed. Net/operating margins are trending up. Why did the stock move this way? First, despite consecutive record earnings, the stock fell to a YTD low of 2,509 yen on May 14, 2026 (10-year high was 3,670 yen in Sept 2025), leaving ~60% upside vs consensus target of 4,200 yen unrewarded. Second, the market view is that NSD is not a SaaS company — it's a staffing/maintenance shop most exposed to AI-driven cost compression, and peers like NRI and TIS are ahead in proprietary-SaaS and AI monetization. Third, the 97-yen dividend and 70%+ total-return policy support the floor.
