Cautious side: SoftBank Group is structurally an investment holding company (Vision Fund + Arm + portfolio); NP swings dominated by mark-to-market portfolio accounting, NOT operating IT performance. Sub_type re-tagged from 'telecom' (census-file guess) to 'other' (investment holding). Operating profit line not consistently reported under IFRS (NP and revenue are the load-bearing operational signals); op_dir marked 'unknown' across all years to avoid false specificity. **業績 axis interpretation caveat (per senior batch-1 review)**: SBG's reported 業績 reflects investment portfolio mark-to-market direction, NOT operating-business performance. A 業績 ✕ year for SBG means its investment portfolio marked down (Vision Fund growth-stock derating); a 業績 ◎ year means marks went the other way (AI-thesis recovery). **These quadrants are NOT comparable to operating-company quadrants** — SBG's de-rated-grower-like shape (if it appears) would not be the same animal as a SaaS de-rated-grower, and pooling them would corrupt the operating-cohort convergence pattern. **EXCLUSION RULE**: SBG (and other investment-holding 'other' bucket names: ETFs, financial holdings, conglomerates with significant portfolio-mark exposure) must be kept OUT of operating-company convergence cohort analysis.

Sources:
- Tempest local cache: data/tempest/9984/
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/company/history/yprice/?scode=9984
▸ Profitability inflection — market just started believing in durable margin expansion 3 names
In plain terms: companies where the market just shifted its view from "growing but not yet profitable enough" to "now durably profitable, and margins are still expanding." Examples in this bucket: Rakus (OP margin tripled from 7.8% → 26.7% over 3 years), Asteria (30% OP margin + JPYC stable-coin catalyst), BIPROGY (plan to double system-development productivity through AI by 2030, ¥100bn+ incremental revenue expected). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names where the documented 2025 driver was a multi-year margin / profitability inflection becoming visible to the market. Why this matters: profitability inflection is one of the most powerful re-rating engines. EPS rises AND the multiple expands at the same time, so the stock can compound much faster than either alone. But the inflection has to be DURABLE — if margins are inflating because of one-time cost cuts (rather than mix shift, scale benefits, or pricing power), the re-rating reverses. What to watch: the next 2-3 quarters of margin trend, the SOURCE of margin gains (mix shift / scale / pricing vs. one-time cost cuts), and whether the company can keep raising prices without losing customers.
3853 Asteria Corporation SI_IT_services small ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Asteria is an independent software company. Its flagship is ASTERIA Warp, no-code / low-code EAI middleware (top market share in Japan for 19 consecutive years), alongside Platio. Via its US subsidiary Asteria Vision Fund I, the company also invests in Silicon Valley startups including SpaceX. How did business move? FY3/2026 (IFRS): revenue 3.389 billion yen (+6.9%), operating profit 1.025 billion yen (+31.2%), net profit 0.7 billion yen (+35.7%). Operating margin: 30.2%. Stock-type revenue share: 77%. Annual dividend: 9 yen (+1 yen YoY). FY3/2027 guidance projects revenue +9.2%, OP +7.3%, with a 10 yen year-end dividend (first double-digit). Why did business move this way? First, ASTERIA Warp captured demand for the on-premises-to-cloud transition. Second, demand for the data integration that generative-AI deployment requires surged; the all-product AI Asset-hook strategy also launched. Third, the US investment business contributed valuation gains — the SpaceX stake from January 2022 ballooned to a ~$1.75 trillion valuation on IPO speculation in March 2026, a more-than-37x markup. Why did the stock move this way? First, on March 25, 2026, US outlet The Information reported SpaceX's plan to file a confidential SEC submission, and Asteria stock surged +22.60% in a single day to close at 1,519 yen. Second, the day after earnings (May 21), the stock jumped +19.58% to 2,144 yen and hit a YTD high of 2,753 yen on May 26 — a +83.1% three-month surge. Third, integration with the JPYC yen stablecoin (issued October 2025) gave the stock additional thematic exposure.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=profitability_inflection.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (structural reform pays off + thematic momentum — MIXED catalyst):* FY3/2025 actual (announced May 14, 2025): revenue ¥3,171M (+9.0% YoY, record high), operating profit ¥781M, net profit ¥580M (came in well above the ¥300M company forecast — beat-and-raise). "ソフトウェア事業に集中する構造改革が奏功" (structural reform of focusing on software business succeeded) per logmi-finance. ASTERIA Warp + Platio both grew double-digit. Stock ¥396 (Apr 7) – ¥2,510 (Aug 26) per Nikkei — **+6.34x intra-year rally** from spring trough to August peak (a 5-year all-time high). **Stock-move catalyst — TWO drivers, honest split:** (a) **sourced fundamental:** FY3/2025 results beat the forecast (NP ¥580M vs ¥300M forecast), structural-reform-margin-recovery story confirmed [logmi-finance article]; (b) **thematic/opinion coverage exists:** note.com piece "アステリア(3853)急騰！連想買いで狙う「次世代の主役」候補20銘柄！" frames the rally around no-code/DX theme — **no fundamental catalyst beyond the May 2025 results-beat is sourced for the magnitude of the ¥396→¥2,510 rally; the size of the move suggests speculative-thematic overlay on top of the fundamental beat.** FY3/2026 actual (just released May 2026): revenue ¥3,389M (+6.9%), OP ¥1,025M (+31.2%) — confirmed margin expansion. 業績 ◎ (structural reform + revenue + margin) / 株価 ◎ (6x rally — fundamental + thematic, partial Fix-3 caveat on magnitude).

Cautious side: 6x intra-year rally substantially exceeds the magnitude justified by revenue/profit fundamentals alone — thematic-speculative overlay risk (theme rotation could reverse the rally faster than fundamentals support); investment-business valuation gains contributed to FY3/2026 OP boost (lower-quality earnings); historic post-mania-pop pattern in this name suggests fragility.

Sign summary: revenue rose +9.0% YoY; record revenue; net profit ¥580M beat ¥300M forecast on structural-reform success; shares rallied +6.34x from spring trough — fundamental beat plus thematic-momentum overlay (note.com thematic-only coverage; no fundamental catalyst sourced for the rally magnitude); shares rallied to ¥2,510 high (Aug 26); business strong, stock strong.


Sources:
- https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/381739 (FY3/2025 details — structural-reform-success framing + record software business)
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/disclosure/tdnr/20250513548178/ (FY3/2025 通期決算説明会資料 May 13, 2025)

3923 RAKUS Co.,Ltd. SaaS mid ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Rakus (TSE Prime) is a major SME-focused cloud SaaS company. Flagship products are Rakuraku Seisan (expense-reimbursement SaaS, 19,418 cumulative installs, #1 in Japan) and Rakuraku Meisai. Other products include Rakuraku Hanbai, MailDealer, and Rakuraku Kintai. How did business move: FY3/2026 actual: revenue +24.6% to ¥44.297 billion, OP +65.7% to ¥12.5 billion, ordinary +70.7% to ¥17.4 billion, net +51.2% to ¥12.1 billion. On April 27, 2026, ordinary-profit guidance revised UP 9% from ¥16.0B to ¥17.4B; year-end dividend raised from ¥3.4 to ¥7. FY3/2027 guidance reflects IT-talent divestiture: revenue ¥59.7B (-1.0%), OP ¥20.5B (+18.2%), net ¥25.2B (+89.6%). Why did business move this way: First, Rakuraku Seisan saw simultaneous subscriber growth and ARPU expansion from the 2024 invoice-system rollout — cloud-segment revenue is 93.5% monthly recurring. Second, the company shifted from aggressive-TV-ad customer-acquisition to harvest phase, with OP margin in Q4 surging from 19.8% to 30.3% YoY. Third, the IT-talent divestiture clarified the cloud focus. Why did the stock move this way: Up over +50% in 2025, continuing to rise. First, +70.7% ordinary growth was a standout in a struggling SaaS sector. Second, the April 27 upward-revision + dividend-hike shifted investor perception toward "mature cash-generating SaaS". Third, JPX-Nikkei 400 inclusion brings ETF demand, and near-zero debt provides a strong floor.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=profitability_inflection.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (gradual stock recovery on continued fundamental acceleration):* stock ¥848.5 (Jan 17) – ¥1,395 (Sep 29) per Nikkei. FY3/2025 actuals (announced May 14, 2025): **revenue ¥48,904M (+27.3% YoY), operating profit ¥10,192M (+83.3% YoY), operating margin 20.8%** (high for SaaS), recurring profit ¥10,218M (+82.1%), net profit ¥8,003M (+91.2%). Cloud Services growth led by Rakuraku Seisan account-count expansion. Stock-move catalyst: FY3/2025 record results + 4th consecutive record-profit-year framing + operating margin reaching 20.8% — sourced fundamental. 業績 ◎◎ / 株価 ◎ (gradual recovery, still well below the 2021 peak).

Cautious side: FY3/2026 figures came back inconsistent across search snippets (one source mentioned rev ¥44.297B / OP ¥125億 / RP ¥140億 +70.7% but these don't reconcile with FY3/2025 ¥48.904B base × +24.6% YoY claim — likely a unit-conversion or attribution error in the snippet, NOT a hallucination in the fixture; **FY3/2026 specific numbers flagged for human verification before downstream use**); 4-year stock still well below 2021 peak even after strong fundamentals; IT HR divestiture transition; SaaS-multiple sensitivity remains.

Sign summary: revenue rose +27.3% YoY; operating profit rose +83.3%; net profit rose +91.2%; record operating profit, operating margin 20.8% (high for SaaS); fourth consecutive record-profit year; shares began recovering off multi-year lows; shares rallied to ¥1,395 high (Sep 29); business strong, stock strong.

8056 BIPROGY ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: BIPROGY was renamed from 'Nihon Unisys Ltd.' in April 2022 — a major systems integrator providing system development/operations, IT solutions, and cloud infrastructure for finance (SMBC, regional banks), distribution/retail, manufacturing, and public sectors. How did business move? FY26 actuals: revenue 433.686 bn yen (+7.3% YoY), OP 42.604 bn yen (+9.1%), net 31.209 bn yen (+15.7%) — fourth consecutive year of record-high earnings. FY27 guidance: revenue 470 bn yen (+8.4%), OP 48.4 bn yen (+13.6%), net 32.2 bn yen (+3.2%). Dividend FY26 130 yen/year, FY27 guide 140 yen. Why did business move this way? First, sustained DX demand from financial institutions. Second, retail/distribution/manufacturing system-rebuild demand. Third, services leveraging proprietary cloud/data-center base grew as stock-type revenue. Why did the stock move this way? First, four consecutive years (five with FY27 guide) of record-high earnings — fits institutional quality-growth screens. Analyst average target 5,470 yen, current price 4,686 yen, Buy rating. Second, sustained large dividend hikes. Third, brand refresh from the Nihon Unisys rename and clarified independence from US Unisys.
Pattern (bucket-templated):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=profitability_inflection.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (peak — price ahead of fundamentals):* the stock hit an all-time high of ¥6,594 on Aug 13 2025, with the business still growing only ~7%. The fundamentals were fine but the re-rating had outrun them — a ◎◎ where price is arguably ahead of the business. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ → Q1, medium confidence (valuation stretched; this is the "watch for the flip" case, not a clean buy).
▸ Inbound / demographic tailwind 3 names
In plain terms: companies riding a long-duration consumer / population shift in Japan. Examples in this bucket: istyle (@cosme captures inbound tourist spending on Japanese cosmetics), Sharing Technology (rides the aging-society demand for life-services — Japan aging rate going from 29.3% in 2024 to 38.7% by 2070), Smaregi (rides the inbound tourism + cashless-payment shift through cloud POS for restaurants and retail). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names whose Round 2-documented 2025 driver was a structural demographic / inbound theme. Why this matters: these tailwinds are durable on a decade scale — demographics and consumer-behavior shifts do not reverse quickly. But there is geopolitical / FX risk (inbound flows depend on yen weakness and travel policy stability), and competitive intensity can squeeze margins as more players chase the same growth. What to watch: monthly tourist-arrival stats (JNTO data), JPY exchange rate (yen weakness is the inbound multiplier), and demographic data points like single-household ratios and elderly-spending shares.
3660 istyle Inc. internet_platform small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +6.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: istyle is a cosmetics platform built around "@cosme", Japan's largest cosmetics review site. Around the @cosme core it operates @cosme STORE (physical stores nationwide), @cosme TOKYO (Harajuku flagship), @cosme SHOPPING (e-commerce), brand-side marketing support (advertising, sampling, data sales), and Asia expansion including @cosme HONG KONG. Listed on TSE Prime. How did business move? FY6/2026 9-month cumulative: revenue ¥59.694 billion (+19.7% YoY), OP ¥2.884 billion (+23.0%). Q1: revenue ¥40.089 billion (+21.2%), OP ¥1.839 billion (+23.0%). Full-year guide left unchanged: revenue ¥83.0 billion (+20.7%), OP ¥3.8 billion (+20.1%), ordinary profit ¥3.8 billion (+14.8%), net income ¥2.65 billion (+13.9%). Dividend forecast at just ¥1 per share (payout ratio ~3.5%) — heavily reinvesting. Why did business move this way? First, Retail business strength — store sales rose +26.5% YoY thanks to renovations and inbound-tourist capture. Second, sustained Marketing Support growth — brand-side advertising, sampling, and data sales using @cosme review and browsing data carry high gross margins, producing operating-leverage where profit grows faster than revenue. Third, EC growth was standout via platform partnerships and successful sales events. Global (@cosme HONG KONG) is loss-making short-term due to launch costs. Why did the stock move this way? First, re-rated as a flagship inbound-recovery name — Korean, Chinese, and Southeast Asian tourist demand for Japanese cosmetics is recognized as a structural tailwind. Second, +20%-plus OP growth in both H1 and 9M consistently meets institutional Quality Growth criteria. Third, the high-margin Marketing Support business plus @cosme's platform power are entrenched long-term valuation drivers. UNVERIFIED: FY6/2026 final results, FY6/2027 guidance, and finalized annual dividend were not yet announced at search time.

3989 Sharingtechnology, Inc. internet_platform small ▸
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Sharing Technology (TSE Growth) operates a matching platform for "life's troubles" (plumbing, lost keys, pest control, junk removal, AC cleaning, funerals, etc.). It networks small/medium service providers nationwide and matches them to consumers. An in-house construction business is expanding. How did business move: FY9/2025 full-year (IFRS): revenue +14.4% to ¥8.58 billion, OP +15.9% to ¥2.07 billion, net profit ¥1.41 billion — 5 consecutive years of revenue growth. FY9/2026 guidance: revenue ¥9.8 billion (+14.2%), OP ¥2.35 billion (+13.3%), net profit ¥1.6 billion (+13.2%). Dividend ¥40 → ¥55 (+37.5%), 3 consecutive hikes, yield ~5.9%. Why did business move this way: First, the years-back peripheral-business divestiture and focus on the core matching business produced simultaneous growth in monthly inquiries and expansion of the provider network to about 6,400 affiliates. Second, the in-house construction segment grew sharply. Third, AI-driven matching-precision improvements raised ARPU. Why did the stock move this way: Stock up over +30% in 2025 and trading firmly around ¥1,198. First, the large FY9/2026 dividend hike to ¥55 attracted retail income-seekers. Second, Asset Value Investors Limited filed a large-shareholder report. Third, the 5-year compounding revenue track record plus ROE 27.4%, equity ratio 69.3%, and a reasonable PER of 17.4x provide a financial floor.

*2025 (record-year #2 — clean ◎):* stock ¥813 (Jan 10) – ¥1,262 (Aug 14); new multi-year high ¥1,262. **FY9/2025 actual (announced Nov 14, 2025): revenue ¥8,580M (+14.4% YoY), operating profit ¥2,074M (+15.9%)** — 2-year-highest profit, **2nd consecutive year of record results**, **annual dividend lifted ¥15** (capital return signal — alpha-menu U67 territory) per kabutan + IR disclosure. Sign summary: revenue rose +14.4% YoY; operating profit rose +15.9%; record results; profit margin expanding; dividend hike of ¥15; shares rallied to ¥1,262 multi-year high (Aug 14); business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ (Q1 Compounder, third year of the rally).

Cautious side: stock has rallied ~15x off the Jan 2022 ¥84 low — valuation has run substantially; the underlying business is genuinely growing but at +14-20% revenue, not at the rate the stock has appreciated; multi-year compounder story is real but stock multiple is now elevated; market for "home-trouble platform" services is steady but not infinite — TAM cap matters.

4431 Smaregi, Inc. SaaS small ▸
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +14.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview: Smaregi (4431) is a SaaS company centered on cloud POS for retail/restaurants. Its iPad-based intuitive POS comes bundled with sales analytics, inventory management, cashless payment integration, and labor management. Main customers are SMB restaurants and retailers. Listed on TSE Growth. How did business move? FY4/2026 Q3 YTD: revenue 9.62 billion yen (+22.1%), OP 2.23 billion yen (+22.8%). ARR crossed 10 billion yen, achieving the mid-term FY2026 target (9.46 billion yen) ahead of schedule. Full-year guidance: revenue 13.149 billion yen (+18.8%), OP 2.954 billion yen (+24.1%), net profit 2.016 billion yen (+22.6%). Year-end dividend guidance 20 yen (vs prior 15 yen — increase). Why did business move this way? First, POS-core cross-sell strategy is working — selling payment, time-card, analytics add-ons continuously lifts ARPU. Second, equipment-subscription plan refresh improved hardware revenue. Third, churn ~0.5% and ARR ~90% of revenue create a high-retention base. Inbound recovery is a tailwind. Why did the stock move this way? First, the ARR 10 billion yen milestone was hit ahead of schedule, signaling stepping out of mid-cap. Second, the dividend hike showed early shareholder-return discipline. Third, structural tailwind from invoice system and revised e-storage law sustains SMB-digitalization demand. Caveat: analyst target 3,800 yen sits well above current price.

▸ Sector-specific tailwind — content/IP/defense category re-rating 3 names
In plain terms: companies riding a category-level tailwind that the market is actively re-pricing. Examples in this bucket: Toho (rode a single mega-hit film 国宝 — 2025's No.1 box-office with ¥5.6 billion gross in 38 days), TV Tokyo (rode the global re-rating of anime IP via overseas streaming — Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video license fees rising), SKY Perfect JSAT (rode Japan's defense-spending ramp — Defense Ministry satellite constellation contract + SpaceX launch contracts doubling capacity). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names whose Round 2-documented 2025 driver was a specific sector / category tailwind. Why this matters: these tailwinds are typically multi-year — the defense budget is targeted at 2% of GDP through FY27, the anime overseas market keeps growing, and mega-hit films validate IP-driven re-rating. But the company needs to be BEST-IN-CLASS in its niche to keep capturing the tailwind; weaker players in the same category may underperform. What to watch: budget cycles (defense procurement schedules), streaming-platform license-fee trends (anime), and the next IP / property catalyst (film slate, sequel announcements, new IP acquisitions).
9412 SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation telecom mid ▸
Revenue: unknown Stock 2025 YoY: +119.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 …
Company overview SKY Perfect JSAT Holdings is Asia's largest satellite operator, with two pillars: Space (satellite comms/broadcasting, satellite imagery, defense-related space intelligence) and Media (SKY PerfecTV! pay-TV, Wowow). Earnings movement FY3/2026: revenue ¥127.58B (+3.1%), OP ¥35.27B (+28.3%), net ¥23.31B (+22.0%) — record-high. Dividend lifted from ¥27 to ¥42, FY3/2027 guided ¥48. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue ¥135B (+5.8%), OP ¥39B (+10.6%), NP ¥27B (+15.8%) — 4th consecutive record-high. Why earnings moved this way 1) Space business grew — revenue ¥69.8B (+7.9%) on Defense Ministry satellite-constellation contract and Space Intelligence growth. 2) Media business cost cuts drove segment profit +74.3%. 3) Space Intelligence new revenue widened margins. Why the stock moved this way 1) Re-rating on defense/national-security themes. 2) Record-high earnings and consecutive growth — Okasan raised target from ¥2,600 to ¥4,200. 3) Large dividend hike + 3-year ¥220B investment plan. Stock has risen +248.96% over the past year — very large re-rating.

Why the stock moved this way 1) Mid-year upward revision + record-high earnings pushed re-rating as 'content-rights holder.' 2) Long-lived anime IP generate decade-plus royalty streams. 3) Dividend hike + TSE PBR-improvement response. Higher content/licensing revenue mix makes earnings less swingy with ad cycles.

Company Overview Toho (TSE Prime, 9602) is Japan's largest film production, distribution and exhibition company. Distributes Godzilla, Your Name, Demon Slayer films. Four pillars: Film (distribution, exhibition, TOHO Cinemas), Theater, Real Estate (Tokyo metro buildings), IP & Anime (overseas distribution via GKIDS, Science SARU). Defensive-growth portfolio. Earnings Movement FY2/2026 actuals: operating revenue 360.66B yen (+15.2% YoY), OP 67.89B (+5.0%), ordinary 70.14B (+8.8%), net 51.77B (+19.4%) — record. Film segment: revenue 182.6B (+30.6%), OP 37.3B (+30.3%). Distribution revenue 139.9B yen — all-time high. Dividend flat at 85 yen annually. Initial guidance (345B revenue, 62B OP) was raised mid-year and beaten. Why the earnings moved this way 1) Two simultaneous megahits — Demon Slayer Infinity Castle Chapter 1 surpassed 40B yen, Kokuhō cleared 20B yen. 2) Mid-tier hits ran in parallel; cinema admissions ~49 million (+27.6%). 3) IP & Anime expanded to about 37.3B yen with overseas contribution from GKIDS, Science SARU. Why the stock moved this way 1) Record-profit / record-distribution-revenue print produced textbook re-rating. 2) Warner Bros. Japan distribution deal (2026) and TOHO-ONE customer data platform investment well received. Analyst stance: 4 strong-buy, 1 buy, 4 neutral, average target ~1,864 yen. 3) Jun 3, 2026 stock closed at 1,218 yen (-4.13%) — correction as market begins to price in FY27 hit-cycle reversion risk.

TOPIX-33 sector: 陸運業（鉄道＋運送＋宅配） (Land Transportation (Railways + Trucking + Parcel))
Total listed in sector 60
Qualifying as R+ 59
R- (currently listed) 1
Delisted / TOB (historical) 0
Math: 59 R+ + 1 R- = 60 currently listed. 0 delisted/TOB shown separately.
Show 1 excluded companies
  • 9024 Seibu Holdings (西武HD) — R- this year (large prior-year RE liquidation reaction-decline); will likely qualify next year on normalization

Universe coverage is exhaustive — all 60 currently-listed TOPIX-33 Land Transportation names classified. Note: 9036 Tobu Network qualifies under 営業利益○ only (revenue -2.8%, OP +44.8%); 9081 Kanagawa Chuo / 9143 SG Holdings / 9051 Senkon Logistics / 9033 Hiroshima Electric Railway / 9145 Being qualify under 売上○ only (revenue up, OP under pressure). The remaining 53 qualify under both filters. Source list cross-checked with Yahoo Finance 陸運業 sector page (60 names, 3 pages of 20).

For Land Transportation, revenue captures real business quality — fare/parcel volume growth. Strong FY3/2026: JR Big 3 + private railways + logistics all growing.

Why these filter options?

Why use Revenue ↑ (default)?
In plain terms: For Land Transportation (railways, trucking, parcel delivery), revenue is a clean signal of business quality. Railway revenue moves with passenger volume (fares × passengers); parcel revenue moves with parcel volume × ARPU; logistics revenue moves with cargo volume × pricing. None of these are distorted by commodity prices (unlike oil or marine shipping). So when revenue grows, the business is genuinely doing more business — more passengers, more parcels, more cargo.



For FY3/2026 specifically, Land Transportation was a STRONG year:

• JR Big 3 (East/West/Central) all set records — Osaka Expo + inbound recovery drove passenger volumes

• Major private railways (Hankyu-Hanshin, Nankai, Keihan, Nishitetsu, Kintetsu) all up — inbound + redevelopment + dividend hikes

• Trucking / logistics (NIKKON +74% stock 1-yr, NX, Senko, Sakai) growing on rate hikes + e-commerce

• Only a few names are R+×S-: Tokyu (Shibuya redevelopment delayed 7 years to 2034), Keisei (NP -31% on special losses), Yamato (NP -64% + later downward revision)



That's why the only filter offered for Land Transportation is 売上○ — it's the natural and sufficient quality signal for this revenue-driven sector.
▸ Inbound tourism beneficiaries 5 names
Railways, leisure operators, taxis, and buses whose core earnings and stock story is driven by the surge in foreign tourists — via unique route monopolies (KIX-Namba, Narita Skyliner, Hakone, Arashiyama, Mt. Fuji, Kyoto/Osaka Golden Route), the Osaka-Kansai Expo, and premium tourism experiences that foreign visitors cannot bypass.
9020 East Japan Railway (JR East) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +6.8% Op profit: ↑ +9.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: down ~-23% from Jan peak (¥4,211→¥3,243)
分類: 鉄道大手 / Major JR railway (East Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9020 + Nikkei 9020 + minkabu 9020 + JR East FY3/20…
Company overview: JR East (9020) is Japan's largest railway, headquartered in Tokyo-Shibuya, born from the 1987 JNR privatization. Centered on Tohoku/Kanto passenger transport, it spans railways (conventional + Shinkansen: Tohoku, Joetsu, Hokuriku, Yamagata, Akita), station retail (Ecute, atre), real estate/hotels (station buildings + Metropolitan Hotels), and Suica payments — a comprehensive urban-transport infrastructure company with 100+ consolidated subsidiaries. How did business move? FY3/2026: revenue ~¥3,084.6B (+6.8%), OP ~¥414.2B (+9.9%), with profit growth across all segments. OP margin moved modestly from ~13.1% in FY3/2025 (revenue ¥2,887.5B / OP ¥376.8B) to ~13.4%, remaining at a high level for a railway. Dividend was raised to ¥74 (+¥8) with FY3/2027 planned at ¥84. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1) Continued recovery in Shinkansen and conventional-line passenger volumes, including inbound demand, lifted transport revenue. 2) Full-year effect of the October-2023 fare revision (barrier-free surcharge etc.) added to per-line revenue. 3) Station-retail (Ecute, atre), distribution/services and real-estate rental income remained firm. Note: the Itochu Urban Development integration was announced Dec-2025, contract signed Apr-2026, effective Oct-1-2026 — it is a FUTURE deal, not a FY3/2026 earnings driver (UNVERIFIED as a FY26 contributor). Why did the stock move this way? In 2026 the stock made a YTD high of ¥4,211 on Jan-13 and a YTD low of ¥3,243 on Jun-4 — i.e., it has weakened year-to-date rather than trending up. Three background themes still attract money: 1) clear consecutive-dividend-increase policy (to ¥84 planned) drawing in long-horizon dividend money; 2) Itochu real-estate integration announcement re-rating JR East as a station/line-side developer; 3) structural inbound + decarbonization + Suica economic-zone themes. US-broker price targets range from ¥3,600 (May, neutral) to ¥4,500 (April, bullish), reflecting uncertainty around fare/traffic outlook.
9007 Odakyu Electric Railway ▸
Revenue: ↓ -0.94% Op profit: ↑ up Net profit: ↓ -28.08% Stock: down ~-6% from Apr high (¥1,758→¥1,650)
分類: 関東大手私鉄 / Major Kanto private railway (Odakyu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9007 + minkabu 9007…
Company overview: Odakyu Electric Railway (9007) is a major private rail headquartered in Tokyo-Shinjuku, founded 1923 (Taisho 12). Core routes: Odakyu Odawara Line (Shinjuku-Odawara) + Enoshima Line + Tama Line, with the iconic limited-express 'Romance Car.' Plus Shinjuku-front Odakyu Department Store, Machida / Hon-Atsugi station-front redevelopment, Odakyu OX + Lopia retail, Odakyu Hotel Century (Shinjuku) + Hyatt Regency Hakone, and monopolistic operation of Hakone-area tourism transport (Hakone Tozan Railway + ropeway + sightseeing boats) — a 'Hakone tourism + Shinjuku urban-development leader.' Put simply: 'a company that connects Shinjuku and Hakone and builds both towns.' How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals (May 13, 2026): Revenue ¥418.732B (−0.94%), recurring profit ¥54.0B (+7.0%, beat consensus), NP ¥37.368B (−28.08%) — NP fell on reaction-decline from prior-year one-off securities-sale gains; core transport solid (sources: Yahoo Finance, IRBANK). Dividend raised ¥40 → ¥50 (+¥10). FY27 guides Revenue ¥461.3B (+10.2%), NP +2.5%, but OP ¥47.9B (−11.3%, decline); dividend ¥60 planned. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Rapid Hakone inbound tourism recovery — Hakone is a 'must-visit suburban Tokyo' for foreign tourists; Chinese / Taiwanese / SE Asian visitors surged. Odakyu monopolistically operates Hakone tourism transport, so visitor growth directly converts to tourism revenue. 2. Romance Car limited-express demand growth — Shinjuku-Hakone Romance Car is popular with both tourists + business; limited-express fare revenue expanded. 3. April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect + stable line-side commercial revenue — Fare revisions reflected fully; Machida / Hon-Atsugi / Odawara / Fujisawa line-side commercial + rentals function as stable revenue sources. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Hakone inbound theme attention — Odakyu's structural ability to monopolize inbound Hakone demand via 'Hakone tourism transport + Hyatt Hakone' is recognized by institutions as a structural tourism revenue base. 2. Shareholder-return strengthening — Shift toward more earnings-linked dividend policy fuels expectations for dividend increases in growth periods, supporting the floor. 3. Nikkei 225 supply-demand stability + Shinjuku-front redevelopment long-term growth — Nikkei 225 ETF-flow effects stabilize supply-demand; plus Shinjuku-front Odakyu Department Store flagship + Nishi-Shinjuku redevelopment is rated as a long-term growth base. But 12-quarter declining net margin / ROE is a capital-efficiency concern; full PER expansion may take time.
9049 Keifuku Electric Railroad (京福電気鉄道) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +2.9% Op profit: ↑ +5.1% Net profit: ↑ +5.6% Stock: -13.5% from Mar high (¥8,300→¥7,180)
分類: 京都観光路面電車 / Kyoto tourism tram (Keifuku / Randen)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9049 + Keifuku IR + Keihan HD FY3/2026 disclosure…
Company overview: Keifuku Electric Railroad (9049) is a mid-tier private railway founded 1942, headquartered in Kyoto. Core: Randen (Arashiyama Main Line + Kitano Line, the tram in Kyoto's Ukyo Ward). Plus Kyoto-city commercial buildings, rentals, condos real estate, Arashiyama sightseeing bus + guides, bus. 'Keifuku' means 'Kyoto + Fukui' — formerly operated railways in Fukui, now concentrated on Kyoto. Randen is the access for Arashiyama (Togetsukyo Bridge, Tenryuji Temple, Bamboo Forest Path) — a Keihan Electric Railway Holdings (9045) consolidated subsidiary. Put simply: 'a Keihan HD group regional railway connecting Arashiyama and Kyoto town by tram.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Operating revenue ¥11.42B (+3.6%), OP ¥2.279B (+7.4%). Full-year guidance ¥14.459B (+2.97%) — real-estate revenue contribution exceeds rail — a Keifuku-specific structural feature. Arashiyama Kyoto inbound + strong real estate drive earnings. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Kyoto inbound structural expansion — Kyoto is a must-visit for foreign tourists; Arashiyama ranks with Kiyomizu-dera and Fushimi-Inari among Kyoto's top three destinations. Randen is the near-monopoly access for Arashiyama — visitor growth flows directly to transit revenue. 2. Kyoto real-estate hidden value + stable rent — Kyoto land prices trending up; commercial / rental hidden value expanding. Rent income stable. 3. Arashiyama tourism-related strength + rail fare-revision effect — Sightseeing bus + guide + merchandise grew on visitor growth; tourism-related ancillary revenue lifted earnings. Plus Randen fare revision improved economics. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Stable growth + Kyoto inbound theme + real-estate hidden value — As a Keihan HD consolidated subsidiary, independent volatility is limited, but earnings-quality improvement is recognized by institutional investors. 2. Randen Arashiyama monopoly access + long-term Kyoto World Heritage tourism growth — Randen is the main rail to Arashiyama — near-monopoly access for foreign tourists. Kyoto concentrates World Heritage + historic cultural assets — one of the strongest inbound long-term growth bases. 3. Keihan HD group synergies + long-term Kyoto real-estate appreciation — Cooperation with Keihan HD enables Kyoto-Osaka expansion + real-estate development know-how + fare-revision timing coordination; Kyoto real-estate hidden value also expands mid-long-term.
9085 Hokkaido Chuo Bus (北海道中央バス) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +6.7% Op profit: ↑ +17.1% Net profit: ↑ +22.9% Stock: up (uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 北海道大手バス / Major Hokkaido bus operator (Hokkaido Chuo Bus)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9085 + Hokkaido Chuo Bus IR + Hokkaido Chuo Bus Q3…
Company overview: Hokkaido Chuo Bus (9085) is Hokkaido largest bus operator with its registered head office in Otaru (administrative functions split between Otaru and Sapporo). Operates local routes + highway buses (Sapporo-Asahikawa, Sapporo-Hakodate, Sapporo-Kushiro) + charter sightseeing buses + airport limousine buses (New Chitose) centered on the Doo region (Sapporo, Otaru, Chitose, Tomakomai). Beyond bus: Hokkaido construction (Chuo Bus Construction Group) + real estate (line-side commercial) + retail (Chuo Bus Shoji) — broad contribution to Hokkaido economy. Put simply: regional infrastructure serving Hokkaido Doo region and New Chitose airport access. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥38.384B (+6.7%), OP ¥2.651B (+17.1%), recurring ¥2.987B (+13.2%), NP ¥2.781B (+22.9%) — meaningful growth across the board. Q3 YTD recurring profit grew 2.6x YoY — a strong result reflecting Hokkaido economic vitality. However, FY3/2027 company guidance is for sharp declines: Revenue ¥37.7B (-1.8%), OP ¥1.6B (-39.6%), recurring ¥1.9B (-36.4%), NP ¥1.2B (-56.8%) — driven by construction-segment normalization plus rising raw-material and labor costs as disclosed by management. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the acceleration. 1. Hokkaido inbound surge — Foreign visitor numbers at record highs; Hokkaido captures winter ski + summer cooling + gourmet themes from China, Korea, Taiwan, SE Asia, Australia. Niseko, Rusutsu, Tomamu ski resorts drove charter + airport-limousine revenue dramatically. 2. New Chitose airport limousine demand + bus fare-revision effect — More international routes + domestic passenger recovery lifted ridership. Plus MLIT-approved fare increases improved revenue. 3. Construction segment strength + cost discipline — Hokkaido infrastructure + hotel / commercial construction lifted Chuo Bus Construction Group. However, management flags this construction surge as a reaction-driven decline risk for next year. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds an uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Recurring profit Q3 cumulative 2.6x + full-year growth + earnings momentum — Sapporo-Securities-Exchange-only listing limits liquidity, but Hokkaido-inbound-themed institutional money is buying. However, FY27 sharp-decline guidance creates uncertainty about momentum continuation. 2. Long-term Hokkaido inbound growth + New Chitose Airport strategic value — China / Korea / Taiwan / SE Asia tourism to Hokkaido is set to expand mid-long-term, with winter-ski + summer-cooling two-season structure as a strength. New Chitose, Hokkaido main international airport, keeps expanding routes. 3. Doo regional share + construction tied to Hokkaido economy + diversified portfolio — Strong Doo local-bus share, construction-segment opportunity from Hokkaido Shinkansen extension + redevelopment + hotels, plus bus + construction + real estate + retail mix diversifies cyclical risk. PBR around 0.6x also provides downside support.
9142 JR Kyushu (九州旅客鉄道) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +10.1% Op profit: ↑ +25.5% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: -18% from Feb peak (¥4,154→~¥3,390)
分類: 鉄道大手 / Major JR railway (Kyushu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9142 + JR Kyushu IR + kabuyoho 9142 + Nishi-Kyushu…
Company overview: JR Kyushu (9142) is the Kyushu-area railway major born from the 1987 JNR privatization, headquartered in Fukuoka. Core: railway (conventional lines + Kyushu Shinkansen + Nishi-Kyushu Shinkansen = Nagasaki route opened September 23, 2022). Plus real estate / construction (station-building Amu Plaza operations) + retail (JR Hakata City, First Cabin) + hospitality (JR Kyushu Hotels) + agriculture / food (JR Kyushu Farm, JR Kyushu Food Service) — a regional integrated company centered on rail. Initially had more loss-making lines than the JR-Honshu-three, but rebuilt its earnings base via station-building development and regional development. Put simply: a comprehensive infrastructure company supporting Kyushu transit and town-building. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥500.4B (+10.1%), OP ¥74.0B (+25.5%), equity ratio 40.4% (improved) — strong revenue + profit beat, with transportation services driving the result. FY3/2027 guidance: continued revenue + operating-profit growth and annual dividend hike to 115 yen; however recurring profit is forecast to decline YoY. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the sharp growth. 1. Rapid Kyushu inbound tourism recovery + fare revision — Fukuoka Airport expanded international routes, more visitors from China / Korea / Taiwan to Nagasaki / Kumamoto / Kagoshima, plus rail fare revisions lifted both transit revenue and station-building retail sales. 2. Nishi-Kyushu Shinkansen (Nagasaki route, opened September 23, 2022) effects now stable — Over 3 years after opening, development around Takeo-Onsen, Ureshino-Onsen, Shin-Omura, Isahaya, and Nagasaki has progressed. The Shinkansen lifted regional economies and shows in both JR Kyushu transit revenue and real-estate income. 3. Amu Plaza + real-estate strength — Operations at Hakata, Kokura, Nagasaki, Kagoshima-Chuo, Miyazaki, Kumamoto, Oita, Saga grew via tenant-mix refresh + inbound customer capture; real-estate also supported overall results. Why did the stock move this way? Stock hit a YTD high of ¥4,154 on February 9, 2026, then sold off to around ¥3,390 by early June 2026 — a correction phase, not a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Strong earnings + dividend hike were positives, but profit-taking and the FY3/2027 recurring-profit-decline guidance capped the upside — The FY3/2026 print and dividend hike were favorable, but recurring-profit guidance for the next year + profit-taking at the highs pressured the stock. 2. Structural inbound advantage from Asia proximity + real-estate long-term growth runway — Kyushu is geographically close to China, Korea, Taiwan, Hong Kong; Asian tourism demand should keep expanding. Fukuoka Tenjin Big Bang redevelopment + Hakata Station high-rise plans + Nagasaki-station-front redevelopment concentrate JR Kyushu real estate at the heart of redevelopment zones. 3. Kyushu Shinkansen network completion expectations + ESG fit — Expectations for the integrated-Shinkansen Takeo-Onsen-Hakata segment and electric-rail decarbonization-positive ESG appeal support long-term growth (overseas rail business scale and expected impact: UNVERIFIED from sourced disclosures).
▸ Logistics 2024-Problem rate-hike winners 7 names
Freight truckers, LTL carriers, parcel operators, and 3PL providers whose earnings and stock recovered sharply because the 2024 driver-hour regulations forced industrywide rate increases; EC-market growth and manufacturer 3PL-outsourcing also provide structural volume tailwinds.
9069 SENKO Group Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.3% Op profit: ↑ +5.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: -6% YoY (~¥1,992→¥1,876)
分類: 総合物流大手 / Major comprehensive logistics (SENKO)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9069 + maonline 9069 + daily-cargo 9069…
Company overview: SENKO Group Holdings (9069) is a major comprehensive logistics company founded 1916; listed holding-company HQ is Tokyo Koto-ku (operating subsidiary Senko Co. is in Osaka). Core: domestic cargo transport (truck / rail / ship), 3PL, warehousing. Plus logistics-related trading, life support, machinery / industrial-equipment sales, and SE Asia / China / North America / Europe overseas logistics networks. Signature: ambidextrous management + aggressive M&A. Put simply: a ¥1 trillion candidate diversifying around logistics and growing via M&A. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥899.62B (+5.3%), OP ¥36.996B (+5.9%), recurring +4.4% — growth on all lines. FY27 guide: Revenue ¥1.02 trillion (+13.4%), OP ¥43B (+16.2%) — hitting the ¥1 trillion company milestone. FY26 dividend raised to ¥50 (+¥4). Why did business move this way? Three reasons for stable expansion. 1. M&A-driven scale expansion — Key 2025-2026 M&A include (1) PDS International (India, April 2025, customs / domestic transport) and (2) Maruun (TOB closed March 12, 2026; 80% acquired; cargo transport); acquired earnings consolidate, sustaining scale expansion. 2. 2024-Problem rate revisions + 3PL contract expansion — Trucking-hour regulation-driven rate revisions permeated, improving economics; new 3PL contract wins expanded the stable base. 3. Overseas + life-support businesses materializing — Overseas logistics network expansion + aging-society life-support services are starting to generate stable revenue. Why did the stock move this way? Near-term direction is NOT clearly an uptrend: ~¥1,992 in Oct 2025 to ~¥1,876 in April 2026, and a major broker downgraded rating to neutral in Sept 2025 — direction tagged UNVERIFIED. However, the narrative drivers cited by the market are: 1. The ¥1 trillion company growth story — FY27 revenue target is the explicit mid-term plan goal, supportive long-term. 2. M&A continuity — 5-year M&A track record + Maruun TOB positions Senko as an M&A-execution growth company. 3. Structural industry growth + return strengthening — 2024-Problem rate hikes, EC logistics expansion, manufacturer 3PL outsourcing, and step-up dividend support long-horizon inclusion.
9090 AZ-COM Maruwa Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ +10.6% Op profit: ↑ +8.3% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: -26% YoY (¥1,023 Jan-2026 high → ¥762 low, downtrend)
分類: EC・小売3PL / EC & retail 3PL logistics (AZ-COM Maruwa)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9090 + AZ-COM Maruwa IR…
Company overview: AZ-COM Maruwa Holdings (formerly Maruwa Unyu Kikan, 9090) is a logistics company headquartered in Yoshikawa, Saitama, founded 1973. Core: retail 3PL (distribution-center operations + product delivery for Aeon, Seven & i, FamilyMart, Lawson), cold-chain food logistics (frozen / refrigerated food delivery for convenience stores), last-mile (Momotaro Bin parcel service using individual-contractor model, last-mile delivery for Amazon / Rakuten EC), and SE Asia + China international logistics. Founder Masaru Wasami (b. 1945, Yoshikawa) distinctive model (individual-contractor use + community-rooted operations). Put simply: a retail + EC logistics specialist supporting the backside of convenience stores and EC. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥230.531B (+10.6%), OP ¥11.864B (+8.3%) — growth on both. FY27 OP guidance ¥14.0B (analyst consensus ¥14.05B); company is in a front-loaded investment growth phase with structural reform + IT / DX + regulation-response investment accelerated. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. New logistics-center openings + customer-base expansion — New large-format centers in Kanto / Chubu / Kansai expanded capacity + volume; Aeon / Seven & i / convenience-store 3PL contracts expanded. 2. Momotaro Bin EC last-mile demand absorption — Individual-contractor Momotaro Bin parcel service captured Amazon / Rakuten EC last-mile demand; new areas + active vehicles expanded. 3. 2024-Problem rate revisions + front-loaded investment for mid-long-term base — Trucking + retail 3PL rate revisions improved economics; structural reform + IT / DX + regulation-response investment front-loaded. Why did the stock move this way? Despite revenue + profit growth, the stock is actually in a DOWNTREND — 2026 YTD low ¥762, YTD high ¥1,023 (Jan 7, 2026). Three reasons. 1. Market wariness of front-loaded investment = short-term margin pressure — Front-loaded structural reform + IT / DX + regulation investment is viewed as compressing near-term OP margin; analyst consensus was just revised down 0.4% to ¥14.05B. 2. EC-logistics theme profit-taking + retail-3PL margin pressure — Profit-taking after past EC-theme rallies plus retail-customer pricing / cost pressure raise questions on the Momotaro Bin cost structure. 3. Valuation correction — Pullback from elevated PER multiples, plus the time lag before structural-reform benefits flow to earnings, has long-horizon investors stepping back. Founder Wasami decision speed remains a differentiator, but stock has reacted negatively despite top- and bottom-line growth.
9147 NIPPON EXPRESS Holdings (NX HD) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +1.1% (Q1 FY12/2026) Op profit: ↑ +32.3% (Q1 FY12/2026) Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up strongly on 1-year view (~¥5,259, % unverified)
分類: 総合物流大手 / Major comprehensive logistics (NX / Nippon Express)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9147 + Nikkei 9147 + NX IR + cargo-news 9147…
Company overview: NIPPON EXPRESS Holdings (Nittsu / NX, 9147) is one of Japan's largest comprehensive logistics companies, headquartered in Tokyo-Chiyoda, founded 1937. Integrates domestic logistics (trucking, rail freight, moving) + international logistics (ocean / air forwarding, global network) + heavy-equipment construction (plant + large-machinery specialty transport) + cash/valuables transport — a Japan + global logistics leader. Overseas revenue ratio is broadly in the low-30s+ range as globalization advances (exact ratio UNVERIFIED). How did business move? FY12/2026 Q1: revenue ¥652.3B (+1.1%), OP ¥14.9B (+32.3%). The headline NP +287% figure could not be independently verified (UNVERIFIED). FY12/2026 full-year guidance: revenue ¥2.7T (+4.9%), OP ¥100B (+94.2%), NP ¥60B. Note: on May 13 2026 NX downward-revised H1 net profit from ¥25B to ¥24B (-4%) while keeping full-year NP at ¥60B — execution risk against the full-year plan has been flagged. Why did business move this way? Three drivers. 1) Air / sea cargo market recovery + rate revisions — international logistics normalized from COVID disruption; rate normalization progressed. Domestic trucking + rail-freight rate revisions tied to the 2024 Problem also lifted domestic margins. 2) Overseas-expansion strategy — multi-year M&A effects materialized, expanding overseas revenue (exact yen / ratio UNVERIFIED). Mid-tier logistics M&A continues across Europe / North America / Asia. 3) Heavy-equipment construction — semiconductor-equipment + large-machinery specialty transport (TSMC Kumamoto etc.) lifted the high-margin segment. Why did the stock move this way? As of May 29 2026 the stock was around ¥5,259 — strong on a 1-year view — but after the May 13 H1 downward revision, full-year-plan execution risk has come into focus. Three themes still attract long-term money: 1) Overseas revenue ¥1.2T (2028) + group revenue ¥3T targets as a global-logistics internationalization story; 2) TSMC Kumamoto + semiconductor logistics theme positioning NX as a major beneficiary; 3) TSE PBR-improvement response via payout-ratio lifts + buybacks + M&A-driven growth. NB: the analyst-consensus average target stood at ¥3,620 as of Nov-2025 — well below the current share price; targets appear stale relative to spot, implying either upcoming upward target revisions or downside risk in the share price.
2384 SBS Holdings (SBSホールディングス) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +10.3% (9M) Op profit: ↑ +31.1% (9M) Net profit: ↑ +27.4% (9M) Stock: firm ~¥3,460 (Oct 2025 reference, % unverified)
分類: 3PL・物流不動産 / 3PL & logistics real estate (SBS Holdings)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 2384 + kabutan 2384 + nikkei 2384 + SBS HD IR Q1…
Company overview: SBS Holdings (2384) is built around 3PL (third-party logistics — taking over a customer's entire logistics operation) as its core business. Plus logistics-real-estate (developing, holding, and renting logistics facilities) and international logistics (ocean / air freight forwarding) — a 'comprehensive logistics platform' rather than a traditional trucker. Founder Masahiko Kamada grew it from a mid-tier to a major player in one generation through M&A and in-house facility development, giving SBS a business structure that transcends traditional shipping. Put simply: 'a logistics platform that grows rapidly via 3PL + logistics REIT + M&A.' How did business move? SBS has a December fiscal year. FY12/2025 Q1 (Jan-Mar): Revenue ¥112.821B (-0.9%), OP ¥3.202B (-60.5% YoY) — sharp drop reflecting prior-year Q1's concentrated logistics-real-estate liquidation gains base (logistics segment revenue still +8.7%). 9M cumulative (Jan-Sep): Revenue ¥364.36B (+10.3%), OP ¥17.338B (+31.1%), recurring ¥17.382B (+27.9%), NP ¥9.625B (+27.4%) — broad double-digit growth on core (sources: kabutan k202511120021, Nikkei). FY12/2025 full-year guidance: Revenue ¥485.0B (+8.2%), OP ¥20.5B (+15.8%), recurring ¥20.1B (+8.9%), NP ¥11.2B (+16.4%) — maintained (sources: matsui, kabutan). Stock ~¥3,460 (Oct 24, 2025). Why did business move this way? Three reasons behind the Q1 decline vs. 9M growth divergence. 1. Reaction decline from logistics-real-estate liquidation gains (the Q1 driver) — Prior-year (FY12/2024) Q1 had concentrated logistics-facility sales to J-REITs / funds; current Q1 has no equivalent, so OP dropped sharply. The logistics-real-estate business creates large quarter-to-quarter swings tied to sale timing. 2. Structural 3PL growth (supports 9M cumulative) — Domestic labor shortage + EC logistics expansion in parallel push shippers to outsource the entire logistics function to 3PLs. SBS has nationwide large-format facilities to capture this wave. 3. Cost-structure improvement + operating leverage — Logistics DX (automated warehouses, WMS, route optimization) investment has matured; fixed-cost ratios are falling while revenue grows — the operating-leverage effect amplifies profit on the underlying business. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds firm as the market prices the Q1 decline as a one-off and values 9M cumulative core-business expansion. Three main reasons. 1. Familiarity with quarterly volatility + 9M cumulative core-business strength — Institutional investors understand logistics-real-estate sale-timing concentration as a known structural feature; they value the 9M cumulative +31% core-business expansion. Focus is on 'true earnings power' excluding sale timing. 2. Re-rating of logistics-real-estate strategic value — Owning and operating logistics facilities is increasingly seen as a stable income source; institutions value SBS as a hybrid 'logistics REIT + shipping company' structure. 3. M&A strategy continuity + structural EC-logistics market expansion — SBS has a long track record of absorbing logistics companies; market trusts founder Kamada's growth strategy. Plus SBS handles logistics for Amazon, Rakuten, ZOZO and other major EC platforms — directly capturing EC's ~10% annual growth attracts long-horizon investor support.
9025 Konoike Transport (鴻池運輸) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.1% Op profit: ↑ +6.5% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up gently (~¥2,751 May 2026, % unverified)
分類: 重工業・空港物流 / Heavy-industry & airport ground handling (Konoike)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9025 + Konoike Transport IR + kabutan 9025 + Konoi…
Company overview: Konoike Transport (9025) is a major comprehensive logistics company headquartered in Osaka, founded 1880 by Konoike Chujiro in Osaka-Dempo, 145+ years of history. Core businesses: steel-mill on-site logistics (raw material / product transport within steel production, scrap handling), airport ground handling (KIX, Narita), medical / pharma logistics (temperature-controlled warehouses; GDP-compliance UNVERIFIED-detail), food-plant logistics, chemical-plant on-site logistics, inspection / quality services, helicopter cargo transport — a high-skill, site-embedded service mix not easily replicated. Put simply: a Meiji-era specialty-logistics craftsman group operating inside steel mills, airports, and pharma warehouses. How did business move? FY3/2026 ACTUALS: Revenue ¥355.55B (+3.1%), OP ¥22.785B (+6.5%) — slightly above original guidance (¥355B / ¥22.5B). Dividend lifted ¥96 to ¥110 (+¥14) — meaningful shareholder-return upgrade. However the company guides FY3/2027 to a profit decline driven by raw-material price increases and labor-cost rises — near-term momentum decelerates. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for steady growth. 1. Long-term steel-mill contracts — On-site logistics for Nippon Steel, JFE Steel, Kobelco span 10-20-year terms; steel-output swings have some impact, but base revenue is stable. 2. Airport ground-handling recovery + on-site rate revisions through 2024 Problem — COVID-crushed flights and freight volumes recovered; KIX / Narita / Chubu Centrair ground-handling revenue is up. Foreign-visitor growth lifts. Driver-hour-limit-driven rate revisions also progressed in on-site contracts. 3. Structural pharma logistics growth — Aging society + pharma market expansion drives long-term demand; temperature-controlled warehouses capture high-margin pharma logistics. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend (~¥2,751 in May 2026). Three main reasons. 1. Big dividend hike (¥96 to ¥110, +¥14) strengthens returns — Attracts dividend-mandate long-horizon demand. 2. Steel-mill on-site logistics monopoly + Meiji-era brand strength — 145-year barrier is valued by institutions as unique high-skill logistics positioning. 3. Pharma logistics + airport ground handling + decarbonization theme long-term runway — Offsetting these, the FY27 profit-decline guidance (raw materials / labor) is a near-term cap on upside that investors are weighing.
9057 Enshu Truck (遠州トラック) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +7.3% Op profit: ↑ +4.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: modest / range-bound (% unverified)
分類: 静岡3PL物流 / Shizuoka 3PL logistics (Enshu Truck)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9057 + Enshu Truck IR + Enshu Truck Q3 disclosure…
Company overview: Enshu Truck (9057) is a mid-tier 3PL logistics company headquartered in Fukuroi, Shizuoka, founded 1947. Operates in Shizuoka / Aichi / Kanagawa, providing 3PL (distribution-center operations) + co-distribution + warehouse + SCM (supply chain management) + trucking + moving — a 'Chubu-region 3PL pure-play mid-tier.' Core: 3PL contracts for Shizuoka manufacturers (Yamaha, Suzuki, Hamamatsu Photonics, Yamaha Motor) + food / consumer-goods makers. Put simply: 'a regional mid-tier supporting Shizuoka's Yamaha and Suzuki factories with 3PL.' How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year guidance: Revenue ¥52.2B (+7.3%, ¥522億), OP ¥3.4B (+4.9%, ¥34億) — revenue + profit growth. But H1 revenue hit record high while OP / recurring / NP fell for 2nd consecutive period — cost pressure surfaced. Full-year guidance still shows profit growth; H2 recovery expected. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'record revenue / profit decline' pattern. 1. Delayed fuel-surcharge pass-through + rising driver wages — Diesel-price persistence outpaced fare pass-through; plus Chubu driver shortage deepened — labor-cost increases pressed profit. 2. New logistics-facility launch initial costs — New facilities in Hamamatsu, Fuji, Shizuoka incurred initial costs temporarily. Full-scale operations should drive mid-long-term revenue. 3. 3PL competitive intensification — Major 3PLs compress new-contract pricing. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades modestly for a mid-cap logistics name. Three main reasons. 1. Record revenue but profit weakness priced in — Modest dividend yield; long-horizon holders are core. Awaits earnings recovery (new-facility effects materializing). 2. Shizuoka manufacturing-cluster strength + 3PL industry long-term growth — Yamaha, Suzuki, Hamamatsu Photonics manufacturing concentration generates stable logistics demand; Chubu logistics-hub status sustained mid-long-term. Domestic 3PL market ~5-7% annual growth also captured. 3. New facilities' mid-long-term growth effect + community-rooted sales base — New facilities are near-term burdens but full-scale operations expand capacity and become a mid-long-term revenue base. High Shizuoka recognition / trust also helps new-customer acquisition + recruiting.
9074 Japan Oil Transportation (日本石油輸送) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.9% Op profit: ↑ +20.2% Net profit: ↑ +19.4% Stock: direction unverified (~¥4,635 Dec 2025)
分類: 危険物・石油輸送 / Hazmat & petroleum transport (Japan Oil Transportation)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9074 + Japan Oil Transport IR + Japan Oil Transpor…
Company overview: Japan Oil Transportation (9074) is a Tokyo-headquartered specialty logistics company jointly owned by ENEOS and JR Freight, focused on hazardous-material transport (petroleum, liquefied gas, chemicals). Founded 1947. Core: petroleum tanker trucks + high-pressure gas (LPG, liquid nitrogen, liquid oxygen) + chemicals + rail freight (tank cars) + container logistics. High-level safety / technical know-how creates a barrier. Put simply: an ENEOS / JR-Freight-affiliated specialty hazmat-logistics player. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥38.537B (+3.9%), OP ¥1.868B (+20.2%), NP ¥1.507B (+19.4%). Strong petroleum + narrowing high-pressure-gas losses delivered growth. The company has NOT disclosed FY3/2027 guidance, citing Middle East geopolitical uncertainty. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Petroleum-transport rate revisions — 2024-Problem driver-hour limits drove rate hikes through. 2. High-pressure gas loss narrowing + container expansion — Loss-making gas segment improved; JR Freight cooperation lifted container volumes. 3. Hazmat barrier — Strict safety / technical requirements bar new entrants; top industry share holds. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trajectory is UNVERIFIED at multi-year level (around ¥4,635 in late 2025). Potential drivers: 1. Core-business profit improvement + high-pressure-gas turn — Specialty-logistics niche attracts positioning. 2. Structural semiconductor liquefied-gas demand growth — Heavy nitrogen / oxygen demand from TSMC Kumamoto and Rapidus Hokkaido ramps; modal-shift policy via JR Freight is supportive. 3. Paradoxical EV-shift benefit + ENEOS-group base — Even as gasoline demand falls, gas-station consolidation sustains large-format logistics; ENEOS-group affiliation gives a stable customer base — though Middle East dynamics keep the outlook uncertain.
▸ Station / urban redevelopment story 3 names
Private railways whose dominant investor thesis is a major station-area or urban real-estate redevelopment — either as a long-term re-rating catalyst (Shinjuku Southwest, Shinagawa/Takanawa, Umekita, Fukuoka Tenjin) or as a near-term overhang when the project is delayed or shelved (Shibuya 7-year delay, Nagoya station effective shelving).
9003 Sotetsu Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.3% Op profit: ↑ +2.7% Net profit: ↑ +10.9% Stock: -23% from Apr peak (¥3,167→¥2,430)
分類: 関東私鉄 / Kanto private railway (Sotetsu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9003 + Sotetsu IR…
Company overview: Sotetsu Holdings (formerly Sagami Railway, 9003) is a major Kanto private rail + comprehensive urban-development company headquartered in Yokohama, founded 1917. Core: Sotetsu Main Line (Yokohama-Ebina) + Izumino Line + the March-2023 Sotetsu Shin-Yokohama Line, providing through-service with JR Saikyo + Tokyu Toyoko/Meguro for direct access to Shibuya/Shinjuku/Ikebukuro. Plus line-side residential development, Yokohama Station-front redevelopment, Sotetsu Hotels, Sotetsu Rosen retail, bus and construction. How did business move? FY3/2026: revenue ¥307.572B (+5.3%), OP ¥38.833B (+2.7%), NP ¥24.848B (+10.9%). Equity ratio improved to 25.0%; dividend raised ¥65→¥70 (+¥5), with FY3/2027 maintained at ¥70. FY3/2027 OP guidance is ¥37.0B (-4.7%) due to Shin-Yokohama-Line-related capex + depreciation — an investment-front-loaded decline phase. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1) Strong real-estate subdivision business — solid condo sales in Yokohama/Kanagawa lifted segment revenue. 2) Hotels captured inbound demand at Yokohama-area Sotetsu Hotels (specific ARPU/occupancy figures UNVERIFIED). 3) Sotetsu Shin-Yokohama Line full-year effect + April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect lifted transport revenue. FY3/2027's projected OP decline is positioned as short-term pressure from new-line capex during mid-/long-term growth-base building. Why did the stock move this way? In 2026 the stock made a YTD high of ¥3,167 (Apr 9) and a YTD low of ¥2,430 (May 28) — it has dropped over 20% from the April peak rather than tracking a straight-line uptrend, reflecting volatility around earnings progress and rates. Mid-/long-term themes still attract money: 1) structural appreciation expectations along the Shin-Yokohama Line corridor; 2) step-up dividend + dividend-maintenance policy through FY3/2027's earnings dip; 3) Yokohama Station-front + Minato Mirai 21 redevelopment as a long-duration real-estate growth option.
9005 Tokyu Corporation ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.0% Op profit: → -0.3% Net profit: ↑ +9.3% Stock: -21% from Mar peak (¥2,011→¥1,580)
分類: 関東大手私鉄 / Major Kanto private railway (Tokyu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Tokyu FY3/2026 IR doc + note denchan article + LIMO 9005 +…
Company overview: Tokyu (9005) is a major Kanto private railway + comprehensive urban-development company headquartered in Tokyo-Shibuya, founded 1922. Core: Tokyu Toyoko / Den-en-toshi / Oimachi lines (largest passenger count among private railways) + central role in Shibuya redevelopment (Shibuya Scramble Square opened 2019) + line-side development of Futako-Tamagawa / Jiyugaoka / Musashi-Kosugi + Tokyu Store / Tokyu Department Store + Tokyu Hotels / The Capitol Hotel Tokyu + overseas real estate and hotels — a line-side + urban-development leader. Put simply: a private rail that builds Shibuya town and supports daily life in southwest Tokyo. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥1.08617 trillion (+3.0%), OP ¥103.193B (-0.3%), NP ¥87.071B (+9.3%) — revenue slightly up, OP roughly flat, NP up. Earnings momentum slowed. Stock fell roughly -20% from a March 2026 high near ¥2,011 to a late-May low near ¥1,580 — classic R+×S- pattern. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Real-estate reaction-decline — Prior year (FY3/2025) booked concentrated large real-estate sales; FY3/2026 was the reaction-decline (segment-level detail UNVERIFIED). Stalled consolidated OP. 2. Strong transport + hotels / resorts — Inbound recovery kept transport + hotels strong, but could not fully offset the real-estate reaction. 3. Structural commuter-pass revenue decline — Remote-work entrenchment reducing Tokyu-line commuter-pass revenue long-term (UNVERIFIED on hard numbers); reduced commuting to Shibuya / Otemachi office areas signals long-term shrinkage of Tokyu stable revenue source. Why did the stock move this way? Stock fell ~-20%; three main reasons. 1. Shibuya redevelopment completion delayed 7 years (FY2027 to FY2034) — Officially disclosed May 2026, the 7-year delay of Tokyu largest growth story rattled long-term expectations — the biggest stock-fall driver. 2. Structural commuter-pass revenue decline concern — Long-term commuter-pass decline implies shrinkage of Tokyu stable revenue source, compressing market PER. 3. EPS-slowdown disappointment from RE reaction-decline — Despite flat OP / +9.3% NP, stripping out prior-year large RE sales, real growth momentum slowed — triggering momentum-investor selling. But a major US securities firm maintained Buy and raised target to ¥2,510 (May 12, 2026); long-horizon institutions still credit line-side real-estate hidden value + Shibuya redevelopment long-term runway.
9048 Meitetsu (Nagoya Railroad) ▸
