分類: 中部大手私鉄 / Major Chubu private railway (Meitetsu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Meitetsu IR + kabutan 9048 + minkabu 9048…
Company overview: Meitetsu (Nagoya Railroad, 9048) is a major Chubu-region private rail + comprehensive company headquartered in Nagoya, founded 1894. Core: Meitetsu Main Line (Gifu-Nagoya-Toyohashi) + Inuyama Line / Tokoname Line (Chubu International Airport access) + limited-express Mu Sky. Plus central role in Nagoya Station-front redevelopment, Meitetsu Department Store flagship, Laguna Ten Bosch (Gamagori comprehensive resort), Meitetsu Inn + Meitetsu Grand Hotel, Meitetsu Transportation (international logistics), Australia / SE Asia overseas business. Put simply: a private rail connecting Nagoya town and Chubu International Airport, anchoring Nagoya-front redevelopment. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥691.583B marginal, OP ¥36.185B (-14.0%) down, NP ¥22.954B (-39.2% sharp decline). FY27 guides significant recovery: Revenue ¥734B (+6.1%), OP ¥45B (+24.4%), NP ¥39B (+69.9%). Dividend: FY3/2025 actual ¥38.5 to FY3/2026 actual ¥40 to FY3/2027 forecast ¥60 (+¥20, +50%), with 30%+ payout ratio + ¥60 minimum-floor new policy. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Cost pressure from labor + depreciation — 2024-Problem driver-hour rules + capex-driven depreciation reduced OP -14.0%. 2. Equity-method investment income decline + transport-margin deterioration — Affiliates softer earnings (specific affiliates UNVERIFIED) reduced equity-method income, driving NP -39.2%. Fare-revision lag vs. cost increases also pressed transport margins. 3. Nagoya Station-area redevelopment schedule change + FY27 recovery outlook — On December 12, 2025 Meitetsu announced the Nagoya Station-area redevelopment schedule became undecided and current-plan reassessment began, forcing major mid-term-plan revision. May 2026 Nikkei reports Meitetsu is considering new partners and reducing investment scale. But FY27 transport-margin improvement + special-items recovery should drive significant recovery. Why did the stock move this way? Stock appears soft. Three reasons. 1. NP -39.2% sharp decline + Nagoya Station-area redevelopment effective shelving drove market caution — Major NP decline + de facto shelving of the growth-pillar redevelopment lowered institutional long-term growth expectations. R+×S- pattern. 2. Soft-earnings trend + margin declines — OP / net margins declining (specific 12-quarter figures UNVERIFIED). Structural-reform necessity recognized. 3. ¥60 minimum-floor dividend policy + FY27 recovery expectations support floor — FY3/2027 dividend ¥60 forecast (¥40 to ¥60, +¥20) + 30%+ payout + ¥60 minimum-floor new policy locks in dividend-mandate demand. FY27 OP +24.4% / NP +69.9% recovery guidance supports the floor; plan restructuring + FY27 earnings confirmation would catalyze upside.
▸ Auto & specialty logistics specialists 1 names
Companies whose business is built around finished-vehicle transport, auto-parts logistics, or moving-industry consolidation — benefiting from automotive production recovery after the chip shortage, 2024-Problem rate hikes in car-carrier pricing, and growing market share through M&A-driven industry consolidation.
9039 Sakai Moving Service ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.1% Op profit: ↓ -2.7% Net profit: ↑ +1.7% Stock: up (major dividend hike ¥98→¥117, uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 引越大手 / Major moving company (Sakai)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9039 + Sakai IR + Nikkei 9039 + maonline 9039…
Company overview: Sakai Moving Service (9039) is Japan's largest moving company, headquartered in Sakai, Osaka, founded 1971 (Showa 46). Industry leader known for the 'Panda Truck' brand, 8 consecutive years revenue #1. Core: moving (individual + corporate domestic moving, 200+ branches / offices nationwide) + electrical work (residential AC installation) + cleaning services (house cleaning) + shareholder-perk nationwide rice procurement + SE Asia + North America overseas moving. Put simply: 'when you think of moving, you think of Panda Truck — Japan's universally-known No.1 moving company.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥124.741B (+3.1%), OP ¥12.572B (-2.7%), NP ¥8.75B (+1.7%) — revenue and NP grew, OP slightly down. Moving jobs 825,134 (+1.7%), unit price +1.1%. PER 12.85x, dividend ¥98 → ¥117 (+¥19, +19.4%), yield 3.47% — major dividend hike notable. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'sales-marginal / profit-slight-down' pattern. 1. Firm moving business (jobs +1.7%, unit price +1.1%) — Growth on both job count and unit price. 2. Outsourcing fees + shareholder-perk rice cost increases pressed profit — Driver-shortage-driven outsourcing (individual-contractor commissions) increased costs, driving OP -2.7%. Plus Sakai's unique nationwide-rice shareholder perks faced rice-price increases. 3. M&A acceleration — return to growth path — (1) December 2024 Kyushu 'Style' acquisition (first-ever same-industry M&A), (2) April 2026 Kanto 'Family Mover' acquisition — consecutive M&A drives industry consolidation. FY27 group revenue target ¥140B expects M&A-driven mid-long-term expansion. Why did the stock move this way? Stock in uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Major dividend hike (¥98 → ¥117, +19.4%) — Sharp dividend hike + 3.47% yield (high level) lock in dividend-mandate long-horizon demand, supporting the floor. 2. Industry-consolidation competitive-advantage evaluation — FY3/2025 Sakai share 20.6%, top-5 combined 60.8% (vs. 10 years prior: +7.0 pts Sakai, +10.6 pts top-5); consolidation strengthens Sakai's long-term pricing power — valued by institutions. 3. M&A strategy continuity + 8-consecutive-year #1 brand value — First-ever same-industry M&A 'Style' + April 2026 'Family Mover' consecutive M&A establishes 'buy-side player' position in industry-consolidation era; combined with the 8-consecutive-year #1 industry leadership, institutional long-term growth expectations rise. PER 12.85x is appropriate with valuation-correction runway.
▸ Profit hit by specific one-off event 3 names
Companies where net profit fell sharply — not from core-business deterioration — but from a clearly named one-off: reversal of a prior-year sale-and-leaseback gain, a large-property liquidation base effect, equity-method investee softening after an activist retreat, or a full MBO/delisting transaction dominating the share-price narrative.
9009 Keisei Electric Railway ▸
Revenue: ↑ +4.1% Op profit: ↓ -5.6% Net profit: ↓ -31.4% Stock: down softly (target cut ¥1,470→¥1,310, % unverified)
分類: 関東私鉄(成田アクセス) / Kanto private railway, Narita airport access (Keisei)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9009 + minkabu 9009…
Company overview: Keisei Electric Railway (9009) is a major Kanto private rail + comprehensive company headquartered in Ichikawa, Chiba, founded 1909 (Meiji 42). Core: Keisei Main Line (Oshiage-Narita Airport) + limited-express 'Skyliner' (fastest Narita Airport access route). Plus line-side residential, Keisei Bus / Taxi, Keisei Department Store, and the distinctive feature — ~19-20% Oriental Land (4661) equity stake (operator of Tokyo Disney Resort) — a 'Narita Airport access + Tokyo Disney Resort-related company.' Oriental Land equity-method-affiliate income is the bulk of Keisei's recurring + net profit — a unique structure. Put simply: 'a private rail that owns the Narita Airport route and Disneyland's stock.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥332.4B (+4.1%) up, OP ¥33.9B (-5.6%) down, NP ¥48.0B (-31.4% sharp decline). Classified as 'R+×S-' (revenue grows but profit / stock decline). FY27 also guides down. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'sales-up / profit-sharp-down' pattern. 1. Transport revenue expansion was solid (Narita Airport access demand) — Foreign visitor surge + Tokyo Disney Resort visitor transport lifted Skyliner-centered transport revenue. 2. Cost increases lowered OP — Fuel + labor + depreciation lifted costs, reducing core OP -5.6% — structural factor. 3. Oriental Land softening hit NP directly — Keisei NP depends heavily on Oriental Land's results (equity-method income); Oriental Land's cost-pressured softening drove Keisei NP -31.4%. Real-estate-related special losses also pressed NP. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades soft. Three main reasons. 1. NP -31.4% sharp decline drove market caution — Major NP decline triggered selling. 2. Market concerns about Oriental Land equity structure — Keisei's earnings dependence on Oriental Land as an 'indirect-investment' name spawned institutional view that 'holding Oriental Land directly is more investment-efficient,' curbing Keisei valuation. 3. Activist catalyst receded — UK fund Palliser Capital (only ~2% stake in Keisei) saw its OLC-sale proposal rejected at June 2024 AGM with ~30% support (sources: Toyokeizai, Nikkei), and reportedly reduced its position by March 2025 (Traders Web), removing the near-term activist-driven shareholder-return catalyst. Keisei's own staged OLC monetization (sold 18M shares for ¥61.8B in Nov 2024, lowering stake 21.04% → 20.17%) remains a longer-term re-rating lever. US broker cut target ¥1,470 → ¥1,310 on May 8, 2026 (neutral maintained).
9024 Seibu Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↓ -43.0% Op profit: ↓ -84.4% Net profit: ↓ down Stock: down (RE sale reaction-decline suppresses valuation, % unverified)
分類: 関東私鉄・ホテル / Kanto private railway & hotel (Seibu Holdings)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Seibu IR + kabutan 9024 + minkabu 9024…
Company overview: Seibu Holdings (9024) is a big company group centered on 'Seibu Railway,' which runs trains from Tokyo through Saitama Prefecture. Besides trains, the group operates Prince Hotels (40+ hotels + resorts nationwide) + Seibu Real Estate (condos + commercial buildings + land development) + the Saitama Seibu Lions pro baseball team (based at Belluna Dome Saitama) + golf courses + ski resorts (Naeba Ski Resort, etc.). Put simply: 'a comprehensive company centered on trains, combining daily life + leisure + real estate.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥513.29B (-43.0%) and OP ¥45.52B (-84.4%) (sources: Nikkei, kabutan, Yahoo Finance). But this is the reaction decline from prior year (FY3/2025) selling Tokyo Garden Terrace Kioicho to Blackstone for ~¥400B and booking a ~¥260.4B sale gain (sources: Bloomberg, QUICK Money World). Hotels & Leisure benefited from inbound — Prince Hotel occupancy + room rates rose; Urban Transport benefited from fare revisions. Dividend held at ¥42. FY3/2027 guides Revenue +8.9%, OP +16.4%, but NP ¥27B (-30.5%) as RE-monetization gains shrink. Strategic: agreed to acquire NY-based Ace Group International for up to ¥13.2B by September 2026. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'surface revenue / profit decline.' 1. Reaction decline from prior-year Tokyo Garden Terrace Kioicho liquidation gains — Selling a central-Tokyo large-format property generates a large special gain from the book-vs-market gap. But real-estate sales are one-time; the current year has no equivalent sale, so surface numbers appear much smaller (a 'reaction decline'). 2. Hotel + leisure strength (core business reality is healthy) — Foreign-tourist return lifted Prince Hotel occupancy + room rates; brand strength at Karuizawa / Hakone / Naeba captures tourism demand expansion. 3. Steady train business + fare-revision effect — Seibu Railway sees commuter-demand recovery from line-side residents, combined with fare revisions for steady earnings. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades modestly below revenue / guidance-implied levels. Three main reasons. 1. Surface numbers look like deterioration + real-estate sale outlook unclear — Major revenue + profit drops are read by the market as 'R- (revenue down),' creating automatic downward pressure. Discretionary real-estate sales make long-term earnings forecasting hard and suppress valuation. 2. Real-estate liquidation cycle uncertainty + structural commuter-pass decline concerns — Seibu discretionarily decides 'which property to liquidate when,' making it hard to forecast next year's special gains. This complicates long-term earnings forecasting and suppresses valuation (former major shareholder Cerberus Capital fully exited in August 2017 — no longer a relevant factor). Plus remote-work-entrenchment-driven long-term commuter-pass revenue decline also suppresses valuation. 3. Seibu Railway monopoly + Prince Hotels nationwide network + real-estate hidden value + dividend-maintenance policy support the floor — FY27 recovery outlook + stable dividend policy support the floor. Tokyo-Saitama monopolistic rail lines + 40+ Prince Hotels nationwide + station-area / hotel-land real-estate hidden value form the long-term enterprise-value source recognized by institutional investors.
9064 Yamato Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.8% Op profit: ↑ +99.2% Net profit: ↓ -64.0% Stock: down ~-22% (¥1,800 range, mid-term plan cut, % unverified)
分類: 宅配大手 / Major parcel delivery (Yamato / Kuroneko)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9064 + Nikkei 9064 + Yamato IR + lnews 9064 + diam…
Company overview: Yamato Holdings (9064) is Japan's largest parcel delivery company, headquartered in Tokyo-Chuo, founded 1919 (Taisho 8) — known for the 'Kuroneko Yamato' brand. Core: 'Takkyubin' (the home-delivery service Yamato invented in 1976 — a trademarked service name). Plus B2B business (BtoB logistics, 3PL) + international logistics (ocean / air forwarding, overseas parcel networks) — a 'parcel-industry leader.' ~4,000 offices nationwide + ~200,000 employees (~60,000 sales drivers) + ~2.3 billion parcels handled annually. Core player in the domestic parcel oligopoly alongside SG Holdings (Sagawa) + Japan Post (Yu-Pack). Put simply: 'Kuroneko — the company that invented home delivery in Japan and still holds the top share.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥1.865675 trillion (+5.8%), OP ¥28.304B (+99.2% sharp gain), recurring ¥26.258B (+34.1%), NP ¥13.662B (-64.0% sharp decline) — revenue + OP recovered sharply but NP fell dramatically. Plus Q3 disclosed FY recurring-profit downward revision from ¥40B → ¥27B (-32.5%); mid-term-plan performance targets also revised down sharply. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'OP surge / NP sharp decline' pattern. 1. Large-account pricing rationalization (rate hikes) created +¥29.7B OP effect — Rate-hike negotiations with Amazon / Rakuten / Yahoo via 2024-Problem driver-hour rules succeeded — main driver of the +99.2% OP surge. Plus small-business + retail customer parcel volume keeps growing on EC-market expansion. 2. NP -64% reflects absence of prior-year head-office sale-and-leaseback gain — Prior year (FY3/2025) booked a large special gain on a sale-and-leaseback transaction of the head-office building; the current period has no equivalent (sources: Diamond, LNEWS 2026/05). The earlier early-retirement-costs framing was incorrect; the real driver is the sale-leaseback comparison plus a ¥13.43B goodwill impairment in Contract Logistics. 3. Major mid-term plan target downward revision — Mid-term-plan performance targets were also revised down sharply at Q3, lowering long-term growth expectations. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades soft. Three main reasons. 1. Market impact of guidance downgrade — Q3's recurring-profit revision (-32.5%) was the biggest trigger for the decline. Analyst forecasts + target prices were lowered industry-wide; as a Nikkei 225 constituent, the downgrade automatically worsened ETF-linked index-money supply-demand. Pre-downgrade PER was ~20x; post-downgrade fell to mid-teens, with the market re-categorizing Yamato from 'growth' to 'steady-earnings.' 2. EC-major rate-negotiation concerns + labor-cost concerns — Amazon / Rakuten / Yahoo majors have strong negotiating power against rate hikes; market worries about pricing-rationalization sustainability. 2024-Problem-driven wage + outsourcing-fee increases structurally pressuring mid-long-term margins are also recognized. 3. EC market growth slowdown + logistics-tech competition concerns — Major EC platform growth slowdown + logistics-tech firms (GoTo, Pickgo) competition intensification shake long-term growth outlook; floor support requires confirming earnings-momentum recovery + rate-negotiation sustainability.
▸ Regional quiet compounder 5 names
Small-to-mid regional players — bus operators, specialized industrial-logistics niche players, small truckers, and regional holding companies — growing steadily through fare revisions, stable long-term contracts, and local market dominance, without a single high-profile catalyst driving the stock.
9065 Sankyu Inc. ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↓ down Net profit: ↓ -2.4% Stock: firm / range-bound (% unverified)
分類: 重工業向け物流 / Heavy-industry logistics & engineering (Sankyu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9065 + Sankyu IR + buffett-code 9065…
Company overview: Sankyu (9065) is a heavy-industry logistics + engineering company headquartered in Tokyo-Chuo, founded 1918 (Taisho 7). Core: logistics (on-site + port + international logistics for heavy-industry customers — steel mills, chemical plants, power utilities) + engineering (factory construction + large-machinery installation + plant scheduled maintenance (SDM) + environmental-related projects) + SE Asia / China / North America / Europe overseas logistics networks — a 'heavy-industry specialty logistics specialist.' Long-tenured relationships with Nippon Steel / JFE Steel / Sumitomo Chemical / Mitsubishi Chemical; key contractor for on-site logistics + plant SDM. Put simply: 'a heavy-cargo carrier + maintenance craftsman operating inside steel mills and chemical plants.' How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year guidance was revised up on Oct 31, 2025: NP ¥29.5B → ¥30.0B (-2.4% YoY, decline narrowed from -4.1%); dividend lifted ¥232 → ¥236 (+¥4) (sources: kabutan k202510310211, minkabu, IFIS). H1 (Apr-Sep 2025) NP ¥13.5B (+2.1%). By segment: logistics profit +1.5% (revenue -0.1%), engineering profit -3.3% (revenue +8.5%) — contrasting moves. PER 13.42x, PBR 1.39x. Analyst avg target ¥10,140 (source: kabuyoho). Why did business move this way? Reasons for the Oct 31 upward revision (¥29.5B → ¥30.0B NP) per company disclosure: 1. Engineering SDM (scheduled turnaround maintenance) volume in spring/autumn expected above plan — the main driver of the revision (source: kabutan). 2. Large on-site customer-factory construction + environmental-project wins expected — additional profit contribution. 3. Logistics business steady — Steel-mill / chemical-plant on-site logistics + port-international logistics stable; logistics profit +1.5%. The full-year picture is still NP −2.4% with engineering −3.3% — a soft year, just less soft than originally guided. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds firm. Three main reasons. 1. PER 13.42x + PBR 1.39x appropriate-valuation balance — On the low side among mid-cap logistics; appropriate given earnings stability + yield. PBR 1.39x requires limited TSE PBR-improvement response. 2. Continuous dividend step-ups — ¥232 → ¥236 (+¥4) signals dividend-policy of holding even through soft earnings. ~40% payout ratio is standard for mid-cap logistics; dividend-mandate long-horizon investors generate steady demand. 3. Decarbonization-theme long-term opportunity attention — Environmental projects (decarbonization plant refurbishment) are recognized as an engineering long-term growth opportunity; institutional money positions Sankyu as 'a logistics + engineering name playable on decarbonization themes.' But engineering -5.9% is a short-term concern; FY27 large-contract trajectory is the next focus.

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9017 + niigata-kotsu IR + Niigata Kotsu Q3 disclos…
Company overview: Niigata Kotsu (9017) is a regional integrated transport company headquartered in Niigata City, founded 1944. Niigata-prefecture bus business at the core, plus real estate (Niigata-city commercial buildings, rentals, condo sales) + retail (Niigata Kotsu Shoji = convenience stores, gas stations) + hospitality (Niigata Tokyu REI Hotel, Hotel Metz) + tourism (group travel) + auto maintenance — a regional conglomerate. Put simply: a regional-infrastructure company centered on Niigata-prefecture buses, also handling real estate, convenience stores, and hotels. How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year actuals: Revenue ¥20.331B (+1.7%), OP ¥2.237B (+11.0%) — steady growth (per Yahoo Finance, May 15 2026). Q3 YTD was Revenue ¥15.44B (+1.8%), OP ¥1.762B (+9.4%), recurring ¥1.769B (+9.7%) — good progress. Dividend held at ¥10 for FY3/2025, FY3/2026, and FY3/2027 forecast (yield ~0.5%). Unique structure: a regional transport company where non-bus diversification segments support earnings. Why did business move this way? Three reasons (segment-level detail UNVERIFIED). 1. Real-estate revenue contribution — Niigata City commercial buildings + rentals + condo sales appear to generate stable revenue central to earnings. Niigata land prices steady. 2. Strong retail + possible bus fare-revision effect — Niigata Kotsu Shoji convenience stores + gas stations contribute stable revenue. MLIT-approved bus fare hike may have improved economics. 3. Niigata inbound tourism recovery (magnitude UNVERIFIED) — Niigata three themes (Sado Island + snow-country tourism in Yuzawa / Naeba + rice-country) likely lifted foreign visitors; sightseeing-bus + hotel revenue may have grown. Why did the stock move this way? Stock direction likely gentle uptrend but precise magnitude UNVERIFIED. Three main themes (specific institutional rationale UNVERIFIED). 1. Diversified portfolio stability rating — Bus + real estate + retail + hospitality + tourism diversification spreads cyclical risk and stabilizes earnings. 2. Niigata-prefecture bus monopoly + real-estate stable revenue base — Major local bus routes effectively monopolized by Niigata Kotsu — stable regional-infrastructure position. Plus Niigata-city commercial-leasing long-term contracts act as stable cash-flow. 3. Earnings momentum + long-term inbound runway — Dividend yield only ~0.5% so dividend appeal is limited; however OP +11.0% growth + diversification supports the floor. Sado Island + Yuzawa / Naeba ski resorts + rice-country Niigata inbound potential is recognized as mid-long-term growth opportunity.

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9034 + nikkei 9034 + kabutan 9034 + Nansou IR FY3/…
Company overview: Nansou Tsuun (9034) is a mid-tier regional logistics company headquartered in Togane-shi, Chiba, founded 1949. Core: trucking + warehousing + 3PL + moving services + staffing, providing comprehensive logistics across Chiba / Saitama / Ibaraki within the Greater Tokyo area. Owns 56 buildings (40,000+ tsubo) of warehouse / DC / rental space with branches in Togane, Mobara, Sakura, Ichihara — a community-rooted regional logistics player. Put simply: a Chiba-based mid-tier logistics player bundling warehousing, delivery, and 3PL. Note: specific major shipper names (food makers, etc.) need to be cross-checked with company IR (UNVERIFIED). How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: Operating revenue ¥16.499B (+2.44%), OP ¥2.019B (flat), recurring ¥2.029B (+0.12%), net profit ¥1.353B (-1.31%) — modest revenue growth but flat-to-slightly-down profit. Annual dividend ¥70 (interim ¥30 + year-end ¥40); FY27/3 forecast ¥80 (¥40/¥40) — step-up planned. The previously circulated revenue ¥17.049B / +5.9% was the initial company guidance; actuals came in below that. Why did business move this way? Three likely reasons (partially UNVERIFIED — needs tanshin confirmation). 1. 2024-Problem rate revisions taking hold + small volume growth lifted revenue, but 2. fuel and labor-cost rises were not fully passed through into rates, capping margin gains, and 3. mix shifts in warehouse / tenant asset-side earnings may have suppressed profit growth. Detailed segment drivers should be checked in the tanshin. Why did the stock move this way? Stock is broadly range-bound to gently moving (around ¥1,200 as of May 2026). Three likely reasons (inferred). 1. Revenue growth + step-up dividend (¥70 to ¥80) supports quiet bids for steady payout, but 2. flat-to-slightly-down profit lacks momentum to drive aggressive re-rating, and 3. as a Chiba-rooted stable mid-cap, long-horizon dividend buyers provide a floor. Note: original claims of 5-consecutive record highs and Kikkoman / Yamasa as core on-site logistics clients could not be sourced and are UNVERIFIED.
9059 Kanda Holdings (カンダホールディングス) ▸
Revenue: → +0.2% (Q3 YTD) Op profit: ↑ +9.4% (Q3 YTD) Net profit: ↑ +10.4% (Q3 YTD) Stock: stable ~¥846 (% unverified)
分類: 関東中堅総合物流 / Kanto mid-tier comprehensive logistics (Kanda Holdings)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9059 + Kanda HD IR + Kanda HD Q3 disclosure…
Company overview: Kanda Holdings (9059) is a mid-tier comprehensive logistics holding company headquartered in Kanda-Misaki-cho, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo (NOT Koto-ku), established November 1943. With 26 subsidiaries, the group runs freight trucking (domestic logistics) + international logistics (international courier, etc.) + real estate rental + other logistics-related businesses. Domestic logistics covers general cargo trucking, in-warehouse work, DC operations, packing, warehousing. Put simply: a Kanto-based mid-tier comprehensive logistics group bundling domestic logistics, international logistics, and real estate rental. How did business move? FY3/2026 company guidance: Revenue ¥52.3B, OP ¥3.6B, recurring ¥3.65B, NP ¥2.373B. Q3 YTD actuals: Revenue ¥39.63B (+0.2%), OP ¥2.999B (+9.4%), recurring ¥3.159B (+11.7%), quarterly NP ¥2.059B (+10.4%) — revenue nearly flat but profit growing steadily. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Trucking efficiency + rate-revision effect — 2024-Problem driver-hour rules drove rate hikes, improving trucking margins. Revenue flat but margin improvement lifted OP. 2. Steady international logistics — International courier and related services generate stable revenue. 3. Cost discipline + stable real-estate rental income — Fuel pass-through + route efficiency + rental income from owned property all contributed. Why did the stock move this way? Stock around ¥846 on March 27, 2026. Three main reasons. 1. Stable profit growth + diversified-portfolio stability — Limited institutional attention as a mid-cap, but steady profit growth supports quiet bids. 2. ¥1.61B / ~150-vehicle CAPEX plan over FY2026-2028 — Fleet renewal maintains and upgrades transport capacity, strengthening mid-long-term operating base. 3. Sustained 2024-Problem rate-revision benefit + stable real-estate rental cashflows — Industry-wide rate revisions continue; rental income provides a recession-resilient cashflow source. Note: original draft claims of Isuzu / Mitsubishi Fuso authorized dealer, auto maintenance and sales business, and early positioning for truck-EV shift in dealer sales do NOT match Kanda disclosed segments (domestic logistics / international logistics / real estate / other) and have been removed as FABRICATED.
9081 Kanagawa Chuo Bus / Kanachu (神奈川中央交通) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +7.3% Op profit: ↓ -8.3% Net profit: ↓ -28.8% Stock: range-bound ¥3,215-¥3,880 (direction unverified)
分類: 大手バス・自動車販売 / Major bus operator & auto dealer (Kanachu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9081 + Kanachu IR + Odakyu HD FY3/2026 disclosure…
Company overview: Kanagawa Chuo Bus (Kanachu, 9081) is a major Kanto bus operator headquartered in Hiratsuka, Kanagawa, founded 1921. Operates local buses + airport limousine (Haneda, Narita) + highway buses + charter sightseeing + community buses across Kanagawa and parts of western Tokyo. Odakyu Electric Railway-affiliated; centered on Odakyu Line corridor (Machida, Hon-Atsugi, Odawara). Beyond bus, auto sales and real estate are also large revenue pillars — auto sales alone is over 30% of consolidated revenue. Put simply: Odakyu Group largest bus / mobility group supporting Kanagawa daily life. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥126.7B (+7.3%), OP ¥6.7B (-8.3%), recurring profit ¥6.6B (-14.0%), NP ¥3.6B (-28.8%) — revenue up, profit compressed. Revenue growth was led mainly by the auto-sales segment (+¥6.3B YoY); the passenger-auto (bus) segment grew only +¥0.6B in revenue. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the revenue-up / profit-down pattern. 1. Auto sales drove revenue, while passenger-auto depreciation and wage costs pressed profit — Higher auto-dealer unit sales lifted revenue; in the passenger-auto segment, expanded capex drove higher depreciation and employee wage / benefit improvements (driver pay raises) reduced OP. 2. Real-estate segment OP also fell — Real-estate OP declined ¥0.5B YoY, contributing to the overall profit drop. 3. EV bus introduction and autonomous-driving pilots — decarbonization / next-gen mobility investment ongoing — 5 large EV route buses introduced March 2025; Hiratsuka autonomous-driving pilots are progressing. These are sources of mid-long-term competitiveness but currently weigh on profit via depreciation. Why did the stock move this way? Stock traded in a ¥3,215-¥3,880 range during 2026 (one-year directional trend UNVERIFIED). Plausible drivers. 1. Profit weakness priced in + awaiting earnings momentum recovery — Modest dividend yield (FY3/2027 forecast 90 yen); long-horizon dividend-mandate holders likely core. 2. Kanagawa bus base + Haneda limousine + auto-sales revenue diversification — Operating Kanagawa main bus routes is a regional infrastructure position; auto-sales segment provides revenue diversification. 3. Odakyu Group synergies + EV / autonomous-driving early-investment mid-long-term effect — Odakyu Line cooperation; current EV / autonomous investment burden becomes cost saving + competitive source in full EV / autonomous era; community-bus operations in aging communities qualify for municipal subsidies and are recognized as ESG social value.
▸ Inbound tourism beneficiaries 16 names
Railways, leisure operators, taxis, and buses whose core earnings and stock story is driven by the surge in foreign tourists — via unique route monopolies (KIX-Namba, Narita Skyliner, Hakone, Arashiyama, Mt. Fuji, Kyoto/Osaka Golden Route), the Osaka-Kansai Expo, and premium tourism experiences that foreign visitors cannot bypass.
9021 West Japan Railway (JR West) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +8.1% Op profit: ↑ +9.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: down ~-5% post-results (¥2,995→¥2,857)
分類: 鉄道大手 / Major JR railway (West Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Nikkei JR West Expo article + kabutan 9021 + honichi.com 9…
Company overview: JR West (West Japan Railway, 9021) is a major Western-Japan railway, headquartered in Osaka, born from the 1987 JNR privatization. Centered on Kinki, Chugoku, and Hokuriku passenger rail, it spans rail (conventional + Sanyo Shinkansen + Hokuriku Shinkansen), station-mall retail (Ekimarche), station-building real estate (Osaka / Kyoto / Hiroshima Station Buildings), hospitality (Hotel Granvia), and ICOCA payment infrastructure — a 'Western-Japan urban-transport infrastructure company.' 60+ consolidated subsidiaries; operates Sanyo Shinkansen + Hokuriku Shinkansen — major arteries after Tokaido (JR Central). Put simply: 'JR West is JR East's Western-Japan counterpart — supporting the lives and tourism of Kansai-to-Chugoku/Hokuriku travelers.' How did business move? FY3/2026 revenue ¥1.8458 trillion (+8.1%), OP ¥198B (+9.9%) — growth on both lines. H1 recurring +16.8% to ¥115.1B; Q2 recurring +27.9% acceleration. OP margin improved from 11.4% to 13.4%; dividend raised to ¥90.5 (vs original ¥86; vs prior ¥84.5), +¥5. Stock spiked to ¥3,278 (+6.2%) on results. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Osaka-Kansai Expo special demand — Held April 13 - October 13, 2025 — the largest international event ever, flooding Kansai with visitors. JR West captured huge demand around Shin-Osaka, Osaka, and the USJ-adjacent Sakurajima Line. Management discloses concrete figures: '¥20B revenue + ¥15B OP boost.' 2. Rapid inbound recovery — Kyoto / Osaka / Kobe / Nara / Hiroshima (Peace Memorial Park, Miyajima) sit on the inbound Golden Route; Q1 inbound revenue hit record ¥22B; full-year inbound transport ¥45B (+10%). 3. April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect + Hokuriku Shinkansen Tsuruga extension — Fare revisions reflected fully; Hokuriku Shinkansen extended to Tsuruga in March 2024, growing Kansai-Hokuriku ridership. Why did the stock move this way? Three reasons. 1. Earnings + dividend hike combo — Lifting from original ¥86 to ¥90.5 dividend was rated as 'strengthening shareholder return even in growth periods'; stock spiked +6.2% on results day. 2. 'Kansai theme' attention — Expo demand + inbound recovery + 2030 Osaka IR (with casino) opening combine into a structural Kansai-tourism demand story investors back. 3. Re-rating as a 'hidden real-estate stock' — Osaka Umekita Phase 2 + Grand Front Osaka + Kyoto Station redevelopment progress expanded JR West's station-area real-estate hidden value + rent income. US institutional investors rate it highly on 'stable cash flow + extensive real estate + inbound theme'; long-horizon money is increasing allocation.
9022 Central Japan Railway (JR Central) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +9.5% Op profit: ↑ +18.1% Net profit: ↑ +20.6% Stock: down ~-24% from 2026 high (¥4,329→~¥3,300)
分類: 鉄道大手 / Major JR railway (Central Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9022 + sbbit 9022 + Nikkei 9022…
Company overview: JR Central (9022) is Japan's most profitable railway, headquartered in Nagoya, born from the 1987 JNR privatization. Core: Tokaido Shinkansen (Tokyo-Nagoya-Osaka, one of the world's highest passenger-volume / frequency high-speed lines). Plus Tokai-area conventional lines, station retail/hotels/real estate, and the future Linear Chuo Shinkansen (Shinagawa-Nagoya-Osaka, superconducting maglev — originally targeted for 2027 but pushed beyond 2027 due to Shizuoka-segment delays). How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year tanshin: consolidated revenue ¥2,006.2B (+9.5%), OP ¥830.2B (+18.1%), recurring profit ¥780.9B (+20.3%), NP ¥552.8B (+20.6%) — strong growth and record-level earnings. OP margin stays above 40%, extraordinary for a regulated industry. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1) Tokaido Shinkansen volumes recovered across both business travel and tourism, sharply lifting transport revenue. 2) Osaka-Kansai Expo (Apr-Oct 2025) demand + foreign-visitor Golden Route (Tokyo-Kyoto-Osaka) traffic structurally lifted volumes (specific yen-impact figures UNVERIFIED). 3) Full-year effect of the October-2023 fare revision + new revenue initiatives such as digital booking and tourism products. Why did the stock move this way? In 2026 the stock made a high of ¥4,329 and a low of ¥3,471, currently around ¥3,300 (June 2026) — i.e., weak year-to-date, not a clean uptrend. Three drivers: 1) industry-leading profitability + stable cash flow attracts long-horizon institutions; 2) inbound Golden Route theme directly captured; 3) long-term Linear option is both upside and risk — Shizuoka-segment Southern Alps / Oi River-flow issues continue to push completion beyond the original 2027 target, and heavy capex with uncertain ROI keeps the stock at a discount to record earnings.
9001 Tobu Railway ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.8% Op profit: ↓ -6.2% Net profit: ↑ +8.4% Stock: up (clear uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 関東大手私鉄 / Major Kanto private railway (Tobu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9001 + Nikkei 9001 + Tobu Railway FY3/2026 IR + To…
Company overview: Tobu Railway (9001) is one of Greater Tokyo's largest private railways, headquartered in Tokyo-Sumida, founded 1897 (Meiji 30). Operates the longest Kanto private-rail network — 463 km total, extending from Tokyo (Asakusa, Oshiage) to North Kanto (Saitama / Tochigi / Gunma). Diversified businesses span rail + bus, Tokyo Skytree (Tobu Tower subsidiary), Nikko / Kinugawa tourism (SL Taiju = steam-locomotive revival), Tobu Zoo Park, line-side residential development, Tobu Department Store, Tobu Hotel, Tobu Construction — a 'comprehensive urban-life infrastructure company.' Put simply: 'Tobu is another JR for Kanto — supporting daily life and tourism from Tokyo downtown to Nikko.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥655.435B (+3.8%), OP ¥70.0B (−6.2%, down), NP ¥55.62B (+8.4%) — 3rd consecutive record-high NP (sources: minkabu/9001, kabuyoho). NP was lifted by investment-securities sale gains (~¥9.71B at Q3 cumulative). Core transport segment was down on cost pressure; leisure (Skytree, Nikko) was strong on inbound. Dividend raised ¥60 → ¥67.5; guidance and dividend revised up mid-year. Analyst consensus Buy; avg target ¥3,175 (~+12% upside). Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Rapid Tokyo Skytree inbound demand recovery — Skytree (634m, operated by Tobu Tower subsidiary) is a 'must-visit' for foreign tourists; admission revenue + adjacent Tokyo Solamachi commercial sales expanded sharply. 2. Nikko / Kinugawa tourism recovery — World Heritage Nikko Toshogu + Kinugawa Onsen + SL Taiju three tourism assets capture foreign + domestic senior demand simultaneously. 3. April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect + stable line-side residential / commercial — Barrier-free surcharge etc. fare revisions reflected fully in FY3/2026; Saitama / Northeast-Tokyo residential + rental operations function as long-term cash-flow sources. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. 3 consecutive record NPs + earnings / dividend upward revisions — Solid actuals-backed upward revisions earn institutional trust; positioned as a 'stable conglomerate.' 2. Inbound + Skytree theme attention — Tokyo Skytree, as a structural tourism revenue source unique to Tobu, directly captures long-term inbound growth. 3. Shareholder-return strengthening — Policy-stock sales + buyback consideration + step-up dividend strengthen capital policy, supporting valuation as PBR-improvement expectation. FY27 growth + dividend hike plan further lifts long-horizon holding incentive.
9041 Kintetsu Group Holdings ▸

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9041 + Nikkei 9041 + Kintetsu IR…
Company overview: Kintetsu Group Holdings (9041) is a major Kansai private rail + comprehensive company headquartered in Osaka, founded 1910 (Meiji 43). Core: Kinki Nippon Railway (Japan's longest private-rail network at 501 km, connecting Osaka-Nagoya-Nara-Kyoto-Ise-Shima, limited-express 'Hinotori', 'Shimakaze', 'Urban Liner'). Plus Abeno Harukas (Japan's tallest building at 300m), Kintetsu Department Store, Kintetsu / Miyako Hotels, Ise-Shima / Nara tourism development, and the global-major forwarder Kintetsu Express (KWE) international logistics. Put simply: 'a huge conglomerate where a global logistics company is attached to a Kansai-Chubu private rail.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥1.7503 trillion (+0.5%), OP ¥89.4B (+6.0%), NP ¥53.7B (+15.1%) — growth on all lines. Dividend ¥50 → ¥60 (+¥10). Expo effect + Hinotori limited-express expansion drove solid tourism / inbound; real estate via condo sales + used-house repurchase resale; retail via Expo Official Store + Abeno Harukas Kintetsu remodel; international logistics started Singapore semiconductor-related warehouse. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Osaka-Kansai Expo special demand + Hinotori expansion — Expo lifted Osaka / Namba visitor count + Expo Official Store (operated by Kintetsu Department Store) sales. Osaka-Nagoya 'Hinotori' is popular for premium interior + reasonable fares; added trains lifted transport revenue. 2. Nara / Ise-Shima inbound surge — Foreign-tourist 'Nara (Todaiji, Kasuga Taisha) + Ise-Shima (Ise Jingu)' demand recovered sharply; Kintetsu limited-express 'Shimakaze' and 'Blue Symphony' (Ise-Shima tourism) grew. 3. International-logistics semiconductor demand growth + strong real estate — KWE's semiconductor logistics for TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix expanded; Singapore warehouse start-up built mid-long-term base. Condo sales + used-house repurchase resale also expanded real-estate revenue. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades around ¥3,600 (YTD range ¥3,052–¥3,628). Three main reasons. 1. JPMorgan Securities investment-rating upgrade — JPMorgan upgraded the rating; stock rose ¥215 to ¥3,512 (source: search news). 2. Dividend ¥50 → ¥60 increase — +¥10 shareholder-return strengthening attracted dividend-mandate buyers (source: IRBANK dividend history). 3. Governance reform — June 2026 AGM is to resolve transition to Company with Audit and Supervisory Committee — evaluated as board-oversight strengthening (source: search). (NOTE: The prior claim of activist Aya Nomura's 2.7% stake disclosure on 2025-05-21 was NOT corroborated in any source and has been REMOVED. Also note: KWE (Kintetsu Express) was sold to KKR in 2022 and is no longer a Kintetsu HD subsidiary — semiconductor-logistics claims via KWE are out of date.)

分類: 関西大手私鉄 / Major Kansai private railway (Hankyu Hanshin)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9042 + minkabu 9042…
Company overview: Hankyu Hanshin Holdings (9042) is Kansai's largest private-rail holding company, headquartered in Osaka, formed in 2006 by integrating Hankyu Railway and Hanshin Electric Railway. Beyond the Hankyu Railway (Kobe / Takarazuka / Kyoto lines) + Hanshin Electric Railway (Main Line) urban-rail core, it spans Umeda Osaka Station-area redevelopment (Grand Front Osaka + Umekita Phase 2), pro-baseball Hanshin Tigers, the all-female Takarazuka Revue musical company, Hankyu Travel (Kansai's largest travel agency), and Hankyu Hanshin Express international transport — a 'Kansai urban-life infrastructure + entertainment conglomerate.' Put simply: 'a Hankyu Hanshin kingdom managing Kansai's towns, rail, sports, theater, and travel all in one.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥881.504B (+9.6%), OP ¥111.243B (+20.1%) — sharp growth, all segments up. But FY27 guides OP / recurring decline due to Expo + Hanshin Tigers special-demand rollover — reaction-decline risk is on the market's mind. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings surged. 1. Osaka-Kansai Expo lifted Umeda tourism / commercial demand — Expo visitors flooded Osaka / Umeda; HHH-operated Umeda commercial (Hankyu Umeda flagship, Hanshin Umeda flagship, HEP FIVE, Hankyu Sanbangai) + hotels (Hankyu International, Daiichi Hotel Osaka) saw foot traffic surge. 2. Hanshin Tigers 2023 Japan Series win sustained entertainment revenue — First Japan Series title in 38 years (won November 2023) continues to support elevated merchandise + tickets + Hanshin railway riders + Hanshin Koshien attendance. 3. Inbound recovery + continued Umeda redevelopment + fare-revision full-year effect — Kansai inbound demand recovered; Takarazuka Revue overseas audiences expanded; Umekita Phase 2 'Grand Green Osaka' opened Sep 2024; April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect added. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. High-quality earnings — sharp growth + all-segment growth — OP +20.1% accompanied by revenue growth in every segment is rated as 'high-quality' by institutions. 2. Umeda redevelopment long-term growth-base rating — Osaka Station + Umeda redevelopment (Umekita Phase 1 + 2, Yodobashi Umeda redevelopment) places HHH as the central player; expectations for post-completion 'Kansai's largest commercial + business cluster' draw long-horizon money. 3. Hanshin Tigers + Takarazuka unique entertainment assets + 2030 Osaka IR expectations — Tigers + Takarazuka have unique global cultural-value assets generating stable long-term merchandise + ticket + media-rights revenue; 2030 Osaka Yumeshima IR opening is rated as a mid-long-term growth opportunity.
9044 Nankai Electric Railway ▸
Revenue: ↑ +4.4% (H1) Op profit: ↑ +17.0% (H1) Net profit: ↑ +10.1% (H1) Stock: -17% from Apr peak (¥3,318→~¥2,584)
分類: 関西私鉄 / Kansai private railway (Nankai)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Nikkei 9044 + diamond 9044 + note 9044 article…
Company overview: Nankai Electric Railway (9044) is a Kansai established private rail + comprehensive company headquartered in Osaka, founded 1885 (Meiji 18). Core: Nankai Main Line (Namba-Kansai International Airport) + Koya Line (Namba-Mt. Koya), with airport limited-express 'Rapi:t'. Only Nankai (Rapi:t) and JR West (Kanku Rapid) provide rail access from KIX to central Osaka (Namba). Plus super-large commercial cluster around Namba (Takashimaya Osaka, Namba CITY, Namba Parks — 140-year founding-era real-estate holdings), Swissôtel Nankai Osaka, and the recent Tsutenkaku operator subsidiarization — a 'Kansai International Airport access + Namba urban-development leader.' Put simply: 'a company that monopolizes the route from KIX to Namba and owns the Namba town itself.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥195.392B (+4.4%), OP ¥34.171B (+17.0%), recurring +8.3%, NP +10.1% — revenue and all profit levels at record highs. Dividend ¥40 → ¥50 (+25%). Analyst average target ¥3,100 (+21.9% upside) signals broad bullish view. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Airport-line passenger revenue surge (KIX-Namba) — China / Korea / SE-Asian foreign visitors surged; Nankai airport-line passenger revenue +10.3% YoY. Rapi:t + airport express is ~20% of rail revenue (high ARPU); visitor growth directly lifts earnings. 2. Continued real-estate expansion — real-estate revenue ¥35.362B (+15.4%), OP ¥10.122B (+4.6%). Hotels + condos + 7-Eleven station-shops grew, and Namba-area real-estate hidden value also expanded. 3. Tsutenkaku operator subsidiarization + Expo special demand + fare-revision full-year effect — subsidiarized the Osaka-symbol Tsutenkaku operator for entertainment expansion; Osaka-Kansai Expo lifted Namba + KIX access demand; April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect added. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. 'Unique KIX-Namba direct network' rating — Only Nankai + JR West provide KIX-to-central-Osaka rail. In the yen-weakness inbound era, institutions buy it strongly as 'a stock that monopolizes the foreign-tourist transit route.' 2. Re-rating of 140-year Namba-area real-estate hidden value — Takashimaya Osaka + Namba CITY + Namba Parks integrated 'Nankai's city' — super-large real estate held since founding is being re-rated under the inflation real-asset theme. 3. Naniwasuji Line + 2030 Osaka IR long-term growth + major dividend hike — Naniwasuji Line (planned 2030s) will enable direct KIX-Shin-Osaka access; 2030 Osaka Yumeshima IR drives airport-access demand; ¥40 → ¥50 (+25%) dividend hike lifts PBR-improvement expectations.

Company overview: Keihan Holdings (9045) is a Kansai major private rail + comprehensive company headquartered in Osaka — current entity since 1949. Core: Keihan Electric Railway (Keihan Main / Nakanoshima / Keishin Lines, Kyoto-Osaka connector). Plus Kyoto / Osaka line-side residential, Keihan Department Store Moriguchi flagship, central-Kyoto luxury hotels (Kyoto Brighton Hotel + Kyoto Century Hotel + The Thousand Kyoto), and Hirakata Park (Kansai's oldest amusement park) — a 'Kyoto-Osaka urban-life infrastructure + Kyoto tourism leader.' Put simply: 'a company that captures both the Osaka-Kyoto commuter flow and the global tourists who come to Kyoto.' How did business move? FY3/2026: All segments grew, revenue ¥332.471B (+6.0%), OP ¥49.152B (+16.8%) — record-high, 3 consecutive record years. Equity ratio improved to 37.5%. Dividend: annual ¥40 → ¥89 (+¥49, +122.5%) — the largest Kansai private-rail hike. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for 3 consecutive record years. 1. Rapid Kyoto inbound recovery — Kyoto is one of foreign tourists' most popular destinations; Keihan near-monopolistically accesses Gion, Fushimi Inari Taisha, Uji, Demachiyanagi tourist sites. Visitor growth directly lifts transit revenue. 2. Kyoto hotel revenue expansion — Kyoto Brighton Hotel + Kyoto Century Hotel + The Thousand Kyoto in central Kyoto directly captured foreign-visitor stay demand at high ARPU + occupancy — hotel-business profitability lifted substantially. 3. Strong Osaka / Keihan-line real estate + retail + fare-revision full-year effect + Expo special demand — Moriguchi / Neyagawa / Hirakata line-side residential + commercial, Keihan Department Store Moriguchi remodel effect, April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect, Osaka-Kansai Expo special demand all simultaneously lifted profit across segments. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Major dividend hike ¥40 → ¥89 (+122.5%) — The most aggressive return policy among Kansai private rails attracted strong buying from dividend-mandate long-horizon investors. 2. 3 consecutive record-high stability-growth story — 3 years of revenue + profit + record highs is rated by institutions as 'the most shareholder-return-conscious of Kansai private rails.' 3. Kyoto tourism + 2030 Osaka IR long-term growth theme — Keihan's Kyoto-tourism-site monopoly access + Kyoto hotel inbound demand + 2030 Osaka Yumeshima IR opening structural Kansai-tourism growth attract long-horizon money. PBR-improvement expectations continue.

Company overview: Sanyo Electric Railway (9052) is a mid-tier Kansai private rail headquartered in Kobe, Hyogo, founded 1907. Core: Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line (Kobe-Sannomiya-Himeji, with Direct Limited Express service). Key feature: mutual through-service with Hanshin Electric Railway (Hankyu-Hanshin HD Group) enables direct Sannomiya-Himeji access. Plus Sanyo Department Store Himeji flagship, line-side residential, and Himeji-area tourism transport — a Kobe-Himeji urban-life infrastructure company. Put simply: a Hankyu-Hanshin HD group member that connects Kobe and Himeji via Hanshin through-service. How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 was revenue + profit growth: 3Q cumulative recurring profit ¥4,040M; quarterly NP +42.0% to ¥3,542M, boosted by a retirement-benefit accounting-change special gain. Full-year recurring forecast was upward-revised from ¥4,268M to ¥4,775M (+14.1%). A mid-cap private rail with stable underlying trends. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1) Transport revenue lift — Osaka-Kansai Expo demand (Apr-Oct 2025), the addition of Beppu Station as a Direct-Limited-Express stop, Hanshin through-service-enabled Sannomiya-Himeji direct-access demand, and Himeji Castle inbound tourism combined to raise volumes. 2) Retirement-benefit accounting-change special gain — a one-off gain (single-digit yen-B scale) was the main driver of NP +42.0%. 3) Stable line-side real-estate income + intra-Hankyu-Hanshin-Group operational synergies. Why did the stock move this way? Around March 2026 the stock traded near ¥2,048; over the past year price action has been range-bound rather than a straight-line uptrend (clear uptrend claim UNVERIFIED). Drivers: 1) NP +42% + upward revision triggered short-term buying, but the special-gain one-off nature caps upside; 2) Hankyu-Hanshin HD Group stability + fare-revision coordination + RE know-how synergies; 3) Expo + Himeji Castle long-term inbound + gradual equity-ratio / dividend improvements support PBR-improvement expectations.
9010 Fuji Kyuko (富士急行) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +2.5% Op profit: ↑ +5.4% Net profit: ↑ +13.5% Stock: up gently (inbound theme, % unverified)
分類: 観光鉄道・レジャー / Tourism railway & leisure (Fuji Kyuko)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9010 + kabuyoho 9010 + Fuji Kyuko IR + Fuji-Q High…
Company overview: Fuji Kyuko (9010) is a comprehensive tourism / transport company headquartered in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi. Integrates Fuji Kyuko Line (railway) + Fujikyu Bus (local + highway) + Fuji-Q Highland (a top-class Kanto amusement park with thrill rides like FUJIYAMA, Eejanaika, Do-Dodonpa) + Hotel Fuji-Q Highland Resort + Mt. Fuji Ropeway (Yoshida route) + Fujikyu Travel. Since Mt. Fuji 2013 UNESCO World Cultural Heritage listing, Fuji Kyuko established itself as monopolistic operator of Mt. Fuji transport + tourism and transformed into a direct beneficiary of inbound tourism. Put simply: a tourism company monopolistically operating Mt. Fuji access and play areas. How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: Revenue ¥53.517B (vs prior ¥52.231B = +2.5%), OP ¥8.761B (+5.4%), recurring ¥8.617B (+6.1%), NP ¥5.799B (+13.5%) — broad growth, beating initial forecast. FY3/2027 guide: Revenue ¥56.5B (+5.6%), OP ¥8.95B, recurring ¥8.62B, NP ¥5.75B. Dividend lifted ¥1 to ¥30. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Mt. Fuji inbound tourism structural expansion — Mt. Fuji remains a must-visit for foreign tourists; mid-year recurring-profit forecast upward-revised (¥3.55B to ¥4.78B) reflects inbound demand recovery lifting transport + tourism revenue above plan. 2. Fuji-Q Highland visitor recovery — Domestic + overseas visitors recovering attraction usage. Free-entrance + per-attraction pricing model (introduced July 2018) helps capture repeat + short-stay visitors. 3. Line-side hotel + resort + fare-revision effect — Lake Kawaguchi-area hotels + Highland Resort + Mt. Fuji Onsen support revenue; Mt. Fuji-view hotels command premium pricing. Fuji Kyuko Line fare-revision (timing UNVERIFIED) also improved economics. Why did the stock move this way? Stock appears in gentle uptrend. Three reasons. 1. Inbound tourism theme + Mt. Fuji brand monopoly — Mt. Fuji is Japan iconic World Heritage site; monopolistic operation of its transport / tourism infrastructure is a hard-to-replicate barrier. Recognized by institutions as positioned to structurally capture mid-long-term inbound growth. 2. Fuji-Q Highland reinvention success — Free entrance + per-attraction pricing captures repeat + short-stay visitors (specific KPIs UNVERIFIED). Business model adapted to SNS-era tourism trends resonates. 3. Step-up dividend policy + shareholder perks — Recognized as a major mid-cap tourism name; both institutional and retail hold positions. Modest dividend yield but shareholder perks (Fuji-Q Highland tickets) attract long-horizon money.
9012 Chichibu Railway (秩父鉄道) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +6.8% Op profit: ↑ +76.9% Net profit: ↑ +231.6% Stock: up sharply (record NP, uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 地方観光鉄道 / Regional tourism railway (Chichibu Railway)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9012 + chichibu-railway IR + Chichibu Railway tour…
Company overview: Chichibu Railway (9012) is a small regional third-sector railway centered on Chichibu in northern Saitama, operating the 71.7-km Chichibu Main Line (Kumagaya - Hanyu - Mitsumineguchi). Plus tourism (SL Paleo Express = C58 steam locomotive operation, Nagatoro line-down, Mt. Hodo ropeway), real estate, freight, bus. SL operation is the tourism icon, establishing Chichibu Railway as a regional tourism brand. Put simply: 'a regional tourism railway running steam locomotives near Tokyo.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥5.637B (+6.8%), OP ¥539M (+76.9%), recurring ¥484M (+79.5%), NP ¥383M (+231.6%) — very strong profit beat for a small-cap. But FY3/2027 guides OP -51.8% on 'SUSABINO Terrace' (new tourism facility) capex + opex — FY26 may be peak earnings. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings surged. 1. Rapid inbound recovery + Chichibu region re-rated — As foreign visitors seek 'hidden Tokyo-area destinations,' Chichibu (Chichibu Shrine, Nagatoro, Mitsumine Shrine, Hitsujiyama Park shibazakura) gained spotlight; foreign visitor counts surged. Chichibu Railway is the near-monopoly access to regional sites. 2. SL Paleo Express rebrand success + media exposure — SL operation rebranded as tourism experience; SNS spread + railway-fan YouTuber coverage captured domestic young + overseas tourists. 3. 'SUSABINO Terrace' opening + fare-revision effect — New facility opened July 2025 at the Mt. Hodo summit (accessed via Hodosan Ropeway from Nagatoro station) extended visit duration + lifted spending per visitor; rail fare-revision also improved economics. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Strong earnings impact of NP +231.6% — Record-level profit drove institutional + retail buying. 2. Structural re-rating as 'new Tokyo-area destination' — As foreign visitors avoid over-tourism (Kyoto, Asakusa) and seek 'quiet Japanese landscapes,' demand for 'hidden gems' like Chichibu grows mid-long-term — institutional long-term growth expectations gather. 3. SL unique tourism asset + regional-rail revival story — Paleo Express is one of Japan's premier SL excursion trains generating stable demand from railway fans + general tourists. Limited mid-cap tourism / rail recognition, but regional-rail revival story is being bought. FY27 profit-decline guidance may slow the upward move.
9023 Tokyo Metro (東京地下鉄) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.6% Op profit: ↑ +3.0% Net profit: ↑ +9.8% Stock: up from ¥1,200 IPO price (stable, % unverified)
分類: 都市地下鉄 / Urban subway (Tokyo Metro)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9023 + Tokyo Metro IR + nikkei 9023 + Tokyo Metro …
Company overview: Tokyo Metro (9023) monopolistically operates Tokyo's 9 subway lines (~195 route-km) — Ginza, Marunouchi, Hibiya, Tozai, Chiyoda, Yurakucho, Hanzomon, Namboku, Fukutoshin — the core urban-transit network supporting Tokyo's economic activity. Originally a special company under MLIT with the national government and Tokyo Metropolitan Government as shareholders, it IPO'd on TSE Prime on 2024-10-23 — one of the largest IPOs in Japanese history (~¥700B market cap at IPO, currently ~¥950B). Put simply: 'the synonym for central-Tokyo infrastructure — the operator of the 9 subway lines running exclusively under Tokyo.' How did business move? FY3/2026 is the first full year as a public company: Operating revenue ¥422.4B (+3.6%), OP ¥89.6B (+3.0%), NP ¥59.0B (+9.8%) — a healthy first-year-public showing. Dividend ¥42 (+¥2 YoY); FY3/2027 plans ¥44. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Inbound tourist recovery lifted transit revenue — Foreign visitor numbers near all-time highs; most Tokyo tourists ride the subway. The monopolistic network connecting Asakusa, Ginza, Shinjuku, Shibuya, Harajuku translates inbound demand directly into transit revenue. 2. Business-travel normalization (commuter / business-trip recovery) — Commuting and business-trip demand crushed during COVID has recovered, lifting commuter-pass and ticket revenue back to growth. 3. Station-commercial + line-side real-estate revenue contribution expansion — Facilities like Echika inside stations + real-estate revenue from developments around Otemachi, Kasumigaseki etc., grew. Non-rail revenue sources contributed to profit. Why did the stock move this way? Stock has risen meaningfully from the ¥1,200 IPO price and is valued as a stable high-dividend name by institutional investors. Three main reasons. 1. Tokyo urban-transit monopoly structural advantage — Operating rights on the 9 lines are exclusive to Tokyo Metro — new-entrant competition is effectively impossible. Urban-infrastructure businesses generate the stable revenue typical of regulated industries with extremely high long-term predictability — a textbook 'defensive economically-resilient' inclusion for institutional portfolios. 2. ESG fit + stable cash flow attracts long-horizon money — Electric rail is decarbonization-positive; long-horizon institutional money (pensions, life insurers) is attracted by the combination. 3. Long-term real-estate growth runway + clearer shareholder-return policy — Many stations and associated real estate in central Tokyo (Otemachi, Kasumigaseki, Yurakucho, Ginza) imply the real-estate business could grow to multiples of the subway business. Post-IPO 'stable dividend + step-up' policy also supports the stock.
9033 Hiroshima Electric Railway (広島電鉄) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +11.2% Op profit: ↑ up Net profit: ↓ -16.0% Stock: range-bound ~¥619-625 (direction unverified)
分類: 路面電車・バス / Tram & bus operator (Hiroshima Electric Railway)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9033 + Hiroshima Den-Tetsu IR + Hiroshima Den-Tets…
Company overview: Hiroshima Electric Railway (9033, Hiroden) is a mid-tier private railway founded 1942, headquartered in Hiroshima. Core: trams (8 lines + Hiroshima Port / Miyajimaguchi line, 35.1 km — Japan largest tram network), local + highway + charter buses, real estate (line-side commercial + condos), auto maintenance. The trams are nicknamed Hiroden by locals and used by tourists to access Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park, Atomic Bomb Dome, and Miyajima (Itsukushima Shrine). Put simply: the tram synonym connecting Hiroshima town, Peace Memorial Park, and Miyajima. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥37.47B (+11.2%), operating loss ¥-290M (narrowed). Double-digit revenue and a narrower OP loss, but parent-attributable net income was ¥1.158B (-16.0% YoY). H1 OP loss was ¥-475M (vs. prior ¥-588M) — operating loss is on a narrowing track. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the double-digit revenue / narrowed OP loss / lower NP pattern. 1. Hiroshima inbound recovery + full-year fare-revision effect — Tram and bus fares were revised effective February 1, 2025 (tram now flat ¥240 system-wide), contributing for the full year and improving transport economics. Visitor flow to Miyajima and Peace Memorial Park supported transit revenue. 2. Continued G7 Hiroshima Summit halo + stable real-estate revenue — The May 2023 G7 Summit raised Hiroshima global recognition, supporting inbound; real-estate rents cushioned earnings. 3. Heavy cost structure + higher expenses pressed NP — Tram operations carry heavy rail / vehicle maintenance + labor as fixed costs. Depreciation and expenses linked to the August 3, 2025 opening of the Ekimae-Ohashi tram route also weighed on net profit. Why did the stock move this way? Stock traded around ¥619-625 in late May 2026 (one-year directional trend UNVERIFIED). Possible drivers. 1. Double-digit revenue + narrower OP loss + inbound theme attention — The narrower operating loss can attract recovery-story interest, though NP decline is a drag. 2. Hiroshima status as a global tourist destination + G7 effect — Hiroshima has global recognition as a Peace Memorial City; inbound potential is large. 3. Tram network monopoly + Ekimae-Ohashi route opening mid-long-term effect — Hiroden exclusively operates Japan largest tram network; the Ekimae-Ohashi route opened August 2025 improves connectivity to JR Hiroshima Station, and the planned loop line could structurally expand transit revenue over time.
9035 Daiichi Kotsu Sangyo (第一交通産業) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +13.1% Op profit: ↑ +17.6% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up (clear uptrend, % unverified)
分類: タクシー大手・不動産 / Major taxi operator & real estate (Daiichi Kotsu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9035 + Daiichi Kotsu IR + Daiichi Kotsu Q3 disclos…
Company overview: Daiichi Kotsu Sangyo (9035) is Japan largest taxi operator (Kitakyushu / Fukuoka HQ; ~8,000 vehicles in around 34 prefectures nationwide) combined with real-estate development / sales / leasing — a taxi + real estate hybrid. Taxi business is community-rooted from Hokkaido to Okinawa; real estate runs condominium / housing-block development + commercial buildings in regional cities, cushioning taxi cycles. Put simply: Japan largest taxi king + regional real-estate developer. How did business move? FY3/2026 actual: Revenue ¥112.48B (+13.1%), OP ¥3.58B (+17.6%) — revenue beat the initial forecast (¥110B / +10.6%) while OP undershot the OP forecast (¥3.75B / +23.1%). Q3 YTD revenue ¥81.8B (+20.8%) and OP +338.1% show a sharp recovery from a low base. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue -2.7% (decline), OP +18.7% — continued profit-quality improvement. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Inbound tourist recovery driving taxi demand — Foreign visitors near record highs; airport-to-center + sightseeing taxi use rebounded above pre-COVID urban levels. 2. Multi-region fare hikes — biggest in ~20 years — MLIT-approved fare increases across 2025-2026 (Osaka +10.88% in November 2025, Nagoya in October, Fukuoka / Kitakyushu in July), structurally improving industry economics. 3. Rideshare policy strengthens incumbent positioning + driver supply progress — Japan rideshare framework is moving toward incumbents as operators; female-driver hiring + work-style reform are helping driver supply. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds an uptrend (precise magnitude UNVERIFIED). Three themes. 1. COVID-recovery + fare-revision + rideshare-policy three-theme attention — Institutional picks under all three themes; mid-cap with rising volume. 2. Largest 34-prefecture share + central consolidator — Nationwide network is hard to replicate; position as central buyer of small regional operators strengthens. 3. Japan-style rideshare central role + real-estate floor — Not US-Uber-style full liberalization but a Japan rideshare with incumbents as operators; Daiichi likely sits at the core. Real-estate cushions taxi cyclicality.
9046 Kobe Electric Railway (神戸電鉄) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.5% Op profit: ↑ +20.7% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: near 52-week low ~¥2,322-2,378 (down from highs, % unverified)
分類: 関西中堅私鉄 / Mid-tier Kansai private railway (Kobe Electric)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9046 + Kobe Den-Tetsu IR + Hankyu-Hanshin HD FY3/2…
Company overview: Kobe Electric Railway (9046) is a mid-tier Kansai private railway centered on Kobe / Sanda / Miki / Arima Onsen — a Hankyu-Hanshin Holdings subsidiary. Core: handles commuter demand to Kobe-Kita / Kobe-Nishi / Sanda New Town via rail. Plus Sanda New Town real-estate development, Shintetsu Bus, Tanigami transfer-terminal area commercial-facility operations. Sanda New Town + Arima Onsen tourism transport function as a 'regional private railway.' Put simply: 'a regional private rail in the Hankyu-Hanshin HD group connecting Kobe, Sanda, and Arima Onsen.' How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: Operating revenue ¥23.347B (+5.5%), OP ¥2.421B (+20.7%) — beat prior guidance (rev ¥22.77B / OP ¥2.23B). The 2024-April fare-revision full-year effect + new real-estate property contribution + Sanda New Town sales drove profit gains. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. April-2024 rail-fare revision flowed for the full year — Kobe Den-Tetsu raised bus + rail fares in April 2024; FY3/2026 is the first year the effect fully reflects. Fare revision structurally improves rail economics. 2. Strong Sanda New Town residential sales — Rising Kobe city housing prices made Sanda relatively cheap, driving demand. Real-estate revenue contribution was meaningful, lifting earnings. 3. Arima Onsen tourism recovery + stable line-side commercial rent — Kansai tourism demand recovered, lifting rail transport to Arima; tourism-related ancillary revenue grew. Plus Aeon / Co-op Kobe and other station-area commercial leasing is a long-term steady revenue source. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Upward guidance + OP +20.7% — Earnings-quality improvement is recognized by institutional investors; dividend yield is stable and long-term holders include the name. As a HHH subsidiary, independent volatility is limited but earnings momentum is solidly communicated. 2. Long-term Sanda New Town residential demand + HHH group synergies — Rising Kobe city housing prices + Osaka-center commutability sustain Sanda demand mid-long-term. Plus as an HHH-group company, it gains station-commercial-development know-how + real-estate-development know-how + fare-revision timing coordination. 3. Arima Onsen tourism monopoly + persistence of fare-revision effect — Kobe Den-Tetsu is the sole rail operator to Arima Onsen — monopolistic capture of tourism demand. Rising inbound visits to Kansai is a structural tailwind, and the fare-revision effect should keep contributing.
9082 Daiwa Auto Transport (大和自動車交通) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.6% Op profit: ↑ up Net profit: ↓ -62.1% Stock: up (clear uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 東京大手タクシー / Major Tokyo taxi operator (Daiwa Auto)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9082 + Shikiho 9082 + Daiwa Auto Transport Q1 disc…
Company overview: Daiwa Auto Transport (9082) is one of Tokyo's four major taxi companies — Chuo-ku-headquartered, founded 1939. Main operating area: Tokyo 23 wards + Mitaka / Musashino, with ~2,400 vehicles. Part of the 'Big Four' (Nihon Kotsu, Kokusai Jidosha, Teito Jidosha Kotsu, Daiwa Auto Transport). Beyond taxi: auto leasing + charter / sightseeing bus + auto maintenance + real estate (using former taxi-depot sites). Put simply: 'one of Tokyo's Big-Four taxi companies running through the city.' How did business move? FY3/2026 guidance: Revenue ¥20.1B (+5.6%), OP ¥320M (turning profitable vs. prior loss), recurring ¥1.7B, NP ¥500M (-62.1%) — Q1 actuals: revenue +7.2%, OP profitable vs. prior loss — V-shaped recovery from COVID is now clear. NP decline reflects comparison with prior-year one-off gains (policy-stock sales); core is much improved. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the sharp recovery. 1. Rapid inbound recovery + taxi demand expansion — Foreign visitor counts at record highs; Haneda / Narita-to-center transport + sightseeing taxi use surging. Daiwa as a Big-Four operator captures inbound demand directly. 2. First Tokyo taxi fare revision in 20+ years + driver supply progress — MLIT-approved fare hikes structurally improve industry economics. Plus restructured pay + work-style reform + female-driver recruitment expansion maintained / expanded fleet. 3. Rideshare-policy debate strengthens incumbent positioning — 'Japan-style rideshare' is moving toward incumbent operators as the operating entities — a structural tailwind. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. OP turn + earnings recovery + inbound-theme attention — Institutional money picks Daiwa under three themes: COVID recovery + structural reform + rideshare policy. Mid-cap but with rising volume / liquidity. 2. Tokyo Big-Four taxi oligopoly + strategic value of Haneda / Narita airport access — New entry effectively impossible; foreign-visitor main routes flow from Haneda / Narita to central Tokyo, with Daiwa's central-Tokyo fleet as a competitive advantage. 3. Central role in Japan-style rideshare + stable real-estate revenue base + EV / autonomous-driving readiness — Daiwa can enter the new rideshare-operating business; former-taxi-depot real estate concentrates in Tokyo capturing land-price appreciation; Daiwa leads in EV + autonomous pilots with scale advantage in tech transition.
9083 Shinki Bus (神姫バス) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.0% Op profit: ↑ +20.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up (clear uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 兵庫大手バス / Major Hyogo bus operator (Shinki Bus)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9083 + Shinki Bus IR + Osaka-Kansai Expo reports…
Company overview: Shinki Bus (9083) is a major Kansai bus operator headquartered in Himeji, Hyogo. Operates local + highway buses (Himeji-Osaka, Kobe-Hiroshima) + charter sightseeing + airport limousine (KIX, Itami, Kobe Airport) across Hyogo. Beyond bus: tourism (Shinki Tourism, group tours) + real estate (line-side commercial + condominiums) + auto maintenance + fuel sales. Put simply: 'regional infrastructure supporting Hyogo daily life and tourist transit.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥55.58B (+5.0%), OP ¥4.199B (+20.9%) — OP +20.9% came from three factors: the Osaka-Kansai Expo (started April 2025) demand + bus fare-revision + inbound tourism recovery. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the acceleration. 1. Osaka-Kansai Expo special demand — Held April 13 – October 13, 2025, the largest international event ever staged, flooding the Kansai region with visitors. Shinki captured huge demand for KIX-to-Yumeshima (Expo venue) + Kansai tourist-site transport, sharply expanding charter + airport-limousine revenue. 2. Bus fare-revision effect + Himeji Castle + Kobe tourism recovery — MLIT-approved fare hike improved economics. Kansai inbound demand fully back; Himeji Castle, Kobe foreign-resident houses, Harborland visits up. 3. Line-side real estate revenue contribution — Bus-route real estate steady; rent + sales support earnings. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Expo demand + earnings acceleration + 'Kansai + Expo + Inbound' theme attention — Among regional bus stocks, Shinki gets short-term attention; as long as momentum continues, PER expansion has runway. But post-Expo (after October 13, 2025) special-demand-rolloff risk exists. 2. Strategic value of Kansai dual-airport access + Hyogo inbound tourism potential — Shinki runs many limousine routes to KIX, Itami, Kobe Airport — tightly linked to Kansai aviation infrastructure. Himeji Castle (UNESCO), Kobe, Arima Onsen, Awaji Island — Hyogo inbound is a long-term growth source. 3. 2030 Osaka IR expectations + diversified portfolio + Hyogo bus monopoly — Osaka Yumeshima IR opens 2030 — mid-long-term Kansai tourism + airport-access expansion driver. Bus + tourism + real estate + auto-related diversification spreads cyclical risk; Hyogo bus monopoly anchors revenue.
▸ Logistics 2024-Problem rate-hike winners 11 names
Freight truckers, LTL carriers, parcel operators, and 3PL providers whose earnings and stock recovered sharply because the 2024 driver-hour regulations forced industrywide rate increases; EC-market growth and manufacturer 3PL-outsourcing also provide structural volume tailwinds.

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9029 + Higashi HD IR + Higashi HD Q3 disclosure…
Company overview: Higashi Holdings (formerly Higashi Twenty One, 9029) is a mid-tier 3PL logistics company headquartered in Chuo-ku, Osaka, founded 1944 from the merger of 13 transport companies in Osaka. Business portfolio combines EC large-format warehouse operations + 3PL / comprehensive logistics + general-affairs logistics (office moves, in-building delivery, PC kitting) + welfare-equipment rental and sales. In recent years, large-format DC operations for major EC operators have driven growth — about 17,000 tsubo of new EC-oriented warehouses have been opened in Kansai including the Kadoma Logistics Center (operational since 2020). Put simply: an Osaka-rooted mid-tier 3PL combining EC logistics, general-affairs logistics, and care-equipment rental. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥57.97B (+20.5%), recurring profit ¥4.15B (+41.4%) — rare double-digit growth in logistics. Large EC warehouse ramp-up + existing 3PL center volume increases lifted all segments — earnings momentum is clearly accelerating. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Direct beneficiary of EC market structural expansion — Domestic EC is structurally growing; Higashi large-DC operations for major EC operators capture demand directly. 2. Newly opened large-format DCs entering full operation — Newly opened large warehouses in Kansai (Kadoma area) entered full operation, translating capacity into revenue and profit (specific facility names/locations should be cross-checked with company IR). 3. 2024-Problem accelerated 3PL outsourcing + value-add / general-affairs logistics expansion — Maker in-house to 3PL outsource shift accelerated post-April 2024; established 3PLs like Higashi saw new-contract demand grow. Plus value-add work (processing, inventory) and general-affairs logistics improved mix and margins. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds an uptrend — hit YTD high ¥2,236 in Jan 2026 and traded near ¥1,972 in early June (per Yahoo Finance / kabutan). Three main reasons. 1. Revenue +20% / recurring +41% earnings acceleration + mid-year upward revision + year-end dividend increase — Institutional investors recognize Higashi as a mid-cap EC-logistics theme name. 2. Long-term EC market growth + new large-DC ramp-up as growth base — Multi-year investment burden is now translating into earnings, enabling multi-year profit growth. 3. Kansai 3PL + EC logistics positioning + 2024-Problem 3PL-outsourcing tailwind — Manufacturer in-house logistics difficulty structurally expands 3PL outsourcing demand, supporting mid-long-term contract opportunities. Note: the specific facility names GLP Takatsuki / GLP Kobe Nishinomiya / GLP Komaki in the original draft are UNVERIFIED against company IR.
