Company overview: Tobu Network (9036) is a Kanagawa-prefecture (Yokohama-area)-headquartered mid-tier trucking company focused on Greater Tokyo, combining chemical / industrial gas / cement / beverage trucking + warehousing + 3PL. Originated from the integration of 13 transport companies in eastern Yokohama; specializes in chemical / industrial-gas transport (including hydrogen). Specific major customer names UNVERIFIED. Put simply: a mid-cap trucker strong in Greater Tokyo chemical and industrial-gas transport. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥10.077B (-2.8%), OP ¥270M (+44.8%), recurring profit ¥370M (+48.2%), NP ¥300M (+184.4%) — a revenue down but profit sharply up pattern. H1 (interim) recurring was +38.4% YoY. Cleanup of low-margin contracts + efficiency + rate normalization drove the OP rebound — a textbook quality-improvement pivot. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Strategic cleanup of low-margin contracts — Deliberate exit from low-margin work to refocus resources on higher-margin work. Revenue down, but profit per resource much higher — structural shift. 2. Rate-revision pass-through + operational efficiency — 2024-Problem driver-hour rules backed rate hikes; route optimization + load factor materialized concurrently. 3. Hazmat transport value-add specialization — Chemical / industrial-gas transport requires strict safety management — high barrier; specialization-driven high-margin work lifted margin. Why did the stock move this way? Stock direction is UNVERIFIED. Potential drivers: 1. Authenticity of the quality pivot rating — Shrinking revenue while expanding profit signals active management-led quality growth; multi-year, builds a healthier base. 2. Chemical / industrial-gas transport barrier + Greater Tokyo logistics network strategic value — Hazmat safety + specialized vehicles + long-tenured customer trust block new entrants; proximity to chemical complexes (Keihin, Keiyo) is a strength. 3. 2024-Problem rate-revision effect + sustained structural earnings-quality improvement — Driver-hour-rule strengthening structurally improves industry economics; the structural earnings-quality improvement story could attract buys.
9037 Hamakyorex (ハマキョウレックス) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +6.0% Op profit: ↑ +11.7% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up gently (% unverified)
分類: 3PL専業中堅 / 3PL pure-play mid-tier (Hamakyorex)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Hamakyorex IR Q3 + minkabu 9037 + Hamakyorex Q3 disclosure…
Company overview: Hamakyorex (9037) is a mid-tier outstanding 3PL (third-party logistics) company headquartered in Hamamatsu, Shizuoka. Founder Masataka Osuga's one-generation-built growth company, recognized industry-wide as a '3PL-only established player.' Core: combination of large-format Distribution Centers (DCs) + own-truck dispatch and receipt, providing fulfillment 3PL nationwide to EC operators, food makers, pharma makers, mail-order businesses. Service design with own trucking network + DC-anchored operations differentiates from competitors. Put simply: 'a mid-tier outstanding 3PL pure-play that combines large warehouses with own trucks to take over shippers' entire logistics.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥155.5B (+6.0%), OP ¥14.76B (+11.7%) — mid-double-digit growth, stable for the 3PL industry. FY3/2027 plans record highs: Revenue ¥165.5B (+6.4%), OP ¥16.3B (+10.4%). Dividend ¥41.75 → ¥46 (+¥4.25 planned) — sustained step-up in shareholder return. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for steady growth. 1. 3PL market structural growth + Hamakyorex's 20-year track record — Domestic labor shortage + EC logistics expansion in parallel keep pushing shippers without own networks to outsource. Mid-tier but 20+ years of pure 3PL track record gives Hamakyorex an edge in competitive bids. 2. High DC utilization + higher-margin EC / food / pharma stacking — Large DCs built in recent years are running at high utilization; revenue leverages fixed costs. Plus temperature-controlled (frozen / refrigerated), pharma logistics (GDP), EC same-day delivery — higher-margin contracts grow as a share. 3. 2024-Problem rate revisions + DC-anchored service differentiation — DC-anchored trucking rates also rose, helping economics. Beyond pure transport, integrated DC-based inventory management + value-add processing + packing + shipping raise customer value. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Stable earnings growth + record-high plans + step-up dividend — Institutional money picks Hamakyorex as a 'mid-cap 3PL pure-play.' As long as momentum continues, the uptrend persists. 2. 20+ year 3PL track record barrier + large-DC strategic value — Industry's combination of credentials + trust + large-DC ownership stops new entrants. In EC logistics, large-DC owners have overwhelming advantage; Hamakyorex's early investments secured large DCs and capture EC growth. 3. Founder-led continuity + decision speed — Osuga's leadership produces rapid, agile decisions vs. larger competitors, recognized as a differentiator with advantage in decision speed vs. major logistics peers.

Company overview: Senkon Logistics (9051) is a mid-tier 3PL logistics company headquartered in Iwanuma, Miyagi, founded 1962. Tohoku-based with sites in Kanto, Chubu, Kansai for wide-area network. Core: 3PL distribution-center operations + trucking + warehouse + moving + passenger-vehicle sales + quarrying, plus specialty transport for electronic components / precision equipment. Put simply: a Tohoku-based mid-tier logistics conglomerate combining transport, warehousing, passenger-vehicle sales, and other businesses (specific major shipper names UNVERIFIED). How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 cumulative: Revenue ¥14.725B (+5.7%), OP ¥512M (-19.9%), recurring ¥495M (-25.1%). Management upwardly revised full-year recurring profit from ¥490M to ¥580M (+18.4%) on Feb 12, 2026, narrowing the YoY decline from -47.6% to -38.0%. In Q4 (Jan-Mar) alone, consolidated recurring profit jumped to ¥270M (+2.6x YoY) and OPM improved from 1.9% to 3.5% — clear profit recovery in the final quarter. Revenue growth + recent profit recovery now run in parallel. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the H1 weakness to H2 recovery. 1. Strength in trucking and passenger-vehicle sales — Rate-revision adoption + steady auto sales lifted profit. 2. Easing cost pressure in warehouse and quarrying — H1 saw cost burden in warehouse / quarrying compress profit; Q4 likely saw improvement. 3. Gradual adoption of 2024-Problem rate revisions — Rate pass-through advanced over the year, margin improvement crystallized in Q4. Note: claim of Toshiba Device & Storage / Renesas / Kyocera as principal shippers could not be sourced from disclosures — UNVERIFIED. Why did the stock move this way? Stock range: YTD low ¥891 (Jan), high ¥1,739 (Aug), ~¥1,260 around April 21, 2026 — significant volatility. Three main reasons. 1. Upward revision + Q4 profit-recovery being priced in — After H1-weakness selloff, the upward revision and Q4 recovery triggered buybacks. 2. Tohoku logistics network + semiconductor-cluster theme (TSMC Kumamoto, Rapidus Hokkaido) — Thematic positioning attracts attention. 3. Dividend-maintenance policy — Maintaining dividends even in soft periods supports the downside.

Company overview: Nippon Logitem (9060) is a Tokyo-headquartered mid-tier 3PL logistics company — its predecessor Osaki Transport was established in October 1944 and renamed Nippon Logitem in 1989. Comprehensive logistics operator: warehouse + distribution center + trucking + moving services + real estate, with Asia-region operations (Taiwan, Hong Kong, Thailand, Vietnam, China, Laos, Myanmar, Cambodia) on top of domestic. Core: distribution-center operations + delivery for food and daily-goods makers, plus asset business (owned warehouse real estate) generating stable revenue. Put simply: a mid-tier logistics operator that owns its warehouses while running 3PL, with Asia presence. (Original draft 1979 partnership with US GAO could not be confirmed in official corporate history and has been removed as UNVERIFIED.) How did business move? FY3/2026: Operating revenue ¥71.317B (+8.1%), OP ¥1.437B (+16.9%) — revenue + profit growth with profit pace outrunning revenue pace = margin expansion. Distribution-center expansion + asset-utilization improvement + fare-hike adoption all contributed simultaneously. Note: FY3/2027 is guided to lower profit due to new-site launch costs — heads-up on a potential headwind quarter. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Distribution-center contract stacking — Food / daily-goods makers added new center contracts steadily, leveraging revenue on fixed costs. 2. Asset business utilization improvement — Tight logistics-real-estate market keeps utilization high; rate revisions also progressing. 3. 2024-Problem rate revisions + disciplined cost management — DC-anchored trucking rates rose, improving economics. Plus route efficiency + IT investment + labor-cost discipline simultaneously delivered margin gains. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Stable growth + margin expansion + stable dividend — Limited mid-cap recognition, but institutional money positions Nippon Logitem as a 3PL pure-play mid-cap grower. 2. Stable asset-business cashflows + recession-resilient food / daily-goods 3PL — Rental income from owned warehouses is a stable revenue base; food / daily-goods 3PL contracts last for years. 3. 3PL pure-play barrier + 2024-Problem outsourcing demand expansion — Integrated DC operations + delivery require system integration + operations know-how + long-tenured customer trust — hard for new entrants. Caution: FY3/2027 is guided to a profit decline due to new-site launch costs.

分類: 京浜総合物流 / Keihin comprehensive logistics (Marzen Showa)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Marzen Showa IR + logmi finance article + Marzen Showa Q2 …
Company overview: Marzen Showa Unyu (9068) is a Yokohama-headquartered, 1931-founded long-established comprehensive logistics company. Provides 3PL (center operations) + on-site logistics (logistics inside customer factories) + ocean container shipping + freight forwarding + warehousing to diverse shippers — chemicals, steel, auto parts, food, pharmaceuticals, electrical machinery. Strong in chemical / steel on-site logistics around the Keihin industrial belt; recently expanding into food / pharma temperature-controlled warehouses and EC logistics. Put simply: 'an established comprehensive-logistics provider supporting the Keihin chemical and steel complexes.' How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥148.6B (+2.8%), OP ¥15.46B (+5.6%), NP ¥12.685B — solid growth. Dividend lifted ¥40 to ¥210 — a meaningful raise. FY3/2027 plans record highs: Revenue ¥162B (+9%), OP ¥17B (+10%) — multi-year growth set to continue. Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Continuing M&A-driven scale expansion effect — Multiple logistics subsidiaries acquired over the past few years are now contributing earnings in consolidation. On top of organic growth, M&A synergies are flowing with a time lag. 2. Stacking new contracts + diversified customer base spreading risk — New 3PL / center-operations wins from chemical and food makers; the 2024 Problem accelerated 'in-house → outsourced' shifts. Diversification across chemicals / steel / autos / food / pharma makes earnings less sensitive to any single industry cycle. 3. High warehouse utilization — Tight logistics-real-estate market keeps utilization high on owned warehouses, leveraging revenue on fixed costs. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Big dividend hike (¥170 → ¥210) + upward guidance + FY27 record-high plan — Within mid-cap logistics, the combination of stable growth + stronger shareholder returns is rare, lifting institutional view of Marzen Showa as a 'quality grower.' 2. Dominant Keihin industrial-belt share + strategic food / pharma expansion — Long-held share in on-site logistics at the Kawasaki / Yokohama / Yokosuka chemical complexes + steelworks creates a hard-to-replicate barrier. Investment in higher-margin temperature-controlled + GDP-compliant pharma logistics shifts the mix toward higher margins. 3. Continued M&A execution + warehouse-real-estate hidden value + 2024-Problem outsourcing demand expansion — Active buyer-side player in industry consolidation; Keihin warehouse hidden value expanding; 'manufacturer in-house → 3PL outsource' shift sustains a mid-long-term revenue uptrend.

Company overview: Kyogoku Transport (9073) is a Tokyo-Koto-ku-headquartered ENEOS-group mid-tier company founded 1948, combining trucking with drum-can manufacturing. Five segments: domestic transport (chemical / petroleum trucking) + international logistics + drum / pail can (manufacturing, sales, washing, recycling) + energy + tank washing. The ability to supply containers AND transport for chemicals / petroleum is the unique edge. Put simply: an ENEOS-group operator that ships chemicals while also making, washing, and recycling drum cans. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥8.702B (+3.3%), OP ¥164M (+867.8%) — major jump from a tiny prior base. Domestic-transport price revisions + strong drum / pail can business drove the gain; equity ratio improved to 52.2%. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Base effect + rate revisions — The +867.8% growth rate reflects a low prior-year base, but the absolute ¥164M is also a real improvement. 2024-Problem driver-hour rules backed rate hikes, lifting trucking margins. 2. Strong drum / pail can business — Chemical / petroleum volumes + recycling demand sustained drum-can revenue. 3. Disciplined cost management — Fuel surcharge + route efficiency contributed (semiconductor / battery-material exposure UNVERIFIED). Why did the stock move this way? The stock showed elevated volatility in 2025-2026 with a notable July 2025 spike linked to logistics-infrastructure re-rating themes (longer-term direction UNVERIFIED). Three potential drivers. 1. OP +867% sharp gain + escape from base effect — Small-cap, low recognition, but earnings surprises move the stock. 2. Trucking + drum-can synergy + circular-economy flow — Rare one-stop chemical-transport + drum-can supply; SDGs / circular economy expands recycling / reuse demand. 3. ENEOS group ties + small-cap re-rating room — ENEOS affiliation gives a stable customer base in petroleum / chemical transport; thin analyst coverage means earnings stability could unlock re-rating.
9075 Fukuyama Transport (福山通運) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.3% Op profit: ↑ +26.9% Net profit: ↑ +56.6% Stock: up (uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 特積み大手 / Major LTL freight (Fukuyama Transport)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + lnews 9075 + Fukuyama Transport IR + Fukuyama HD FY3/2026 …
Company overview: Fukuyama Transport (9075) is an established LTL (less-than-truckload, mixed-cargo) trucker headquartered in Fukuyama, Hiroshima. Founded 1948 — the Fukutsu brand parcel / LTL operator spans Kanto / Chubu / Kansai / Chu-Shikoku / Kyushu nationwide. Beyond pure domestic freight: charter (full-vehicle) + distribution-processing (warehouse / DC operations) + international logistics (ocean / air forwarding, SE Asia + China) + real-estate rental — a comprehensive logistics operator. Put simply: Fukutsu — Western Japan LTL major connecting Hiroshima to nationwide. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥318.58B (+5.3%), OP ¥9.347B (+26.9%), recurring ¥11.48B (+15.7%), NP ¥13.70B (+56.6%) — all segments grew. Segment detail: trucking ¥244.57B (+4.3%, OP +31.3%), charter ¥27.81B (+5.9%, OP +16.8%), distribution-processing ¥23.7B (+6.0%, OP +16.0%), international ¥15.24B (+28.5%, OP +55.4%). Dividend stepped up from ¥70 to ¥76 per share. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the sharp earnings gain. 1. 2024-Problem rate-revisions paid off — Alongside Yamato, Sagawa, and Seino, Fukuyama actively pushed shipper rate increases. Trucking segment OP +31.3% shows the rate-hike effect in force. 2. International-logistics segment surge — Revenue +28.5%, OP +55.4% combines SE Asia + China logistics demand growth and forwarding wins. 3. Double-digit OP growth in distribution-processing + charter — Distribution-processing OP +16.0%, charter OP +16.8%; rate revisions + operational efficiency + price normalization rippled across segments. NP +56.6% reflects operating-level gain (+26.9%) plus uplift in below-operating items; the specific composition of any special gains (policy-stock sales / real-estate gains) is UNVERIFIED from public disclosures. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds an uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Strong earnings beat across all metrics — Recognized as a central LTL beneficiary of the industry structural improvement (2024-Problem rate cycle). Dividend trajectory ¥70 to ¥76 to FY27 guidance ¥100 supports step-up returns (though yield ~1.5% means income appeal is limited). 2. Hiroshima / Chu-Shikoku geographic advantage + international-logistics growth runway — Network centered on Hiroshima and Fukuyama is critical for Kansai-Kyushu transit. SE Asia + China international logistics is the structural growth area with high growth rates, potentially a major earnings pillar long-term. 3. Sustained LTL rate-hike effect + EC-logistics adoption progress — 2024-Problem revisions structurally improve industry economics. The company is positioned to capture EC growth.

Company overview: Seino Holdings (9076) is one of Japan largest LTL (less-than-truckload) freight companies, anchored by the Kangaroo-Mark Kangaroo Bin brand. Founded 1930 in Ogaki, Gifu; nationwide network. Beyond pure BtoB freight: automotive sales (Honda dealership) + EC last-mile (Rakuten / Yahoo / Amazon) + 3PL (pharma / food temperature-controlled) + air-freight forwarding. Put simply: one of Japan largest LTL operators, known by the Kangaroo Mark. How did business move? FY3/2026 guidance: Revenue ¥813.7B (+10.4%), OP ¥37.6B (+25.8%), recurring ¥38.3B (+36.2%), NP ¥22.0B (+14.3%). Q3 YTD recurring +27.5% to ¥31.8B — tracking record highs. Dividend yield ~4-4.5%. FY3/2026 full-year dividend looks set to rise to ¥104 (¥43 interim + ¥61 final) from FY3/2025 ¥102. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Early positioning ahead of the 2024 Problem — April 2024 work-style reform tightened driver-hour limits (960-hour annual cap); industry capacity tightened. Seino had negotiated rate hikes for years; 2025-2026 saw those settle through. 2. 3PL expansion + new consolidated subsidiary contribution — Pharma / food temperature-controlled, EC last-mile higher-margin 3PL contracts stacked up; M&A-acquired logistics companies added to consolidation. 3. Completed fuel-cost pass-through — Diesel surcharges to shippers improved margins. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds an uptrend (exact recent spot UNVERIFIED). Three drivers. 1. Record-high earnings + upward guidance + freight-rate / labor-secure theme — Institutional view lifted; payout growth attracts long-horizon money. 2. 2024-Problem-anticipating pricing power + diversified portfolio — Industry rate-negotiation leader alongside Yamato and Sagawa. Auto sales + EC + 3PL + air freight diversification stabilizes earnings. 3. Gifu / Ogaki geographic advantage + 4%+ dividend yield — Central-Japan Tomei-Meishin highway nexus + proximity to Toyota / Honda / Mitsubishi Heavy industrial clusters supports stable BtoB demand. 4%+ yield is attractive for a mid-cap logistics name, drawing steady dividend-mandate demand.
9087 Takase (タカセ) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +2.0% (Q3 YTD) Op profit: ↑ +120.3% (Q3 YTD) Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up gently (% unverified)
分類: 国際・国内ハイブリッド物流 / International & domestic hybrid logistics (Takase)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9087 + kabutan 9087 + Takase Q3 disclosure…
Company overview: Takase (9087) is a Tokyo-Chuo-ku-headquartered mid-tier logistics company founded 1954. Combines international logistics (ocean / air forwarding, customs clearance) + domestic logistics (warehousing, trucking, 3PL) + moving — an 'international + domestic hybrid logistics' operator. Since founding, strong in import / export cargo customs / forwarding for textiles, apparel, sundries, electrical machinery. Recently strengthened domestic 3PL + entered EC logistics. Put simply: 'a logistics company with 70+ years of customs know-how that delivers from overseas procurement to domestic distribution as a one-stop service.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Operating revenue ¥6.321B (+2.0%), OP ¥162M (+120.3%). Full-year guidance: Revenue ¥8.6B (+3.1%), OP ¥200M (+153.2%) — OP margin improving 1.2% → 2.6%. Small-cap with concurrent margin expansion + earnings recovery in progress. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the sharp OP gain. 1. Base effect — prior OP was low + international-logistics profit recovery — The +120% / +153% growth rates reflect a small prior base, but international segment, previously squeezed by ocean spot-rate declines, recovered; forwarding revenue improved. 2. Domestic 3PL expansion + 2024-Problem rate-revision effect — EC + food 3PL contract wins are starting to function as a stable revenue base; domestic trucking rate revisions improved economics. 3. Disciplined cost management — Operational efficiency + labor-cost discipline + IT investment in parallel delivered margin gains. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. OP +153% sharp gain + clear margin improvement — Limited small-cap recognition, but as the earnings-improvement trend continues, re-rating runway exists. Dividend-policy review could accelerate the rally. 2. Accumulated international-logistics know-how + international + domestic hybrid structure — 70+ years of international-logistics (customs + forwarding) know-how is a hard-to-replicate barrier. Combining international logistics with domestic 3PL offers customers 'overseas procurement → domestic delivery' one-stop service — a differentiator. 3. EC-logistics entry + re-rating runway as a small-cap — EC logistics market expansion provides a mid-long-term growth opportunity; combining overseas-procurement customs + domestic EC delivery captures cross-border EC tailwinds. Thin analyst coverage gives sizable upside as earnings stabilize.

Revenue: ↑ +11.2% Op profit: ↑ +2.7% Net profit: ↑ +1.6% Stock: down softly (~¥1,437, % unverified)

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9143 + SG HD IR + moomoo 9143 + SG HD Q3 disclosur…
Company overview: SG Holdings (9143) is Japan's #2 parcel / logistics company, anchored by Sagawa Express. Kyoto-headquartered, the 'Hikyaku Express' parcel brand is core; also operates BtoB freight + 3PL + real estate + international logistics (SGH Global Japan, Sagawa Global Logistics) + TMS services — a 'comprehensive logistics conglomerate.' Originally light-freight 'Hikyaku' delivery; today a massive operator handling 1.3+ billion parcels annually. Put simply: 'a logistics conglomerate housing Sagawa Express — the other side of parcel delivery alongside Yamato.' How did business move? FY3/2026 actual (announced May 8, 2026): Revenue ¥1,644.762B (+11.2%), OP ¥90.247B (+2.7%), recurring ¥91.782B (+3.3%), NP ¥59.066B (+1.6%) — all up (sources: SG HD short report, lnews s0508705). Dividend ¥52 → ¥53 (+¥1). Feb 6, 2026 Q3 trimmed recurring guidance ¥90B → ¥89B (-1.1%); Q3 YTD recurring -5.3%, Q3 standalone -9.0%, OP margin 9.1% → 7.6%. FY3/2027 plan: Revenue ¥1.74T, OP ¥97B. Stock ¥1,437 (May 11, 2026, -2.58%). Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the 'revenue-up / profit-pressured' pattern. 1. 2024-Problem driver wage increases — Industry-wide hourly wages up; SG's labor costs rose. Rate hikes partially offset, but not fully matching wage pace. 2. Rising outsourcing costs + deteriorating international logistics market — SG outsources volumes beyond own-fleet capacity to subcontractors; their cost increases pressured profit. Plus SGH Global Japan international business saw ocean spot rates fall + FX squeeze profit. 3. EC platform price competition — Amazon, Rakuten, Yahoo demanding lower unit prices continues to compress parcel-unit margins. Why did the stock move this way? Stock trades modestly below revenue / guidance-implied levels. Three main reasons. 1. Profit weakness + industry structural challenges priced in — Modest dividend yield; institutional view: 'cautious — pricing earnings downside risk while still valuing the long-term logistics-restructuring central role.' 2. Parcel-market oligopoly position with Yamato + 2024-Problem rate-hike runway — Yamato + Sagawa hold ~70% domestic parcel share — effectively impossible for new entrants. Mid-long-term margin recovery is possible via continued rate revisions. 3. Long-term EC logistics growth + international logistics expansion runway + stable real-estate revenue — Domestic EC growth ~10% annually keeps parcel-volume secular growth intact; SGH Global expanding networks in SE Asia / China / India; nationwide logistics hubs + former-office real estate generate stable rent + sale revenue with hidden value expansion.
▸ Station / urban redevelopment story 3 names
Private railways whose dominant investor thesis is a major station-area or urban real-estate redevelopment — either as a long-term re-rating catalyst (Shinjuku Southwest, Shinagawa/Takanawa, Umekita, Fukuoka Tenjin) or as a near-term overhang when the project is delayed or shelved (Shibuya 7-year delay, Nagoya station effective shelving).

Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Nikkei 9031 + lnews 9031 + logmi 9031…
Company overview: Nishi-Nippon Railroad (Nishitetsu, 9031) is a Fukuoka/Kyushu-based private rail + diversified company headquartered in Fukuoka, founded 1908. Core: Nishitetsu Tenjin Omuta Line + local-bus, but the larger profit engines are international logistics (Nishitetsu Transport = air + sea forwarding), Tenjin-area redevelopment (One Fukuoka Building opening), hotels (Nishitetsu Hotel + Solaria Nishitetsu), Nishitetsu Store + Iwataya, and the agricultural-materials business added via Hinomaru HD subsidiarization (decided H2-2025). How did business move? FY3/2026 saw recurring profit upward-revised from ¥27.6B to ¥34.3B (+24.3% revision, +19.4% YoY) — second consecutive record. NP ¥25B (+20%, record, ¥3.8B above prior guidance). Revenue grew YoY (precise figure UNVERIFIED in our checks). Dividend lifted ¥40→¥50 (+¥10 major hike). Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1) Logistics strength — international (air + sea) forwarding market recovery + rate revisions + new contract wins lifted the segment profit substantially (exact +61% magnitude UNVERIFIED in primary tanshin). 2) Real estate + hotels + Hinomaru HD subsidiarization — condo sales expansion + inbound-driven hotel demand (ARPU / occupancy specifics UNVERIFIED), plus H2-2025 Hinomaru HD (agricultural materials) consolidation added a new revenue stream. 3) Fukuoka/Kyushu structural growth — TSMC Kumamoto-led semiconductor investment energizing the local economy + Tenjin Big Bang redevelopment + One Fukuoka Building opening underpinned earnings. Why did the stock move this way? After a 10-year low of ¥1,926 in April 2025, the stock recovered to ¥3,053 by March 2026 then eased to ~¥2,741 by May 2026 — uptrend on a 12-month view but in a near-term pause. Three drivers: 1) 2 consecutive record earnings + ¥40→¥50 dividend hike (+¥10) attracted both institutional and retail buying; 2) TSMC-Kumamoto-led Kyushu semiconductor build-out supports long-term line-side / property value (note: Rapidus is in Hokkaido and not directly tied to Nishitetsu network); 3) logistics-segment earnings recovery re-rated the business mix, while Tenjin Big Bang + One Fukuoka Building opening underpin the real-estate long-term story.
9008 Keio Corporation ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↓ -2.9% (Q3 YTD) Net profit: ↑ up Stock: up sharply (stock split + buyback + dividend hike, % unverified)
分類: 関東大手私鉄 / Major Kanto private railway (Keio)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9008 + Nikkei 9008 + diamond ZAi 9008…
Company overview: Keio Corporation (9008) is a major Kanto private rail + comprehensive urban-development company headquartered in Tama, Tokyo, founded 1948. Core: Keio Line (Shinjuku-Hachioji-Takaosanguchi) + Inokashira Line (Shibuya-Kichijoji), with limited-express Keio Liner. Plus a central role in Shinjuku Station Southwest Exit redevelopment, Hachioji / Tama / Chofu line-side residential, Keio Department Store Shinjuku flagship, Keio Plaza Hotel Shinjuku, Yomiuriland and Mt. Takao tourism, and Keio Bus. Put simply: a private rail connecting Shinjuku town and the Tama area, anchoring Shinjuku-front redevelopment. How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥360.163B (+7.6%) up, OP ¥48.118B (-2.9%) down. Full-year FY3/2026 actuals: Revenue ¥496.9B, OP ¥52.3B, NP ¥42.9B. FY3/2027 guide: Revenue ¥502B (+10.8%), OP ¥51B (-5.8%), NP ¥42B (-2.0%). Stock rose sharply on major shareholder-return package (dividend ¥100 to ¥110 pre-split + buyback + 1-for-5 stock split effective April 1, 2026). Why did business move this way? Three reasons for the sales-up / profit-down pattern. 1. RE sales + hotels led revenue — Line-side condo sales + inbound recovery (Keio Plaza Hotel Shinjuku, etc.) appear to have lifted revenue (segment-level detail UNVERIFIED). 2. Cost increases lowered OP — Fuel + labor + depreciation lifted costs — a structural factor. 3. Shinjuku Station Southwest Exit redevelopment progress — Keio-anchored Southwest Exit redevelopment (south block 37 floors / north block 19 floors) is in the long-term growth-base build phase. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a clear uptrend. Three reasons. 1. Major shareholder-return package (dividend + buyback + stock split) — Simultaneous announcement of dividend ¥100 to ¥110 (+¥10) + ¥15B / 5.6M-share buyback + 1-for-5 stock split was the biggest trigger. The split was especially welcomed (retail accessibility + liquidity). 2. PBR ~1.0 value level + TSE PBR-improvement-request response — Keio PBR around 1.0 needed response; the shareholder-return package was highly valued. 3. Shinjuku Station Southwest Exit redevelopment next-100-years growth expectations — Keio anchoring the redevelopment captures long-term growth of one of central Tokyo most valuable real-estate areas; institutional long-term expectations gather (specific broker target-price hikes UNVERIFIED).
9006 Keikyu Corporation ▸
Revenue: ↑ +3.5% Op profit: ↓ -5.9% Net profit: ↑ +13.1% Stock: up (major dividend hike ¥26→¥46, uptrend, % unverified)
分類: 関東大手私鉄(羽田アクセス) / Major Kanto railway, Haneda access (Keikyu)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9006 + minkabu 9006…
Company overview: Keikyu Corporation (9006) is a major Kanto private rail + comprehensive urban-development company headquartered in Tokyo-Minato, founded 1898. Core: Keikyu Main Line (Shinagawa-Yokohama-Yokosuka-Miura Peninsula) + Airport Line (Haneda Airport access, with limited-express Airport Kaitoku). Plus Shinagawa + Yokohama Station-front redevelopment, Keikyu Department Store, Keikyu EX Hotel, Miura Peninsula leisure, Keikyu Bus. Keikyu plays a key role in Haneda Airport access for both inbound tourists and business travelers. Put simply: a private rail connecting Haneda to central Tokyo and Miura Peninsula, anchoring Shinagawa-front redevelopment. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥304.192B (+3.5%) up, OP ¥33.553B (-5.9%) down. Special gains lifted NP to ¥27.492B (+13.1%). In November 2025 NP forecast was sharply upward-revised, and the FY3/2026 dividend was raised from prior-year ¥26 to ¥46 (+¥20, +77%). FY27 guides revenue + profit growth on real-estate expansion. Why did business move this way? Three reasons. 1. Haneda Airport access demand + fare-revision full-year effect — Inbound recovery drove Haneda-to-Shinagawa / Tokyo center demand; Keikyu Airport Line lifted transit revenue. April-2024 fare-revision full-year effect added. 2. Cost increases lowered OP — Fuel + labor + depreciation reduced OP -5.9% — structural. 3. Special gains lifted NP + FY27 real-estate growth outlook — Real-estate-sale special gains lifted NP +13.1%; FY27 will see significant growth from real-estate expansion. Why did the stock move this way? Stock appears in uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. Major dividend hike (¥26 to ¥46, +¥20) + earnings upward revision — The aggressive shareholder-return move (dividend lifted in two steps to ¥46) responding to PBR-improvement requests was the biggest catalyst. 2. Haneda Airport access advantage — Keikyu through-running access to central Tokyo + surging foreign visitors + Haneda international-route expansion is a structural tailwind. 3. Shinagawa / Takanawa 3-chome urban development + Linear Shinagawa Station opening — Toyota / Keikyu Takanawa 3-chome joint development (FY2029 target completion) + future Linear Chuo Shinkansen Shinagawa Station + Shinagawa-area redevelopment are recognized as long-term real-estate growth bases.
▸ Auto & specialty logistics specialists 3 names
Companies whose business is built around finished-vehicle transport, auto-parts logistics, or moving-industry consolidation — benefiting from automotive production recovery after the chip shortage, 2024-Problem rate hikes in car-carrier pricing, and growing market share through M&A-driven industry consolidation.
9072 NIKKON Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ +8.9% Op profit: ↑ +2.9% Net profit: ↑ +10.2% Stock: up substantially over 1-2 years (% unverified)
分類: 自動車物流 / Auto-logistics specialist (NIKKON)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9072 + Nikkon IR + finance.logmi 9072…
Company overview: NIKKON Holdings (Nihon Konpou Unyu Soko, 9072) is an auto-related + comprehensive logistics company headquartered in Tokyo-Chuo (Akashi-cho), founded 1948. Integrates transport (finished-vehicle + auto-parts transport for Honda Motor, via trucks + RoRo vessels) + packaging (auto-parts / industrial-machinery export packaging, high-margin segment) + warehousing (storage / handling at major domestic sites, new warehouses recently operating) + international logistics (ocean / air forwarding) — an auto-logistics specialist. Honda is the dominant customer — a Honda-anchor company. Put simply: a dedicated carrier + warehouse operator supporting Honda global production and sales. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥269.862B (+8.9%), OP ¥23.818B (+2.9%), recurring ¥24.853B (+3.7%), NP ¥18.237B (+10.2%) — all segments grew. Stock returns up substantially over 1-2 years (point-in-time exact percentages UNVERIFIED-magnitude as of current date). Why did business move this way? 1. New-warehouse-driven storage volume increase — New warehouses built 2-3 years ago entered full operation; storage volume + profit expanded. Fixed-cost leverage structure. 2. Strong transport + packaging (Honda-dependence strength) — Honda finished-vehicle + auto-parts transport stable; export packaging is high-margin. 3. 2024-Problem rate revisions + 14th mid-term plan launch prep — Trucking rate revisions permeated; the 14th mid-term plan starts FY3/2027 (initial-year guidance Revenue ¥285B, OP ¥26.7B), signaling continued growth path. Why did the stock move this way? Stock in strong uptrend (precise magnitudes are point-in-time and tagged UNVERIFIED-magnitude). Three main reasons. 1. Honda-anchor stable revenue base — Long-tenured Honda relationship boosts predictability; long-horizon institutional money concentrates. 2. Warehouse fixed-cost-leverage structural margin expansion — Utilization-driven leverage is recognized by institutions. 3. Step-up dividend + return strengthening — 14th mid-term plan signals dividend uplift; dividend-mandate long-horizon demand is steady.
9028 Zero (ゼロ) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +5.0% Op profit: ↑ +64.4% Net profit: ↑ +73.0% Stock: +77.5% YoY (Bloomberg, as of May 2025)
分類: 完成車輸送 / Finished-vehicle logistics specialist (Zero)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9028 + kabutan 9028 + scouter 9028 + Zero IR FY6/2…
Company overview: Zero (9028) is a finished-vehicle logistics specialist — transporting new cars from factories to dealers / ports — for the major domestic OEMs (Nissan, Mitsubishi, Suzuki, Mazda, Subaru). Founded 1961 as Nissan Riku-so, became independent via MBO from Nissan in 2001. Currently ~51% owned by ZENITH LOGISTICS PTE. LTD. (Singapore, Logisteed / former Hitachi Transport group) — Zero is effectively a majority-owned subsidiary of the Logisteed-affiliated entity. Core: new-car carrier trailer transport + marine transport (owned RoRo vessels) + port handling + used-car-auction logistics. June fiscal year (unusual vs. other LT names). How did business move? FY6/2025 actuals: Revenue ¥147.843B (+5.0%), OP ¥10.228B (+64.4%), NP ¥7.179B (+73.0%) — record-high. FY6/2026 guidance: Revenue ¥145B, OP ¥10.3B — roughly flat. However H1 FY6/2026 (Jul-Dec 2025) actuals were DOWN: revenue 95.5% YoY, OP 89.6% YoY on weak new-car sales and underperformance from commissioned manufacturers. Payout ratio raised 25% to 33% from FY6/2025. Dividend ¥138.4 to ¥139.9 to ¥140.3 (the FY6/2026 ¥140.3 is UNVERIFIED-precision in this round). Why did business move this way? Through FY6/2025, three reasons. 1. Domestic vehicle production recovery + transport demand expansion — Chip shortage resolved, OEM production recovered, completed-vehicle transport demand expanded. Zero has many long-term OEM contracts. 2. 2024-Problem rate revisions — Tightened carrier-driver hour limits tightened industry capacity; Zero secured rate increases. 3. Higher-margin used-car-auction logistics + stable marine (RoRo) business — Japan used-car market expansion + offshore exports (Russia, SE Asia, Africa) lifted auction-logistics volume; owned RoRo vessels are stable revenue. The H1 FY6/2026 slowdown reflects soft new-car sales and weak commissioned-OEM production — Zero is highly sensitive to external auto-industry conditions. Why did the stock move this way? Stock is in a medium-term uptrend (Bloomberg measured ~+77.5% 1-year total return as of May 2025, point-in-time and dated). Three main reasons. 1. FY6/2025 record-high results + OP margin improvement + sustained dividend hikes + payout-ratio uplift (25% to 33%) — Niche vehicle-logistics monopoly draws institutional quality-growth mid-cap logistics inclusion. 2. Vehicle-logistics entry barrier + EV-shift readiness — Specialized carriers, trained drivers, and long-tenured OEM relationships block new entrants; EV-dedicated carriers + charging infrastructure + driver training position Zero to treat the EV shift as opportunity. 3. June fiscal-year uniqueness + ~4% dividend yield — Diversifies dividend-portfolio timing vs. March-end peers; ~4% yield supports the stable-income long-horizon base. However, the H1 FY6/2026 revenue / profit decline is a caution flag for further upside.

Company overview: Being Holdings (9145) is the holding company of the Being Group — a comprehensive logistics group rooted in 'Kanto Land Transport' — operating nationwide trucking + SCM (supply chain management) + warehousing + on-site logistics. Auto-related logistics (finished-vehicle transport, parts) is the traditional business area; specific auto-customer relationships (Toyota / Nissan / Honda / Subaru) are UNVERIFIED in our search scope. Plus diversification into pharma / food / electrical machinery / industrial equipment 3PL. How did business move? CRITICAL: Being HD has a December fiscal year-end (January-December cycle, NOT March). FY12/2024 actual: Revenue ¥30.19B (+14.6%), OP ¥2.243B (+24.8%), NP +5% — record highs (source: LNEWS 2025/2/14). FY12/2025 Q1 (Jan-Mar 2025): Revenue ¥8.355B (+10.6%), OP ¥388M (-28.8%) — Tokai SCM Center relocation impact. H1 (Jan-Jun) recovered: Revenue ¥15.82B (+9.5%), OP ¥1.169B (+27.2%) — record interim. NOTE: Prior version's '8 consecutive years' claim is UNVERIFIED in our searches; only 2 consecutive years confirmed (Matsui Securities). The longer streak requires direct IR verification. Why did business move this way? 1. Q1 OP -28.8% caused by one-off Tokai SCM Center relocation costs concentrated in Q1 (per 2025/5/15 disclosure). 2. H1 recovered to +27.2% as relocation costs annualized and volume grew, hitting interim record. 3. Multi-year streak count UNVERIFIED — only 2 consecutive years confirmed in our scope; the longer-term track record suggests ongoing growth but the exact streak number needs re-verification. Why did the stock move this way? Specific stock-move catalysts are UNVERIFIED — no analyst notes or specific news identified in searches; thin small/mid-cap coverage. Verifiable structural anchors: 1. Interim record-high results plausibly support sentiment (source: LNEWS). 2. Post-relocation Tokai capacity expansion is a plausible medium-term thesis (source: Q1 short report). 3. Specific causal stock link is UNVERIFIED — no public catalyst identified; the market may simply be pricing in the earnings momentum.
▸ Profit hit by specific one-off event 1 names
Companies where net profit fell sharply — not from core-business deterioration — but from a clearly named one-off: reversal of a prior-year sale-and-leaseback gain, a large-property liquidation base effect, equity-method investee softening after an activist retreat, or a full MBO/delisting transaction dominating the share-price narrative.
9066 Nissin Corporation ▸
Revenue: ↑ +13.5% (H1, pre-delisting) Op profit: ↑ +10.1% (H1, pre-delisting) Net profit: ↑ up Stock: converged to TOB price ¥8,100; delisted Oct 2025
分類: 総合物流(上場廃止) / Comprehensive logistics (delisted Oct 2025)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 9066 + Nissin IR…
Company overview: Nissin (9066) was a comprehensive logistics company headquartered in Yokohama, Kanagawa, founded 1938 (Showa 13). Integrated international logistics (air + ocean forwarding + customs, networks in 20+ countries) + warehousing + land transport + port logistics — a 'comprehensive logistics specialist.' CRITICAL: Nissin was delisted from TSE Prime on October 15, 2025. Timeline: (1) May 12, 2025 — Bain Capital-backed BCJ-98 announced MBO at ¥8,100/share. (2) TOB period May 13 – July 8, 2025; total purchase ¥72.1B–¥112.1B range. (3) July 14, 2025 TOB established (11,051,842 shares tendered vs. lower limit 8,896,100). (4) September 12, 2025 EGM approved share consolidation. (5) Delisted October 15, 2025. Now a wholly owned subsidiary of BCJ-98 (+ Nissin Shoji); shares no longer traded (sources: lnews, tob-research, stockexpress). How did business move? MBO/delisting dominates the analysis — standard fundamental drivers do not apply. Pre-MBO reference data (FY3/2025 H1): Revenue ¥93.452B (+13.5%), OP ¥4.543B (+10.1%) — healthy results, but post-delisting financials are not public. TOB price ¥8,100, total purchase ¥72.1B–¥112.1B range (sources: tob-research, kabukiso, nihon-ma). Why did business move this way? Per Bain Capital / management rationale (per JMD, MA-CP articles): execute mid-to-long-term management reform under private ownership. Standard MBO logic: (1) freedom from quarterly public-market pressure, (2) longer-horizon reform capacity, (3) Bain Capital resources. TSMC Kumamoto warehouse / EC-logistics entry claims are outside the scope of public disclosure post-delisting and cannot be verified. Why did the stock move this way? Stock simply converged to ¥8,100 TOB price; no longer traded post-October 15, 2025. 1. MBO announcement (May 12, 2025) gapped the stock to near ¥8,100 as TOB price exceeded market price. 2. During TOB period (May 13 – July 8) the stock was anchored at ¥8,100 — break above TOB price is structurally impossible. 3. October 15, 2025 — trading ended. Thereafter no stock price exists; fundamental valuation and analyst targets are moot.
▸ Regional quiet compounder 3 names
Small-to-mid regional players — bus operators, specialized industrial-logistics niche players, small truckers, and regional holding companies — growing steadily through fare revisions, stable long-term contracts, and local market dominance, without a single high-profile catalyst driving the stock.
9027 Loginet Japan (ロジネットジャパン) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +1.0% Op profit: ↑ +1.2% Net profit: ↑ +22% Stock: up gently (~¥3,220, % unverified)
分類: 北海道・海上3PL / Hokkaido marine & 3PL logistics (Loginet Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9027 + Loginet Japan IR + Chuo Warehouse Group dis…
Company overview: Loginet Japan (9027) is a Sapporo-headquartered mid-tier 3PL logistics company, Hokkaido-based. Formed in October 2005 as a joint share-transfer holding company by Sapporo Tsuun and Chuo Tsuun (中央通運 — NOT Chuo Warehouse / 中央倉庫 9319); listed on Sapporo Securities Exchange. Core: Hokkaido-Honshu marine transport (RoRo) + distribution-center operations + trucking + food 3PL + housing-material logistics + moving services integrated as a comprehensive logistics operator nationwide. Group structure: holding company Loginet Japan over Sapporo Tsuun, Loginet East Japan, Loginet West Japan, Loginet Kyushu. Put simply: a mid-tier logistics player connecting Hokkaido and Honshu by ship. How did business move? FY3/2026: Revenue ¥78.008B (+1.0%), OP ¥3.711B (+1.2%), NP +22% to ¥2.7B — major-customer volume increases absorbed labor-investment cost rises. Year 2 of mid-term plan 2025-2027 progressing well. Stock ~¥3,220 (May 2026); dividend forecast ¥135 / yield ~4.19%. Why did business move this way? Three reasons for steady growth. 1. Stable Hokkaido-Honshu marine transport revenue — Hokkaido is a major source of food (dairy, seafood, agriculture) + housing materials + construction materials, generating stable Honshu-bound transport demand. Loginet long-tenured Hokkaido-Honshu RoRo marine transport is a stable revenue source. 2. Food 3PL + major-customer volume growth — 3PL contracts for Hokkaido food makers expanded stably; incremental volumes from existing major customers also lifted earnings. Food is recession-resilient. 3. Strategic continued labor investment + 2024-Problem rate revisions — Driver recruitment + industry-workforce training investment maintained; advantage under the 2024 Problem. Marine + trucking rate revisions improved economics. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend (~¥3,220 in May 2026). Three main reasons. 1. Steady growth + mid-term plan progress — Mid-term plan 2025-2027 target-progress earns trust. 2. Hokkaido-Honshu marine entry barriers + rail-container #2 position — RoRo marine transport requires specialized vessels + port facilities + long operational know-how; new entrants face high barriers. Reportedly holds #2 share in rail-container transport behind Nippon Express. 3. Attractive dividend yield (~4.19%) + long-horizon money — Sapporo-only listing limits liquidity, but yield is attractive; long-horizon retail investors + regional bank-related financial institutions + dividend-mandate long-horizon investors are core holders.
9040 Taiho Transportation (大宝運輸) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +4.2% Op profit: ↑ +31.0% Net profit: ↓ -33.1% Stock: stable ~¥4,190 (% unverified)
分類: 地方中堅トラック / Regional mid-tier trucker (Taiho Transportation)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9040 + Shikiho 9040 + Taiho Transport disclosures…
Company overview: Taiho Transportation (9040) is a small-mid trucking + warehouse company headquartered in Kanayama, Naka-ku, Nagoya — incorporated 1951 (roots back to 1920). Core: trucking (consumer-related logistics centered on food / daily goods / sundries) + warehouse + temperature-controlled logistics + auto-vehicle maintenance (mainly own fleet) + industrial-waste recycling, branded GREEN HAND. Main shippers are Suntory Group and Kokubu Chubu (food / beverage distribution), plus JIT delivery for Toyota-related and Mitsubishi-related factories. Put simply: an independent veteran Chubu trucker handling regional delivery of food and consumer goods. How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: Revenue ¥8.085B (+4.2%), OP ¥321M (+31.0%), recurring ¥333M (+28.3%), net profit ¥204M (-33.1%). Operating-level double-digit growth but NP declined as the prior-year base (¥305M, +199%) included one-off lift. FY3/2027 company guidance is softer: Revenue ¥8.3B (+2.7%), OP ¥280M (-12.9%), recurring ¥300M (-9.9%), NP ¥190M (-7.0%). Why did business move this way? Three reasons for operating-level growth. 1. Steady Chubu consumer-related logistics — Food / beverage / daily-goods distribution is recession-resilient; transactions with Suntory Group and Kokubu Chubu underpin stable revenue. 2. Steady JIT delivery for Toyota-related and Mitsubishi-related plants — Not in-factory auto-parts logistics, but JIT delivery work serves as a Chubu logistics-demand base. 3. 2024-Problem rate revisions + cost discipline — Rate hikes + route efficiency + labor discipline improved margins; OP rose +31%. NP fell due to reversal of prior-year one-offs (tax / other effects — confirm in tanshin). Why did the stock move this way? Stock around ¥4,190 in March 2026, dividend yield ~2.4%. Three main reasons. 1. Significant operating-level profit gain + rate-revision effect — Limited small-cap recognition, but OP +31% is recognized as momentum. 2. Stability of Chubu consumer-related logistics — Food / beverage / daily-goods distribution is recession-resilient and underpins demand. 3. Conservative FY27 guidance (-13% OP / -10% recurring) caps further re-rating — Sustained re-rating likely needs renewed momentum. Note: original draft listing of Toyota / Denso / Aisin / Yamaha Motor as PRIMARY customers is not supported — main customer profile is food / consumer-goods (Suntory, Kokubu Chubu) with auto-related as supplementary.
9063 Okayama Freight Transport (岡山県貨物運送) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +0.9% (Q3 YTD) Op profit: ↑ +17.7% (Q3 YTD) Net profit: ↑ +177.4% (Q3 YTD, incl. RE gain) Stock: up gently (% unverified)
分類: 中四国地方トラック / Chu-Shikoku regional trucker (Okayama Freight)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 9063 + Okayama Freight IR + Okayama Freight Q3 dis…
Company overview: Okayama Freight Transport (9063) is a mid-tier regional trucking company headquartered in Okayama City, founded 1948 — an established Chu-Shikoku regional trucker. Operating area: Okayama + Hiroshima + Yamaguchi + Shikoku. Core: LTL (mixed-cargo) + charter + 3PL + warehousing + moving + auto maintenance — serves Okayama-area manufacturers (Kuraray, Mitsui Kinzoku, Ryobi Group) + food makers with stable transport / logistics. Put simply: 'an established regional trucker supporting Okayama Kuraray and Mitsui Kinzoku's logistics in Chu-Shikoku.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Operating revenue ¥29.301B (¥293.01億, +0.9%) [prior entry showed ¥29.30億 — wrong magnitude]; OP ¥934M (+17.7%); NP ¥2.269B (+177.4%). The NP surge came from a transfer gain on the former Kyoto sales office (exact amount NOT disclosed in public summaries — UNVERIFIED). Full-year forecast (IRBANK): Revenue ¥38.99B (+1.4%), OP ¥1.26B (+39.0%), NP ¥2.72B (+184.0%). Dividend ¥70 — FLAT vs FY3/2024 and FY3/2025, NOT a hike (Yahoo Finance dividend history). Why did business move this way? Three reasons earnings grew. 1. Strategic Kyoto sales-office sale — Former Kyoto office property sold, booking ~¥1.5B special gain. Well-timed sale into rising Kyoto land prices boosted NP via special gains. 2. Core-trucking rate revisions + stable revenue from Okayama manufacturers — 2024-Problem driver-hour rules drove rate hikes, lifting trucking OP +17.7%. On-site logistics + delivery for Kuraray, Mitsui Kinzoku, Ryobi steady. 3. Continued asset rationalization — Selling underperforming offices + efficient relocation continue; fixed-cost optimization in progress. Why did the stock move this way? Stock holds a gentle uptrend. Three main reasons. 1. NP +177% sharp gain + dividend hike to ¥70 — Institutional money picks Okayama under 'asset rationalization + stronger shareholder return' themes; high dividend yield attracts steady dividend-mandate long-horizon demand. 2. Chu-Shikoku regional logistics dominance + strategic use of real-estate hidden value — Okayama + Hiroshima + Yamaguchi + Shikoku network has high regional entry barriers. Former offices in regional cities also see land-price appreciation; sequential sales can generate special gains + cash flow — hidden value on balance sheet is recognized. 3. Long-tenured Okayama manufacturer relationships + asset rationalization + efficiency in parallel — Long contracts with Kuraray, Mitsui Kinzoku are recession-resilient revenue sources. Underperforming asset sales + efficient office relocation + IT investment together sustain structural earnings-quality improvement.
TOPIX-33 sector: 鉱業 (Mining)
Total listed in sector 6
Qualifying as R+ 4
R- (currently listed) 2
Delisted / TOB (historical) 1
Math: 4 R+ + 2 R- = 6 currently listed. 1 delisted/TOB shown separately.
Show 3 excluded companies
  • 1605 INPEX (国際石油開発帝石) — R- (FY3/2026 revenue ↓, OP ↓ on oil/gas price decline; both revenue and OP down)
  • 1662 JAPEX (石油資源開発) — R- (FY3/2026 revenue -10.8% to ¥347B, OP -37.1%, NP -44.5%; sector-wide commodity weakness)
  • 1606 Japan Drilling (日本海洋掘削) — DELISTED 2018-07-23 via corporate reorganization (~¥90B liabilities; crude oil price collapse). Now operates as JX Nippon Oil & Gas subsidiary.

Universe coverage is exhaustive: all currently listed TOPIX-33 Mining names are classified. Source: omotenashi.site Mining list (cross-checked with kabutan/minkabu IR pages).

For mining (oil & gas exploration), operating-profit-up best captures business performance — revenue moves with crude oil & gas prices.

Why these filter options?

Why use Operating profit ↑ (default for Mining)?
In plain terms: Mining sector revenue moves with global oil/gas/commodity prices — NOT with how well the business is being run. When crude prices fall, every mining company's revenue drops, even if they're operating their oil fields efficiently. So we default to 営業利益○ (operating profit up) for the same reason as Petroleum.



For FY3/2026, oil/gas prices fell sharply, so both major Japanese mining companies (INPEX, JAPEX) saw revenue AND operating profit decline. **0 companies qualify under this filter this year.** The transparency block above the cards shows which companies were considered and why each was excluded.
Why use Revenue ↑ (strict)?
In plain terms: Same as Petroleum — strict revenue-up filter usually excludes most of the universe in commodity sectors when prices fall. For FY3/2026, no Mining companies qualify under this strict filter (both INPEX and JAPEX had revenue down).
Why use Either ↑ (revenue OR operating profit up)?
In plain terms: Union of the two filters above. Mining sector FY3/2026 has 0 companies qualifying under either revenue-up or operating-profit-up — both INPEX and JAPEX had both metrics down.
▸ Investment-income driven 1 names
These companies earn their headline profit primarily from returns on minority stakes in overseas mines or diversified investment assets, not from their own operating volumes. When commodity prices shift, dividend income and mine-investment returns swing sharply, disconnecting earnings from the core business.
