Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 1514 settlement + kabutan 1514 finance + nikkei 15…
Company overview: Sumiseki Holdings (1514) is a long-established Meiji-era coal + resources company headquartered in Tokyo-Minato. Founded in 1893 — originally as 'Sumitomo Coal Mining,' it extracted coal in Japan. After domestic mines closed, the company today runs coal import / sales (importing coking + thermal coal from Australia and selling to Japanese steel + power companies) + advanced materials (ceramics, etc.) + crushed stone (construction aggregates) as three pillars. In addition, Sumiseki holds a minority stake in Australia's BMA coal mine (operated by BHP Billiton Mitsubishi Alliance — the world's largest coking-coal complex), and dividend income from that mine makes up the bulk of recurring + net profit — a unique structure. How did business move? Initial FY3/2026 full-year guidance: revenue ¥97B (-5.5%), recurring profit ¥16B (-66.0%), net profit ¥16B (-61.9%), dividend cut ¥30 → ¥15. On April 15, 2026 the company revised recurring profit up by +71.9% and lifted the year-end dividend from ¥15 to ¥20 (source: kabutan k202604150003) — driven by Wanbo dividends in 2H coming in above conservative original guidance. Even so, recurring profit remains well below FY3/2025 levels and the dividend is still cut from prior ¥30. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed the 'profit decline driver' clearly. The coal business segment itself performed well, through accelerated coal sales timing + new business expansion. But the biggest factor dragging earnings down was 'reduced dividend income received' — that is, dividend income from Australia's BMA coal mine (via subsidiary 'Wanbo') fell sharply. From 2024 into 2025, coal prices (especially coking coal) fell from peaks, and domestic coal demand also declined. As a result, BMA mine profit shrank, and the dividend income Sumiseki receives also crashed. Sumiseki's earnings structure depends more on Australian mine dividends than on the core coal-import / sales business — a feature that means even when the core business does well, overall earnings can drop sharply if global coal prices fall. Why did the stock move this way? The stock fell from ¥1,078 (March 2026) to ¥561 (June 2026), a sharp -48% drop in about 3 months. Three main reasons. 1. The dividend cut (¥30 → ¥15, halved) was the biggest negative — Long-horizon investors holding Sumiseki for the dividend (retail expecting annual income + pension funds, etc.) rushed to sell when they saw the dividend would be cut in half. 2. Long-term coal-price decline outlook — With global decarbonization progressing, long-term coal demand is set to decline. BMA dividend income is likely to keep falling, raising worries about Sumiseki's long-term revenue base. 3. Caution about 'reliance on dividend income over the core business' — Management itself disclosed 'coal business is strong but dividend income decline pressures profit,' making the market clearly recognize how dependent Sumiseki is on external factors (Australian mine dividends). The 89.1% equity ratio (financial soundness) reduces bankruptcy risk but isn't enough to offset the structural earnings fragility.
▸ Post-coal business reinvention 1 names
This company proactively exited coal to adapt to the decarbonization era, and is now growing through M&A of entirely new business segments. Its profit expansion is driven by acquired subsidiaries rather than legacy resources.
1518 Mitsui Matsushima Holdings (三井松島HD) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +8.1% Op profit: ↑ +25.7% Net profit: ↓ -22.3% Stock: 上昇トレンド / uptrend (no specific % stated)
分類: 産業用製品・金融・生活消費財 / Industrial products, financial services & consumer goods (former coal)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 1518 settlement + nikkei 1518 chart + Yahoo Financ…
Company overview: Mitsui Matsushima Holdings (1518) is a 'former coal company' headquartered in Fukuoka. Founded in 1913 (Meiji era), it supplied Japan's energy through pre- and post-war eras by mining coal at the Matsushima Coal Mine (Nagasaki) + Australian coal mines. As the world moved toward decarbonization, the company closed its Australian mines in July 2023 and fully exited coal in FY3/2024. Today it's reinventing itself with three pillars: 'Industrial Products business (machinery / equipment sales),' 'Financial / Other business (leasing, factoring),' and 'Consumer Goods business.' Simply put: 'a company that voluntarily abandoned coal to adapt to the decarbonization era, rebuilding itself around three entirely new businesses.' How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year (announced May 13, 2026): revenue ¥65.468B (+8.1%), OP ¥9.573B (+25.7%), recurring ¥9.944B (+17.7%) — all three segments posted revenue + profit growth. However NP fell -22.3% to ¥6.716B, reflecting the absence of FY3/2025's one-off ¥2.72B coal-interest transfer gain. Current-period booked ¥1.24B solar divestment gain + ¥744M securities-sale gain, offset by a ¥1.429B loss on Mitsui Matsushima Resources share sale. Annual dividend ¥64 (interim ¥23 + year-end ¥41); FY3/2027 guidance ¥74. FY3/2027 plan: Revenue ¥68B (+3.9%), OP ¥9.7B (+1.3%), NP ¥7.1B (+5.7%). Why did business move this way? The company disclosed two main 'profit-increase drivers.' 1. Industrial Products segment revenue expanded — Subsidiary 'Japan Chain Holdings' (which sells industrial machinery / equipment) grew revenue substantially by capturing manufacturer demand. 2. M&A-driven new subsidiary contribution — In July 2024, 'MRF' was subsidiarized, and its earnings flowed into consolidated results. Using cash from coal exit, Mitsui Matsushima is actively executing M&A in consumer goods + industrial products + financial services, building a structure where acquired-company earnings continuously support business expansion. In short: 'The new three pillars are now operating in earnest, with Industrial Products segment + M&A effects driving profit higher' — that's the central story of this period's recovery. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. Business recovery is in full force — OP +25.7% and recurring +17.7% earnings beat moves the perception beyond 'recovery from last year's valley' to 'a company that can consistently make money under the new structure.' 2. High dividend is being maintained — Even through softer earnings periods, dividend has been maintained, with ~4.5% yield supporting the stock floor. Steady demand from dividend-mandate long-horizon investors (pension funds, retail) continues. 3. ESG investor sentiment shift — Full coal exit means institutional investors (especially European ESG-strict funds) that previously avoided the name due to coal exposure are now positively including it. Additionally, FY27 (next year) guidance for further revenue + profit growth is building trust in earnings momentum, supporting the uptrend.
▸ Investment-income driven 1 names
These companies earn their headline profit primarily from returns on minority stakes in overseas mines or diversified investment assets, not from their own operating volumes. When commodity prices shift, dividend income and mine-investment returns swing sharply, disconnecting earnings from the core business.
1515 Nittetsu Mining (日鉄鉱業) ▸
Revenue: ↓ -2.3% (H1) Op profit: ↑ +60.9% (H1) Net profit: ↑ +15.6% (H1) Stock: 上昇トレンド / uptrend (no specific % stated)
分類: 石灰石鉱業・金属資源投資 / Limestone mining & metal resource investment
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 1515 settlement + kabutan 1515 upward revision new…
Company overview: Nittetsu Mining (1515) is Japan's largest mining company that extracts 'limestone' — an essential ingredient for steelmaking. Founded in 1939, it was originally established to supply limestone to the Nippon Steel Group. Today, in addition to limestone mining, it operates a diversified business structure: metal resources (investing in copper + gold mines overseas in Chile, Brazil, etc.), Tokyo real estate, building materials, and industrial-waste recycling. How did business move? FY3/2026 H1 (interim, Nov 7, 2025): revenue ¥93.07B (-2.3%), operating profit ¥8.585B (+60.9%), recurring profit ¥9.566B (+45.5%), net profit ¥6.624B (+15.6%). Full-year recurring guidance raised from ¥9.2B to ¥14.2B (+24.2%); annual dividend raised to ¥142 on a post-1:5-stock-split basis (split effective Sept 30, 2025; pre-split prior year was ¥224). However, FY3/2027 guidance points to recurring profit declining to ¥11.5B — a peak-out concern. Why did business move this way? The company itself disclosed the 'profit increase drivers' clearly. The main three are: 1. Metals segment posted a major profit gain — Profit from investments in overseas copper / gold mines in Chile, Brazil grew substantially as copper / gold prices stayed at high levels globally. Copper is in expanding demand for global decarbonization + EV adoption + power infrastructure, so its price stays high long-term. Gold also stayed high amid global economic uncertainty. Overseas mine profit contribution was the central engine of this period's profit growth. 2. Resources segment (domestic limestone) was also strong — Limestone revenue itself declined slightly, but cost discipline + price negotiation drove margin improvement. By pushing through transport-fee increases and electricity-cost pass-through in negotiations with domestic steelmakers, the limestone business's profitability improved. 3. Real estate segment contributed — Rental income from properties held in central Tokyo (Chiyoda, Minato, etc.) trended steadily, and Tokyo's land-price uptrend expanded the real-estate business's earnings foundation. In short: 'Volume sold went down, but the high-margin businesses (overseas metals + domestic real estate) grew significantly, lifting overall profit' — a structural shift in earnings composition. Why did the stock move this way? After the upward-revision and major dividend hike announcement, the stock has held a clear uptrend. Three main reasons: 1. The ¥109 → ¥142 major dividend hike was the biggest positive — A roughly 30% dividend increase brought the dividend yield to around 5%, a level several times higher than bank deposits. This is attracting long-horizon investors who 'want stable holdings for the dividend.' 2. The upward-revision provided confidence — When management itself acknowledged 'earnings are better than originally planned' and revised guidance up, this is one of the most reliable earnings signals an investor can get. Analysts also raised their forecasts, lifting valuation in a chain reaction. 3. Resource + decarbonization theme attention (but Archelos has risks too) — Long-term copper price upside + the Archelos copper mine under development in Chile draw institutional attention as a 'decarbonization-era resource stock.' However, company disclosure flags that Archelos has cost increases and operational start-up delays — it is a risk factor, not pure upside (source: search results). The long-term copper-demand story supports the stock, but combined with FY3/2027's recurring-profit-decline guidance, peak-out concerns may cap upside.
▸ Niche commodity growth operator 1 names
This company is an active operator of a niche, globally-demanded commodity (iodine) whose price and volume are rising on structural demand trends. Growth is organic — driven by capacity expansion and favorable pricing — not by acquisitions or passive investment income.
1663 K&O Energy Group (K&Oエナジーグループ) ▸
Revenue: ↓ -1.2% Op profit: ↑ +20.1% Net profit: ↑ +35.9% Stock: 上昇トレンド・過去最高益 / uptrend on record earnings (no specific % stated)
分類: 天然ガス採掘・ヨウ素事業 / Natural gas extraction & iodine
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabuyoho 1663 + kabutan 1663 finance + logmi finance K&…
Company overview: K&O Energy Group (1663) is a uniquely structured energy company in Mobara, Chiba. It was formed in 2009 when two companies merged: 'Kanto Natural Gas Development' (which extracts natural gas from the seabed off Kujukuri Coast, Chiba — the 'K') and 'Otaki Gas' (which supplies city gas to homes + businesses in Chiba — the 'O'). Two main businesses: natural gas (self-extracting from the offshore South Kanto Gas Field and supplying city gas) and iodine (extracting and refining iodine from underground brine for sale). Iodine is a key global commodity used as medical disinfectant, LCD-panel material, and agrochemical feedstock — Japan is the world's #2 iodine producer (after Chile). Simply put: 'a company that extracts and sells two resources sleeping beneath Chiba — natural gas and iodine.' How did business move? FY12/2025 (December fiscal year) posted record-high earnings: Revenue ¥91.354B (-1.2%), OP ¥10.594B (+20.1%), recurring ¥11.699B (+19.0%), NP ¥8.379B (+35.9%) — OP and recurring are record highs since the company's 2009 formation (sources: logmi finance, Yahoo Finance, minkabu). Iodine business revenue ¥15.092B (+11.6%), volume 1,800 tons (+4.5%). Dividend ¥42 → ¥54; FY12/2026 plan ¥60. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed two main 'profit-increase drivers.' 1. Strong iodine business — Iodine's global selling price rose, which sharply lifted K&O's segment profits. With global iodine demand (LCD panels + medical disinfectants + agrochemicals) steadily expanding, iodine prices stayed at high levels. To strengthen the iodine business's competitiveness, the company is actively investing: of FY2025's total capex of ¥7.546B, the ~¥800M increase year-over-year is being directed mainly to building iodine refining / production facilities — expanding capacity and improving margins in parallel. 2. Special gains booked — On top of iodine's strong performance, special gains were booked, lifting net profit further. In short: 'The iodine business is benefiting from global demand expansion and growing rapidly, lifting the company's overall profit to a record high' — that's the central story of this period's strength. Why did the stock move this way? 1. Record-high OP since inception + a 3-year dividend ramp ¥42 → ¥54 → planned ¥60 are the clearest sourced positives (company IR). 2. Iodine price expected to stay at high levels + capacity expansion underpin the growth narrative (company disclosure). 3. CCS JV with INPEX (via Kanto Natural Gas Dev) and H2/CO₂ venture with Air Water in Mobara support a longer-term decarbonization-positioning story (logmi article). NOTE: Specific analyst commentary explicitly attributing recent stock-price moves was not found in accessible sources — UNVERIFIED. Also note: FY2026 capex plan reduces iodine-equipment spend; brine-production equipment spend increases — the prior version's narrative was directionally inverted.
TOPIX-33 sector: 石油・石炭製品 (Oil & Coal Products)
Total listed in sector 9
Qualifying as R+ 7
R- (currently listed) 2
Delisted / TOB (historical) 2
Math: 7 R+ + 2 R- = 9 currently listed. 2 delisted/TOB shown separately.
Show 4 excluded companies
  • 5010 Nippon Seirou (日本精蝋) — R- (FY3/2026 revenue -29.16% to ¥19.8B; NP +57% from one-off effects, but core business contracted)
  • 5013 Yushiro (ユシロ化学工業) — R- (FY3/2026 revenue ↓ due to China JV deconsolidation Sep 2025; H1 recurring profit -7.2%; ordinary profit guidance upward-revised but YoY still down)
  • 5008 Toa Oil (東亜石油) — DELISTED 2021-12-15 via Idemitsu TOB (became wholly-owned subsidiary)
  • 5017 Fuji Oil (富士石油) — DELISTED 2026-01-20 via Idemitsu TOB (became consolidated subsidiary Nov 2025, delisted Jan 2026)

Universe is now exhaustive — all 9 currently-listed TOPIX-33 Petroleum & Coal Products names classified. 5008 and 5017 listed in excluded as historical reference (delisted via Idemitsu TOB).

For petroleum refining, operating-profit-up captures business performance better than revenue-up because revenue moves with crude oil prices.

Why these filter options?

Why use Operating profit ↑ (default for Oil)?
In plain terms: Oil sector revenue moves with global crude oil prices — NOT with how well the business is being run. When oil prices fall, every refiner's revenue drops, even if they're running their refineries efficiently and earning healthy refining margins. So 'revenue up (売上○)' is a misleading quality signal for oil companies — it mostly tells you about OPEC decisions and global demand, not about the company itself.



Operating profit (営業利益) captures the actual business performance: refining margins, inventory management, cost discipline, downstream demand strength. When operating profit grows, the business is genuinely doing better, regardless of whether crude prices were a tailwind or a headwind.



That's why for the oil sector, this view defaults to 営業利益○ (operating profit up). For FY3/2026, crude oil prices fell sharply, so almost every Japanese refiner saw revenue decline (-4% to -12%). But operating profit grew strongly at the top players (Idemitsu +31%, ENEOS +340%, Cosmo +13%) because refining margins were favorable. The strict 売上○ filter would exclude all of these stories — which is why the profit-based filter shows the real picture.



You can switch to 売上○ if you want only revenue-growth companies (this year that's just Fuji Kosan, thanks to diversification into environmental energy and recycling). Or 両方 (both) to see all companies tagged under either filter.
Why use Revenue ↑ (strict)?
In plain terms: This is the strict 'revenue up' filter — same as the IT sector default. For oil companies, this filter usually excludes most of the universe because crude oil price declines drag down revenue across the sector even when business is performing well. Use this if your senior or framework specifically requires a revenue-based filter; otherwise the 営業利益○ default is more informative for the sector.
Why use Either ↑ (revenue OR operating profit up)?
In plain terms: This is the union of the two filters above. Companies that have EITHER revenue up OR operating profit up appear. Use this when you want the broadest view of the sector and don't want to be restricted to one signal. For oil this typically shows the same set as 営業利益○ since the revenue-up subset is a small intersection of the OP-up set.
▸ Major refiners — crude-driven 3 names
Japan's three large-scale oil refiners whose earnings rise and fall primarily with crude-oil prices and inventory valuation time-lag effects; all are simultaneously building adjacent growth axes (renewables, high-functional materials) to reduce that dependency.
5019 Idemitsu Kosan ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↑ +30.84% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: est. down (no % stated; -70.2% OP in Q3-cum FY3/2026 weighs)
分類: 石油精製・販売 / Oil refiner & distributor (+ high-functional materials)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + LIMO 5019 article + STOCK EXPRESS 5019 + Nikkei 5019 + log…
Company overview: Idemitsu Kosan (5019) is Japan #2 oil refiner and distributor — about 30% domestic gasoline-station share, with refineries in Hokkaido, Chiba, Aichi, and Tokuyama. Plus a High-Functional Materials segment — organic-EL blue emitter materials (for smartphone / TV displays), lithium-ion battery materials, overseas lubricant sales — gives Idemitsu a unique diversification axis. Simply put: a comprehensive energy company anchored in refining while growing high-tech material businesses. How did business move? FY3/2026 Q3 cumulative (Apr-Dec): revenue ¥5.9446T (-13.6%), operating profit ¥36.7B (-70.2%), recurring profit ¥54.9B (-66.8%), net profit attributable to parent shareholders ¥52.578B (-58.75%) — both revenue and profit fell sharply. The main cause is the fuel oil segment deterioration; the High-Functional Materials segment held strong. Full-year guidance is unchanged, with a continued severe business environment expected. The prior year (FY3/2025) achieved operating profit +30.84% via cost discipline, but this year is the reaction, with profit sharply down. Why did business move this way? The biggest factor is the major decline in the fuel oil segment. Idemitsu profit partially depends on inventory valuation time-lag effects (crude-price changes affecting profit with a time lag), so earnings swing with crude oil prices. This year, crude prices entered a downward phase, shrinking inventory valuation gains on held crude — combined with cost increases from major periodic maintenance — sharply squeezed fuel oil segment profit. Meanwhile, the High-Functional Materials segment (organic-EL blue emitters, lithium-battery materials, etc.) stayed strong, and overseas lubricant sales also grew. But the High-Functional Materials segment is still small in scale and has not been enough to offset the fuel oil drop. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. The market has priced in earnings deterioration — A -70.2% operating profit drop is downward pressure on the stock. 2. Caution about crude-price-dependent structural fragility — The structural fragility of inventory valuation effects swinging profit is being recognized by the market. 3. ESG investor screens — Institutional investors limiting fossil-fuel exposure (especially European ESG funds) will not add even with earnings improvement, capping upside. On the other hand, Idemitsu has historically continued PBR-improvement measures (a past 1-for-5 stock split, buybacks, dividend hikes). The strong shareholder-return stance supports the floor (UNVERIFIED: whether a new buyback or dividend hike was announced specifically within FY3/2026 is not confirmed in this verification).
5020 ENEOS Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↑ up Net profit: ↑ +14.4% Stock: est. up (no % stated; 91% guidance beat + ¥50B buyback)
分類: 石油精製・販売 / Oil refiner & distributor (largest in Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + Yahoo Finance 5020 + Globe and Mail 5020 + Simply Wall St …
Company overview: ENEOS Holdings (5020) is Japan largest oil refiner and distributor — with about 50% market share of domestic gasoline stations, leading the industry in refinery utilization. Renamed from JXTG Energy, it is a comprehensive energy company with subsidiaries including ENEOS (oil refining / distribution), JX Metals (copper + electronic materials production / sales), and JX Oil & Gas Exploration (overseas oil-field development). Simply put: the leader of Japan largest oil company + copper / electronic materials business + overseas oil-field development. How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year results: operating profit ¥466.6B (¥474.4B excluding inventory impact), and net profit attributable to parent shareholders ¥258.7B (+14.4%) — beating the initial guidance of ¥135B by 91%, a major upside surprise. Dividend held stable at ¥34 annually (¥17 interim + ¥17 year-end); FY3/2027 also plans ¥34. In addition, a buyback decision was made with an upper limit of 82M shares or ¥50B. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed that the biggest factor in the profit surge was that the oil refining / distribution segment profitability improved much more than expected. The margin (spread between crude oil price and refined product selling price) improved across the industry, and ENEOS, as the country largest refiner, benefited the most. In addition, industry consolidation is increasing oligopoly concentration and strengthening pricing power (UNVERIFIED: specific current status of Toa Oil full subsidiarization / Fuji Oil TOB was not directly confirmed in this verification). Subsidiaries JX Oil & Gas Exploration (income from overseas oil fields) and JX Metals (copper + electronic materials for EV-related demand) also contributed to profit, building earnings sources beyond oil refining. In short: the core refining business profit margin improved substantially, with subsidiaries also complementarily contributing, delivering earnings 91% above the original guidance. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. Major guidance beat + stable dividend maintenance — Achieving 91% above the original guidance + stable dividend maintenance triggered institutional buying. 2. Strong FY3/2027 (next year) outlook — Revenue +9.2% (¥12.85T), operating profit +30.7% (¥610B), net profit +60.4% (¥415B) FY27 guidance is building trust in earnings momentum. 3. Buyback + shareholder-return strengthening — The ¥50B-cap buyback decision is judged by the market as raising stock valuation through PBR improvement + EPS lift. The forecast dividend yield (around 2.7%) + PBR around 1.0x attract income-mandate long-horizon investors (UNVERIFIED: the specific claim of +8.7% outperformance vs Nikkei over the past 6 months could not be corroborated in this verification).
5021 Cosmo Energy Holdings ▸
Revenue: ↓ -4.4% Op profit: ↑ +12.9% Net profit: ↑ +28.4% Stock: est. flat to up (no % stated; analyst avg target ¥4,845-4,983, Buy)
分類: 石油精製・販売・再エネ / Oil refiner & distributor + wind power
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 5021 + Cosmo Energy IR Q2 + minkabu 5021 + Reinfor…
Company overview: Cosmo Energy Holdings (5021) is a mid-tier energy company with three pillars: oil refining / distribution (Cosmo Oil), petroleum exploration (Cosmo Oil Exploration), and renewable energy (Cosmo Eco Power = wind-power led) — domestic gasoline stations sell under the 'Cosmo' brand. Simply put: 'an energy company anchored in oil refining while diversifying through Middle East oil-field development + wind power.' How did business move? FY3/2026 full-year results: revenue ¥2.6776T (-4.4%), operating profit ¥144.8B (+12.9%), recurring profit ¥149.2B (-1.0%), net profit attributable to parent shareholders ¥74.0B (+28.4%). Revenue down, but profit metrics clearly improved — operating profit +12.9%, net profit +28.4%. Meanwhile, FY3/2027 (next year) guidance, factoring in Middle East tensions (assuming crude at $89/bbl, FX at ¥155/$), is for revenue ¥2.87T (+7.2%), operating profit ¥102B (-29.6%), net profit ¥44B (-40.6%) — a profit decline forecast. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed the 'profit-increase drivers' clearly by segment. 1. Oil segment profit growth — segment profit ¥76.3B (+¥14.5B YoY) — major gain. Inflation-driven cost pass-through advanced, sustaining real margin improvement. Tight domestic petroleum-product supply supported pricing. 2. Renewable energy business reaches sustained profitability — wind-power segment profit ¥2.8B (+¥1.5B YoY). The Cosmo Eco Power-led wind business expanded, with business diversification steadily showing through in profit. This is an important inflection where the decarbonization shift of an oil company is starting to appear as real earnings. 3. Petrochemical loss narrowed — petrochemical segment loss ¥3.1B (vs. prior-year loss ¥5B), an improvement. Meanwhile, petroleum exploration was ¥65.3B (-¥17.1B YoY) — a decline due to Middle East tensions. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. Analyst 'Buy' ratings — 7-analyst average rating 2.13 (1 = strong buy, 5 = strong sell), leaning heavily Buy. Average target prices ¥4,845-4,983 imply upside. 2. High dividend maintenance — FY3/2026 annual dividend ¥165 (¥75 interim + ¥90 year-end) at a high level attracts steady demand from dividend-mandate long-horizon investors. 3. Wind-power profitability lifted long-term growth expectations — Renewable energy is now growing into a real profit pillar, lifting institutional view as a 'decarbonization-aligned company.' But FY27 profit-decline guidance (from Middle East tensions) caps upside; the market is awaiting Middle East stabilization.
▸ Specialty lubricants — niche moat 2 names
Premium lubricant makers whose growth comes from brand strength or dominant niche market share rather than refining volume; margins are structurally protected from commodity-price competition.
5015 BP Castrol (ビーピー・カストロール) ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↑ +20.9% Net profit: ↑ up Stock: est. flat to up (no % stated; ~5% dividend yield, stable growth)
分類: 潤滑油 / Lubricants (BP Castrol Japan)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 5015 settlement + kabuyoho 5015 + Shikiho 5015 + i…
Company overview: BP Castrol (5015) is the Japan arm of the UK BP Group's automotive lubricants brand. Founded 1888 as Wakefield Oil Company in the UK, later integrated into BP, the Castrol brand is operated in Japan by this Japanese subsidiary. Core products: automotive engine oil (passenger / commercial / motorcycle) + ATF (automatic transmission fluid) + grease + industrial lubricants. The Castrol brand is globally recognized in the high-performance engine-oil market for motorcycles + sports cars, with brand value built through long-running F1 / MotoGP / WRC sponsorships. Simply put: 'the Japan arm of the global Castrol brand, selling high-performance lubricants.' How did business move? As a December-fiscal-year company, FY12/2024 actuals were ordinary profit ¥1.41B (+20.9%), and FY12/2025 guidance is ordinary profit ¥1.57B (+11.2%) — achieving 5 consecutive years of revenue growth + 3 consecutive years of profit growth as a stable-growth stock. Dividend planned at ¥44 annually (+¥2 YoY). The ~5% dividend yield is sustained at a high level. Why did business move this way? The 5-year revenue / 3-year profit growth track record is supported by three structural drivers. 1. Premium pricing sustained through brand strength — 'Castrol' built a 'high-performance / high-quality' brand image through long-running motorsports (F1 / MotoGP / WRC) sponsorships. This sustains a premium-pricing structure not pulled into price competition. 2. Maintenance demand recession resilience — Lubricant demand for existing-vehicle oil-change and maintenance is insulated from economic cycles, generating stable base revenue. 3. Strength in high-value motorcycle / agriculture products — Sales of specialty lubricants with higher unit prices + margins are growing, contributing meaningfully to earnings. Additionally, resolved chip shortage + auto-production recovery + inventory replenishment provided tailwinds. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. ~5% dividend yield is a high level — A yield many times higher than bank deposits attracts steady demand from dividend-mandate long-horizon investors (pension funds, life insurers, dividend-mandate retail) — the main floor-support factor. The 103% payout ratio isn't a one-off splurge — it reflects stable earnings + accumulated retained earnings. 2. Positioning as a 'high-yield value' stock — Institutional investors value the combination of 'high dividend + earnings stability + brand value' as an undervalued name, generating steady long-horizon demand. 3. 3 consecutive years of profit growth + this period's +11.2% guidance — Earnings momentum continuing is a factor for upside. The ¥42 → ¥44 dividend hike signals shareholder-return strengthening and is welcomed.
5018 MORESCO (モレスコ) ▸
Revenue: ↑ +6.2% Op profit: ↑ +25.8% Net profit: ↑ +28.3% Stock: est. up (no % stated; stock being re-rated, analyst buy signal)
分類: 特殊潤滑油・合成油 / Specialty lubricants & synthetic oils (HDD, EV, battery)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 5018 settlement + kabutan 5018 finance + nikkei 50…
Company overview: MORESCO (5018) is a specialty lubricant / synthetic oil specialty chemical maker headquartered in Kobe, founded 1958. Originally founded as 'Matsumura Sekiyu Kenkyusho' (Matsumura Oil Research Lab), later renamed MORESCO — an 'established niche specialty lubricants' player. Main businesses: ultra-thin HDD (hard disk drive) lubricants (special oil coated on HDD magnetic disk surfaces — a critical material for HDD manufacturing), automotive synthetic oils / ATF / precision-part lubricants, industrial synthetic oils, and battery materials (lithium-ion battery electrolytes) — all specialized in 'specialty-use / high-value-add' niche products. In HDD lubricants in particular, MORESCO is the global leader with >60% world share, supplying nearly monopolistically to Western Digital, Seagate, Toshiba, and other global HDD makers. Put simply: 'an unflashy lubricant maker that holds top global share in specialty applications.' How did business move? As a February fiscal-year company, FY2/2026 (this period) guidance is revenue ¥36.5B (+6.2%), OP ¥1.75B (+25.8%), NP ¥1.3B (+28.3%) — revenue + profit growth across the board. Q3 YTD (Mar-Nov) recurring profit already reached ¥1.79B (+32.3%), at 85.6% of the full-year plan. Q3 standalone OP was ¥1.67B (+50.2%) — a major quarterly profit jump. Annual dividend held at ¥45 (¥20 interim + ¥25 final), same as prior period. Why did business move this way? Three main reasons. 1. HDD lubricant stable demand — Earlier views had SSDs replacing HDDs, but data-center 'mass storage' demand remains HDD-centric. As Microsoft / Google / Amazon / Meta hyperscalers expand data centers, HDD lubricant demand grows steadily, and MORESCO's >60% world share directly captures this benefit. 2. Sales-price adjustment + cost-reduction effects — The company itself cites as 'profit growth factors' (1) expansion of high-value-add product sales, (2) expense control, (3) major profit improvement in the Japan segment. Niche high-value products preserved selling prices while crude-price softening lowered procurement — double margin expansion. 3. Semiconductor-fab + EV-related growth — TSMC Kumamoto + Rapidus Hokkaido semiconductor investments accelerated demand for precision-part lubricants, and EV-specific ATF + electric-motor synthetic oils also lifted automotive-segment profit. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. OP +50.2% (Q3 standalone) / +25.8% (full-year guidance) high growth rate — With sales roughly flat but profit surging via 'high-value-add mix lift + cost cuts,' institutional investors highly rate this as a 'structural profitability improvement.' 2. 'Data-center investment beneficiary' theme attention — AI + cloud demand expansion driving data-center investment is a major global theme. As 'HDD lubricant >60% world share,' MORESCO is positioned as a direct beneficiary of structural demand expansion. 3. PBR 0.89x + ~2.6% dividend yield undervalued valuation — Long stuck below PBR 1.0x, the combination of profit expansion + shareholder-return maintenance (¥45 dividend continued) is now being re-rated as 'cheap + growing.' Analyst signal: 'buy conversion' rating active.
▸ Diversified small energy — portfolio pivot 1 names
A small-cap energy company actively reorienting its revenue mix away from petroleum toward recycling, environmental energy, and other non-oil segments to counteract the secular decline in domestic gasoline demand.
5009 Fuji Kosan ▸
Revenue: ↑ +4.9% (Q1) Op profit: ↑ +21.5% (Q1) Net profit: ↑ up Stock: est. up (no % stated; rated undervalued with ¥1,413 target)
分類: 石油多角化 / Diversified energy (petroleum + recycling + environmental)
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + kabutan 5009 + irbank 5009 + minkabu 5009 + Fuji Kosan IR …
Company overview: Fuji Kosan (5009) is a small / mid-cap energy company that originated as a petroleum-products distributor and has since diversified into 5 segments: petroleum (gasoline / diesel sales), recycling (used-oil recycling), environmental-related (environment-friendly energy), rental, and home energy (residential LPG, etc.). Revenue is a fraction of major oil names (ENEOS, Idemitsu, Cosmo), but the unique portfolio is driving growth. Simply put: 'a small energy company growing through a unique diversification strategy, less swayed by crude-oil prices than the majors.' How did business move? FY3/2026 Q1 (Apr-Jun) actuals: revenue ¥15.578B (+4.9%), operating profit ¥149M (+21.5%) — revenue and operating profit both up. Both rising means Fuji Kosan is the only company in the petroleum sector that qualifies under both the 売上○ (revenue-up) and 営業利益○ (operating-profit-up) filters. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed that 'the center of profit growth' is the expansion of environment-related energy business and strengthened recycling business — non-oil segments. With Japan's gasoline demand declining long-term, Fuji Kosan diversified early. As gas-station consolidation and decarbonization shrink the overall oil industry, the strategy is 'build revenue pillars beyond oil.' The multi-year investment in diversification is finally translating into profit this period. Additionally, Fuji Kosan is planning a shift to holding-company structure, aiming for expanded strategic investment including M&A and new-business creation — a structural reform to accelerate growth. Why did the stock move this way? The stock is rated 'undervalued,' with a retail-investor consensus target of ¥1,413 and a 'Buy' classification. Three main reasons for the stock move. 1. Stock valuation hasn't caught up to earnings improvement — While earnings are clearly improving, the stock valuation lags behind, in an 'undervalued state' as retail investors see it. This is typical for a small cap with thin institutional analyst coverage. 2. Attention as a 'growth story distinct from oil majors' — While ENEOS / Idemitsu / Cosmo are heavily swayed by crude oil prices and inventory effects, Fuji Kosan grows through a unique 'environmental energy + recycling' ESG theme — attractive to investors. 3. Expectations from the holding-company shift — Room for M&A and new-business creation under the holding-company structure supports the stock as a mid-term growth expectation.
▸ Infrastructure asphalt & road paving 1 names
A specialist in asphalt products and road-paving construction whose customers are central and local government plus expressway operators; demand is driven by the national resilience program and aging-road renewal rather than consumer fuel demand or crude prices.
5011 Nichireki Group (ニチレキ) ▸
Revenue: ↑ up Op profit: ↓ down Net profit: ↓ -13.8% (recurring profit) Stock: est. flat (no % stated; dividend raised ¥75→¥80 despite profit decline)
分類: アスファルト・道路舗装 / Asphalt products & road paving
Genuine research (combined-prompt verification) 📚 Original source_hint: Combined-prompt JP web search 2026-06 + minkabu 5011 settlement + kabuyoho 5011 + Yahoo Finance 50…
Company overview: Nichireki Group (5011) is Japan largest asphalt-applied processing-product maker headquartered in Tokyo-Chiyoda, founded 1943 as Nippon Bituminous Chemical Industries. Today it operates two pillars: Asphalt-applied processing products (production of asphalt emulsions, modified asphalt, waterproofing, paving materials) + Road paving (paving construction work) — supporting Japan road infrastructure. The company shifted to a holding-company structure on October 1, 2024 and was renamed Nichireki Group Inc. Main customers: MLIT + local governments + expressway operators (NEXCO East / Central / West) + construction firms. Simply put: a company that, as the unsung backbone of Japan road infrastructure, manufactures asphalt and paves roads. How did business move? FY3/2026 actuals: revenue ¥75.853B (slight increase), recurring profit ¥6.07B (-13.8%). Revenue grew but profit fell. Dividend raised ¥5 YoY to ¥80 (¥40 interim + ¥40 year-end). FY3/2027 guidance: revenue ¥80B (+5.6%), operating profit ¥7B (+11.7%), recurring profit ¥7.3B (+3.6%) — a recovery, with management signaling the down-cycle is temporary. Why did business move this way? The company disclosed two main profit-decline drivers. 1. Asphalt-applied processing-products segment profit declined — this segment profit drop is the main driver of overall earnings deterioration. Asphalt feedstock is petroleum-derived, so sustained-high crude prices + yen weakness structurally lifted feedstock procurement costs. The company stated it worked on product-price reviews + cost management, but full pass-through to selling prices lagged, compressing margins. 2. Road paving segment profit grew — meanwhile, road paving saw a profit increase. National-resilience plan + aging-road replacement + disaster-recovery demand continue, so paving demand is firm and volume keeps growing. In short: Materials manufacturing lost profit due to high feedstock costs, while paving gained profit on strong demand — but the materials decline outweighed paving gains, so overall it was a profit decline. Why did the stock move this way? Three main reasons. 1. Continued dividend increase (¥75 to ¥80) — Raising the dividend even with a profit decline raises institutional view of shareholder return and supports the floor. 2. FY27 earnings recovery guidance — Revenue +5.6%, operating profit +11.7%, recurring profit +3.6% recovery guidance is being absorbed by the market as the decline is temporary. 3. Attention as a national-resilience theme stock — Asphalt / road paving has stable demand insulated from economic cycles under public investment / national resilience themes, attracting steady demand from long-horizon institutional investors.

No companies qualifying as R+×S+ under the current filter for this sector this year.
